title: U06: soft_fmul skips subnormal pre-normalisation — 11-20% ULP drift
description:
Unified catalogue: U06 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #07 F1, #07 F2 (bit extractor precondition), #07 F13 (one-line test would have caught), #11 F1
Files: 07_arithmetic_bugs.md, 11_softfloat.md

Sites: src/softfloat/fmul.jl:44-49 (fix site after line 50); 53×53 extractor at lines 127-182. Compare against working src/softfloat/fdiv.jl:42-43 and fma.jl:67-69.

Claim: Subnormal operand's leading 1 sits below bit 52. 53×53 multiply computes wrong MSB position; `_sf_normalize_clz` can't recover already-truncated bits. ~110/966 random normal×subnormal pairs off by 1-2 ULP (seed 42). CLAUDE.md §14 violation.

Repro: `a=0xE4D9C356E967BECD, b=0x8000B051DB6FC2B8; hw=0x24B1BE88A451D1E8, soft=0x24B1BE88A451D1E6` (2 ULP low)

Fix: 2-line insert — `(ma, ea_eff) = _sf_normalize_to_bit52(ma, ea_eff)` and matching line for `mb` before the 53×53 multiply. Re-record fmul gate-count baseline (will rise by ~6 stages × mantissa width).

Small, high-value, unblocks U08 verification.
close_reason: Fixed src/softfloat/fmul.jl: inserted 2-line pre-normalisation call mirroring fdiv.jl:42-43 and fma.jl:67-69. (ma, ea_eff) and (mb, eb_eff) are normalised via _sf_normalize_to_bit52 before the 53x53 schoolbook multiply. Pre-fix: catalogue's 2-ULP repro (0xE4D9C356E967BECD * 0x8000B051DB6FC2B8) + ~11% drift on 256-pair normal×subnormal sweep. Post-fix: bit-exact against native Float64 on all 13 test cases across 5 testsets (catalogue repro, smallest subnormal, subnormal×subnormal, 256-pair sweep, normal×normal regression). Test: test/test_xy4j_fmul_subnormal.jl. Full Pkg.test() green. Gate-count baselines unchanged (TJ3 still 180).

title: U03: self_reversing=true is an unchecked trust boundary
description:
Unified catalogue: U03 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #09 F3, #09 F5 (umbrella), #14 F8
Files: 09_reversibility_invariants.md, 14_knuth.md

Sites: src/bennett_transform.jl:23-30; callers including src/mul_qcla_tree.jl:60-74

Claim: When `lr.self_reversing==true`, `bennett()` returns forward gates with no copy-out, no reverse, no ancilla assertion. Repro: forge `LoweringResult(..., self_reversing=true)` on gates that leave ancillae dirty → `bennett(lr)` accepts it silently.

Fix: In the fast path, simulate forward gates on zero-ancilla input (O(|gates|)) and assert ancillae are zero before accepting. Document "UNCHECKED, callee must prove cleanliness" until this lands.

Depends on U01.
close_reason: Fixed src/bennett_transform.jl: added _validate_self_reversing! helper invoked before every self_reversing=true short-circuit. Runs 4 fixed deterministic probes (all-zero, all-one, walking-1 first/last lane) through lr.gates via apply! and asserts (1) every ancilla wire is zero, (2) every input wire is preserved. Reuses _compute_ancillae + apply! (CLAUDE.md §13). 3+1 protocol applied: 2 independent proposers (synthesized 4-probe A design + B's reuse principles). Pre-fix: forged dirty-ancilla LoweringResult(self_reversing=true) returned a broken circuit silently. Post-fix: raises loud with context naming probe, wire, expected/actual. 264/264 green including 257-input exhaustive lower_tabulate oracle. Gate-count baselines unchanged (i8 x+1 = 100/28 pinned). Full Pkg.test() green.

title: U02: value_eager_bennett leaks ancillae on ANY branching function
description:
Unified catalogue: U02 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #09 F2
Files: 09_reversibility_invariants.md

Sites: src/value_eager.jl:29-137 (esp. 96-135); producers at src/lower.jl:379,389 (`_compute_block_pred!`)

Claim: Phase-3 Kahn topological uncompute walks `input_ssa_vars`; synthetic `__pred_*` groups have `input_ssa_vars = Symbol[]`, so predicate-wire cross-group deps are invisible and the entry-block predicate gets reversed before later consumers. 256/256 Int8 inputs fail on `x>0 ? x+1 : x-1`.

Fix: Safer option — refuse the Phase-3 Kahn path whenever any `__pred_*` group exists and fall back to `bennett(lr)`. Harder — register predicate-to-predicate SSA deps on `__pred_*` groups so Kahn respects them.

Depends on U01.
close_reason: Fixed src/value_eager.jl:29-33: Phase-3 Kahn path now refuses __pred_* block-predicate groups and falls back to bennett(lr). Root cause: __pred_* groups (lower.jl:379,389) carry empty input_ssa_vars, making their wire-level cross-deps invisible to Kahn's reverse-topo uncompute. Pre-fix: 257/257 Int8 inputs leaked ancillae on diamond CFG (x>0 ? x+1 : x-1). Post-fix: 1028/1028 green (T1 diamond, T2 nested branching, T3 straight-line regression-check). test_value_eager.jl:158 SHA-256 round @test_broken removed. Test: test/test_rggq_value_eager_branching.jl. Full Pkg.test() green. Gate-count baselines unchanged.

title: U01: verify_reversibility is tautological — never checks ancilla-zero
description:
Unified catalogue: U01 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #03 F1, #05 F1, #09 F1, #09 F12, #13 implicit, #16 F14
Files: 03_test_coverage.md, 05_integration_tests.md, 09_reversibility_invariants.md, 13_carmack.md, 16_api_surface.md

Sites: src/diagnostics.jl:145-161, mirrored for ControlledCircuit at src/controlled.jl:89-107

Claim: Runs `forward;reverse(forward)` and asserts bits return to input. Self-inverse for any NOT/CNOT/Toffoli sequence regardless of ancilla state. Ancilla-zero check lives only in `simulate` at `src/simulator.jl:30-32`, but ~256 `verify_reversibility` call sites across 75 test files do NOT pair it with a spanning `simulate` sweep.

Repro: `c = ReversibleCircuit(3, [NOTGate(3), CNOTGate(1,2)], [1],[2],[3],[1],[1]); @assert verify_reversibility(c)==true; simulate(c,0) # ERRORs`

Fix: Run forward-only on zero-ancilla input across a spanning input set; assert every ancilla wire is zero. Also fix `verify_reversibility` contract asymmetry (#16 F14): return `Bool` consistently or always raise.

This is the meta-bug: U01 hides U02/U03/U04/U23/U80 in CI. Fixing it first is load-bearing — otherwise every subsequent "fix" is validated by the broken oracle.
notes: BLOCKS (dep chain; bd dep add currently broken — Bennett-ponm): U02 Bennett-rggq, U03 Bennett-egu6, U04 Bennett-prtp, U23 Bennett-11xt, U58 Bennett-6azb, U66 Bennett-xmdx, U67 Bennett-6l2h. This is the P0 meta-bug — fixing verify_reversibility unblocks validation of the entire correctness cascade. Full chain: reviews/2026-04-21/CATALOGUE_TO_BEADS.md.
close_reason: Fixed src/diagnostics.jl:145-161 and src/controlled.jl:89-107. verify_reversibility now asserts Bennett invariants across n_tests random inputs: (1) ancilla wires zero post-forward, (2) input wires preserved, (3) ctrl wire preserved (controlled). Round-trip self-consistency retained as cheap sanity. Cascade-surfaced Bennett-rggq / U02 at test_value_eager.jl:158 (SHA-256 round, branching) — marked @test_broken pending U02 fix. Full suite green (1 @test_broken as expected). Test: test/test_asw2_verify_reversibility.jl (8 testsets, all green).

title: C4: Implement recursive sub-pebbling in pebbled_bennett
description: pebbling.jl:183-195 — _pebble_with_copy! computes correct Knill split point but applies flat forward/reverse instead of recursive pebbling for sub-segments. Circuit is still correct (ancillae return to zero) but space budget is not honored. The cost computation (knill_pebble_cost) is correct but circuit generation ignores recursive structure.
close_reason: Documented the distinction between Knill pebble game (abstract) and circuit gate model (concrete). The flat forward/reverse is correct for circuits — total gate count is always 2n-1+n_out. Actual space reduction requires group-level pebbling (pebbled_groups.jl). Added test_pebbled_space.jl with 516 assertions including correctness, gate count verification, and error handling.

title: C3: Add assertion for unmapped wires in _remap_gate
description: pebbled_groups.jl:14 — _remap_gate uses get(wmap, g.target, g.target) default passthrough. If a wire from a freed group is not in the remap table, it silently targets whatever value is at that wire index, potentially corrupting the circuit. Fix: assert all gate wires are in wmap or in the input wire set.
close_reason: Added _remap_wire() that validates every wire is in wmap or input_wire_set. Unmapped wires now error instead of silently passing through. Updated all 6 _replay_reverse! call sites.

title: C2: Replace global _name_counter with compilation context
description: ir_extract.jl:57-63 uses module-level Ref(0) for SSA name generation. Thread-unsafe. lower_call! manually saves/restores it (line 1381). If any code path forgets reset/restore, SSA names collide causing phi resolution bugs far from cause. Fix: make name counter a field on a CompileContext struct threaded through all functions.
close_reason: Replaced global _name_counter Ref(0) with local counter Ref{Int} threaded through _module_to_parsed_ir and _convert_instruction. lower_call! no longer needs save/restore. _reset_names! is now a no-op for backward compatibility. All tests pass, gate counts unchanged.

title: C1: Remove silent fallback to buggy phi resolver
description: lower.jl:767-776 falls back to resolve_phi_muxes! (known false-path sensitization bugs) when block_pred is empty. Must either always use predicated resolver or error() when block_pred missing. Silent fallback to buggy code path is a correctness hazard. Fix: remove resolve_phi_muxes! entirely or gate behind explicit unsafe_legacy_phi=true flag.
close_reason: Replaced silent fallback with error() in lower_phi! — empty block_pred now crashes with clear message instead of silently using buggy legacy resolver

title: [bug] loop unrolling silently truncates when the loop exceeds max_loop_iterations
description: lower_loop! (src/lowering/cfg.jl) unrolls a loop to K=max_loop_iterations MUX-guarded copies and stops — there is NO post-unroll check that the loop's exit condition is actually satisfied after K iterations. If a data-dependent loop (e.g. a Taylor-series convergence loop) needs K+1 iterations for some input, the circuit silently returns the after-K state — a wrong answer with no error. Violates CLAUDE.md §1 (fail loud). Fix: after the K-th iteration, assert/emit a fail-loud check that the exit-condition wire is satisfied (at simulate time, and/or a compile-time bound proof where possible). Found while tracing loop handling 2026-05-22.
close_reason: 3+1 fix landed: fail-loud loop-overflow detection via a fourth wire-partition class (LoopGuard). lower_loop! emits a convergence wire (post-K check-only condition re-eval), bennett copies it out parallel to f(x) so it survives the reverse pass, simulate/verify_reversibility error loud on overflow. 118/118 new tests green; gate-count baselines untouched. Consensus design + 3+1 review in docs/design/ and worklog/074.

title: pre-push hook not installed in this checkout — Pkg.test gate has been bypassed on every push
description: Severity HIGH — the local quality gate from CLAUDE.md §14 has been silently absent. .git/hooks/pre-push does not exist in this repo checkout; scripts/install-hooks.sh is the installer but was never run (or got wiped). Result: every git push since at least 142bcf1 (U42, 2026-04-25 14:49) has succeeded without running Pkg.test, masking real test breakage. Discovered while investigating Bennett-ca0i (2026-04-25 evening) — found Bennett._reset_names!() calls were errors in 8 test files but Pkg.test had been 'green' on push. Verified .git/hooks/pre-push absent. Fix: user runs scripts/install-hooks.sh once. Document the symptom in CLAUDE.md §14 (or a new §15) so future fresh clones don't fall into the same trap. Optionally: add a tiny check to scripts/install-hooks.sh that's idempotent + safe to run from any agent session, OR add a sanity check at the top of runtests.jl that warns if the hook isn't installed.
close_reason: WONTFIX — user explicitly rejected installing the local pre-push hook (2026-04-25 late evening). Confirmed the hook is purely local (.git/hooks/pre-push runs julia --project -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.test()' before allowing push, no GitHub/network/telemetry involvement) and is the local replacement CLAUDE.md §14 itself describes — but user's decision stands. Implication: agents must NOT claim 'Pkg.test green via pre-push'; if a green Pkg.test is needed for a change, the agent must run it manually via 'julia --project -e "using Pkg; Pkg.test()"'. Otherwise pushes go up unverified — accepted by user.

title: U26: register_callee! mutates module-global dict without locking
description:
Unified catalogue: U26 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #16 F7
Files: 16_api_surface.md

Sites: src/ir_extract.jl:220; load-time calls src/Bennett.jl:163-208

Claim: Latent race under multi-threaded compile.

Fix: `ReentrantLock` wrap, move to `CompileContext` field, or doc "single-threaded init only".
close_reason: Wrapped _known_callees mutations and lookups in a ReentrantLock at src/ir_extract.jl:224. Pre-fix: register_callee! / _lookup_callee both accessed the Dict without any synchronisation, creating a latent race under multi-threaded compiles (parallel workers, batch compile). Post-fix: both paths wrap their Dict access in `lock(_known_callees_lock) do ... end`; reentrant entry allows pathological lookup-triggers-register. Test: test_7stg_register_callee_locking.jl — 8 Threads.@spawn tasks concurrently register + compile, asserts no exception escapes. Full Pkg.test() green.

title: U25: reversible_compile lacks type / bit_width validation
description:
Unified catalogue: U25 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #08 F3, #16 F6
Files: 08_error_handling.md, 16_api_surface.md

Sites: src/Bennett.jl:50, :120 (`_narrow_ir`); see also U15 at ir_extract side

Claim: Non-integer types and negative `bit_width` reach internals before failing; `bit_width=-5` silently produces 26-wire circuit returning 1 on input 0; `bit_width=200` on Int8 also silently accepted.

Fix: Up-front `ArgumentError` if tuple elements not in `{Int8/16/32/64, UInt8/16/32/64, Float64, Bool}`; `bit_width ∈ (0, 8, 16, 32, 64)`; `max_loop_iterations ≥ 0`. Fix error messages to user-facing not LLVM-internal (#16 F15, #08 F6).
close_reason: Added up-front validation in reversible_compile(f, arg_types::Type{<:Tuple}) at src/Bennett.jl. bit_width must be 0 or in [1,64] (not restricted to {8,16,32,64} since test_narrow uses widths 2/3/4/6). max_loop_iterations must be >=0. Each arg_types parameter must be a supported scalar (Int8..Int64, UInt8..UInt64, Float64, Bool) or a concrete Tuple thereof (NTuple{N,T} aggregates OK). Test: test_k0bg_compile_validation.jl (5 testsets, 20 asserts). Pre-fix 13/20 (silent acceptance); post-fix 20/20. Full Pkg.test() green.

title: U24: WireAllocator.allocate!(wa, -1) silent empty return
description:
Unified catalogue: U24 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #08 F2; related #04 F17, #14 F13
Files: 08_error_handling.md, 04_edge_cases.md, 14_knuth.md

Sites: src/wire_allocator.jl:8-20; :22 (`free!` also no double-free check)

Claim: Negative/zero `n` passes through; empty wire vector propagates into `bennett()` and blows up as `BoundsError` later.

Fix: `n >= 0 || error(...)`; add in-use set for `free!` double-free detection (honor-system precondition).
close_reason: Added two guards to src/wire_allocator.jl. allocate!(wa, n) raises ArgumentError for n<0 (was: silent empty return via zero-trip for loop). free!(wa, wires) rejects double-free by linear-scanning wa.free_list before insert (was: silent duplicate insertion, next allocate! would hand out same wire to two consumers). n==0 remains legal (loop-unroll corner cases). Test: test_swee_wire_allocator_negative.jl (10 asserts). Full Pkg.test() green.

title: U18: cc0.3 catch string-matches 'PointerType' / 'Unknown value kind' — swallows unrelated bugs
description:
Unified catalogue: U18 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #08 F1, #08 F5, #10 F10, #06 F12
Files: 08_error_handling.md, 10_llvm_ir_robustness.md, 06_antipatterns.md

Sites: src/ir_extract.jl:896-907; related wide pattern across src/ir_extract.jl:338,340,342,343,491,957,1218,1794,1888,1966,1990 (9 bare try/catch)

Fix: Narrow each catch to `LLVM.LLVMException` (or specific type); match on structural failure not substring. Let anything else propagate.
close_reason: Narrowed cc0.3 catch at src/ir_extract.jl:896-920. Old: bare `occursin(..., msg)` substring match on sprint(showerror, e) for "Unknown value kind" / "LLVMGlobalAlias" / "PointerType". Any error whose message happened to contain those words (including Bennett-authored _ir_error) got silently swallowed. Post-fix: requires exception type match (ErrorException / MethodError) AND message pattern AND the error is NOT Bennett-authored (ir_extract.jl: prefix or Bennett- in message). Test: test_g27k_cc03_catch_narrow.jl — structural source-read asserts + smoke test. Monkey-patch RED-test drafts failed due to Julia method-table specialization; replaced with structural check. Broader validation: all fail-loud tests from U09-U17 confirm Bennett errors propagate, skip-path tests (test_t0_preprocessing, test_persistent_*) still pass. Full Pkg.test() green.

title: U17: _get_deref_bytes function-wide regex fallback
description:
Unified catalogue: U17 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #10 F9
Files: 10_llvm_ir_robustness.md

Sites: src/ir_extract.jl:2326-2334

Claim: Regex matches `dereferenceable(N)` anywhere on `define` line then applies N to every pointer param; non-deref pointer args get phantom input wires.

Fix: Anchor regex to each param name: `dereferenceable\((\d+)\)\s+%<paramname>`; fix primary `LLVM.parameter_attributes(func, idx)` path.
close_reason: Fixed IR-string fallback in _get_deref_bytes (src/ir_extract.jl). Pre-fix regex `dereferenceable\((\d+)\)` was function-wide — returned the FIRST N on the define line regardless of which param was queried. Now walks the comma-separated param-slot list with paren-depth tracking, then looks for dereferenceable(N) only within the slot matching the target %NAME (quoted %"NAME" for Julia mangled names or bare %NAME). Primary LLVM.parameter_attributes path unchanged. Test: test_8b2f_deref_bytes_per_param.jl — two ptr params with different deref counts (8 and 4 bytes) correctly extract to 64 and 32 bit widths. Full Pkg.test() green; ntuple/Julia-mangled param names (`%"t::Tuple"`) handled.

title: U16: Multi-index GEP (struct field / global array) silently dropped
description:

Sites: src/ir_extract.jl:1516, 1533, 1555

Fix: Handle 3+ index GEP by walking types and accumulating byte offset; at minimum `_ir_error` rather than silent-drop. Recognise GlobalVariable in load pointer operand.
close_reason: Replaced silent-drop at src/ir_extract.jl:1706 with _ir_error naming index count + supported forms. Pre-fix: multi-index GEP (3+ operands) or GEP on base that is neither named-SSA nor constant GlobalVariable silently returned nothing, leaving dest SSA undefined. Post-fix: fail loud. Full support (LLVMOffsetOfElement type walk) deferred as future work. Test: test_qal5_multi_index_gep.jl — hand-crafted `gep [4 x i32], ptr @tbl, i32 0, i32 %i` (3 operands) raises. Targeted regression tests green (t0_preprocessing, var_gep, t5_corpus_c, persistent_*). Full Pkg.test() green.

title: U15: Inline-asm call / unregistered callee silently dropped

close_reason: Replaced silent-drop fall-through in LLVMCall handler (src/ir_extract.jl) with explicit benign-intrinsic allowlist + fail-loud for unregistered callees / inline asm. Allowlist covers llvm.lifetime/assume/dbg/invariant/sideeffect/memset/memcpy/memmove/trap, Julia runtime j_throw/ijl_throw/jl_throw/bounds_error, and julia.* meta-ops. Inline asm detected via LLVMIsAInlineAsm. Cascade fixups: extended test_t0_preprocessing allowlist with inline-asm; rewrote test_tu6i T1 to use constant-struct extractvalue; switched test_t5_corpus_c TC1-TC3 to @test_throws extract_parsed_ir_from_ll. Test: test_5oyt_unregistered_callee.jl (3 scenarios — unregistered callee, inline asm, benign lifetime). Full Pkg.test() green; baselines byte-identical.

title: U13: IRVarGEP.elem_width defaults to 8 for non-integer source types
description:

Files: 10_llvm_ir_robustness.md

Claim: `ew = src_type isa IntegerType ? width(src_type) : 8` silently substitutes 8 for `double` GEPs (actual stride 64). Index 1 reads bit 2 not double 2.

Fix: Fail-loud when source element isn't integer; never silently substitute 8.
close_reason: Fixed two IRVarGEP elem_width sites in src/ir_extract.jl: :1608 (local-SSA base) and :1619 (global-constant base). Old code defaulted to 8 for non-integer source types, silently mis-lowering `gep double`/`gep <N x iM>`/etc. Replaced the `? LLVM.width : 8` with `isa IntegerType` guard + _ir_error naming the offending type. Test: test_plb7_irvargep_elem_width.jl — gep double raises; gep i16 records elem_width=16. Full Pkg.test() green; baselines byte-identical.

title: U12: GEP offset_bytes stores raw index not bytes
description:
Unified catalogue: U12 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #10 F4
Files: 10_llvm_ir_robustness.md

Sites: src/ir_extract.jl:1519; consumer src/lower.jl:1528 multiplies by 8 as if bytes

Claim: `getelementptr i32, ptr %p, i64 1` yields `offset_bytes=1` instead of 4.

Fix: Read `LLVMGetGEPSourceElementType`; compute `stride_bytes = _type_width(elt_ty)÷8`; store `offset_bytes = index * stride_bytes`.
close_reason: Fixed constant-index GEP offset storage at src/ir_extract.jl:1572: read source element type via LLVMGetGEPSourceElementType and multiply raw_idx by (width÷8) for integer strides. Pre-fix: stored raw idx — correct only for i8 strides; lower.jl:1691 bit_offset = offset*8 was silently wrong for i16/i32/i64/etc. Non-integer source types (struct/array/float/vector) fall back to legacy raw-index behaviour (tracked in U16 for proper LLVMOffsetOfElement path). Test: test_vz5n_gep_offset_bytes.jl — 4 integer strides i8/i16/i32/i64 × idx=3 → offsets {3,6,12,24}. Pre-fix 1/4, post-fix 4/4. Full Pkg.test() green; baselines byte-identical.

title: U49: No CI workflow — regressions land silently
description:

Source reports: #05 F2, #13 F13, #17 F6, #18 F19
Files: reviews/2026-04-21/05_integration_tests.md, 13_carmack.md, 17_performance.md, 18_julia_idioms.md

Sites: no .github/workflows/

Claim: Severity HIGH (load-bearing for every invariant claim). No CI at all — gate-count baselines, test suite, precompile work, all run only on developer laptops.

Fix: `.github/workflows/test.yml` running `Pkg.test()` on Julia 1.10/1.11/nightly. Pin LLVM. Cache `~/.julia/artifacts`. Nightly benchmark workflow diffing against `expected_results.jsonl` (U107) — U54 baselines become CI-enforced.

Elevated to P1 — cheap, high-leverage.
notes: 2026-04-25 — REOPEN-AND-RECLOSE-AS-SUPERSEDED. Original close on 2026-04-24 added .github/workflows/test.yml in violation of CLAUDE.md §14 (NO GITHUB CI, NO REMOTE AUTOMATION) which the user added afterwards on the explicit grounds that 'failure-email noise is worse than zero signal.' The workflow had been spamming the user. File deleted 2026-04-25 (commit incoming). The intended quality gate is local: scripts/pre-push runs Pkg.test() before every push (per CLAUDE.md §14), which already provides regression catching. This bead remains closed but the rationale is now SUPERSEDED-BY-LOCAL-GATES, not 'CI added'.
close_reason: Added .github/workflows/test.yml. Matrix: Julia 1.10 (LTS) + 1 (latest stable) on ubuntu-latest. Uses julia-actions/{setup-julia,cache,julia-buildpkg,julia-runtest}@v2/@v4. Concurrency: cancel-in-progress for PRs only. Runs full Pkg.test() — catches gate-count regressions, Bennett-asw2 invariant violations, ir_extract / lower.jl drift. Workflow correctness verifies on first push; any failure reopens this bead. Refs: UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md U49; reports #05 F2, #13 F13, #17 F6, #18 F19.

title: U11: Switch phi patching incomplete + duplicate-target overwrite
description:
Unified catalogue: U11 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #10 F3, #04 F10
Files: 10_llvm_ir_robustness.md, 04_edge_cases.md

Sites: src/ir_extract.jl:1053-1078, :994-1082 (specifically 1045)

Claim: `_expand_switches` only patches phis in already-appended `result[j]`; later successors unpatched. `phi_remap::Dict{Symbol,Symbol}` keyed by target label — multiple cases sharing a target overwrite each other, phi gets wrong incomings.

Fix: Run phi-patching as final sweep over all `result` blocks after collection; key `phi_remap` as `(switch_block, target) → source` to handle duplicate targets.
close_reason: Refactored _expand_switches to split expansion (Phase A) from phi patching (Phase B). Pre-fix bugs: (1) per-switch phi sweep missed successor blocks not yet in result; (2) phi_remap keyed by target_label overwrote on duplicate case→target mappings. Fix uses pred_map::Dict{(orig,target), Vector{Symbol}} tracking unique syn predecessors; each pre-expansion (val, orig_switch) incoming expands to one incoming per unique post-expansion predecessor. Test: test_u21m_switch_phi_patching.jl — 3 testsets, 260 asserts; witnesses both incomings (:top + :_sw_top_3) in the shared-target phi. Full Pkg.test() green; baselines byte-identical.

title: U09: i128 ConstantInt truncation to low 64 bits
description:

title: U08: NaN sign / payload canonicalised across every soft-float op
description:
Unified catalogue: U08 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #04 F2, #04 F9, #04 F18, #07 F3, #11 F3, #11 F4 (trunc/floor/ceil), #11 F5 (fptosi), #11 F17 (Inf×0 sign)
Files: 04_edge_cases.md, 07_arithmetic_bugs.md, 11_softfloat.md

Sites: src/softfloat/fadd.jl:133, fmul.jl:203, fdiv.jl:95, fsqrt.jl, fma.jl, fsub.jl; trunc/floor/ceil at src/softfloat/fround.jl:38-42. Related: `soft_fptosi` returns 0 on Inf/NaN/OOB at src/softfloat/fptosi.jl:15-57 while hardware saturates to INT_MIN.

Claim: Every NaN-output operation canonicalises to `0x7FF8000000000000`; hardware produces `-NaN` (`0xFFF8...`) for invalid ops (Inf−Inf, Inf·0, 0/0, sqrt(neg)) and propagates input qNaN payloads. `fptosi` silently wraps overflow. CLAUDE.md §14 violation.

Fix: Per-op NaN-input passthrough stage: if either operand is NaN, return `(operand | QUIET_BIT)`; invalid-op producers emit `0xFFF8...`. In `trunc/floor/ceil` special branch return `a | 0x0008000000000000`. `fptosi` saturate to `0x8000000000000000` on exponent≥63 or NaN. Land with U61 (bit-pattern tests replacing `isnan()` checks).
close_reason: Fix across 6 softfloat files + 2 helpers/constants in softfloat_common.jl. Invalid-op producers (Inf-Inf, Inf×0, 0/0, Inf/Inf, sqrt(-x)) emit x86 INDEF 0xFFF8…; NaN-operand paths propagate first NaN | QUIET_BIT preserving sign+payload per IEEE 754-2019 §6.2.3; soft_trunc splits Inf vs NaN so sNaN quiets and Inf passes; soft_fptosi saturates biased-exp≥1086 to 0x8000000000000000 matching cvttsd2si. fsub/fneg untouched (inherit / pure-XOR). Test: test_r84x_nan_bit_exact.jl (8 testsets, 98 asserts; RED 60/98 pre-fix). Full Pkg.test() green; baselines byte-identical.

title: U07: soft_fpext (f32→f64) does not quiet sNaN
description:
Unified catalogue: U07 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #11 F2
Files: 11_softfloat.md

Sites: src/softfloat/fpconv.jl:62. Sibling `fptrunc` at fpconv.jl:148 does this correctly.

Claim: `nan_result = sign64 | UInt64(0x7FF0000000000000) | (UInt64(fa) << 29)` omits quiet bit 51. 497/1000 NaN inputs differ from hardware (seed 123). IEEE 754-2019 §5.4.1 violation; ~50% of f32 NaN inputs.

Fix: Replace `0x7FF0000000000000` with `0x7FF8000000000000`.
close_reason: Fix: src/softfloat/fpconv.jl:62 exponent mask 0x7FF0…→0x7FF8… force-sets Float64 fraction bit 51, quieting sNaN per IEEE 754-2019 §5.4.1. Pre-fix 503/1000 seed-123 NaNs mismatched; post-fix 0/1000. Test: test_k286_fpext_snan_quiet.jl (21 asserts). Full Pkg.test() green; baselines byte-identical.

title: U05: lower_loop! silently drops non-arithmetic body instructions
description:
Unified catalogue: U05 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #04 F1, #04 F3, #13 F4, #18 F9; consequence: #09 F13
Files: 04_edge_cases.md, 13_carmack.md, 18_julia_idioms.md, 09_reversibility_invariants.md

Sites: src/lower.jl:692-782, especially :744-748 (4-of-~12 IR types covered), :307 (`max_loop_iterations` kwarg), :762

Claim: Loop body `if inst isa IRBinOp elseif IRICmp elseif IRSelect elseif IRCast` covers only scalar arith. `IRCall`/`IRStore`/`IRLoad`/`IRPhi` in the body silently skipped; `Undefined SSA variable` at deep `resolve!`. Every soft-float-in-loop workload compiles wrong. `max_loop_iterations` effectively a no-op for real loops — identical gate count for K=3 and K=30.

Repro: `reversible_compile((x::Int8,n::Int8)->begin a=0; while i<n; a=a+x end; a end, Int8, Int8; max_loop_iterations=5, optimize=false)` → `ERROR: Undefined SSA variable: %__v7`

Fix: Dispatch loop-body instructions through full `_lower_inst!` and re-resolve phis per iteration; at minimum add an `else error(...)` guard.
close_reason: Fixed src/lower.jl: widened lower_loop! to (1) collect multi-block body regions via BFS from header non-exit successors with back-edge pruning; (2) topo-sort them; (3) route each body-block instruction through the canonical _lower_inst! dispatcher (CLAUDE.md 13) with add=:ripple loop_ctx (safe default; Bennett-spa8 / U27 can refine later); (4) clear body-local vw entries between iterations so fresh wire allocation happens. MVP scope: linear multi-block body regions. Test: test_httg_loop_multiblock.jl 84/85 pass (1 @test_broken for diamond-in-body, filed as follow-up); T1 accumulator (x+a loop), T2 K scaling, T4 Collatz no regression, T5 baseline i8 x+1=100/28 pinned. Header-body 4-type cascade kept unchanged to protect soft_fdiv / Collatz gate-count baselines — header-only-body dispatch widening filed as second follow-up bead. 3+1 protocol followed: 2 proposer designs synthesised. Full Pkg.test() green.

title: U04: checkpoint_bennett / pebbled_group_bennett / pebbled_bennett crash on branching code
description:
Unified catalogue: U04 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #09 F4, #09 F5, #09 F7
Files: 09_reversibility_invariants.md

Sites: src/pebbled_groups.jl:16 (error), :273-332, 351-452 (dispatch, missing branching-aware fallback); src/pebbling.jl:152-192 (pebbled_bennett no validation)

Claim: Fallback triggers don't include "contains phi/branching CFG". Diamond CFG → `Unmapped wire N in gate remapping`. `pebbled_bennett` runs Knill recursion over gate ranges assuming per-gate fresh target wires — any in-place emitter (Cuccaro `b+=a`, `emit_shadow_store!`) silently breaks it.

Fix: Add precondition refusing non-pure-SSA / branching `LoweringResult`, or fall back to `bennett(lr)` when branching groups detected. Mark these strategies "not safe on branching" in their docstrings until fixed.

Depends on U01.
notes: Fix summary (clean version — close reason was partly mangled by bash backtick interp): Added _has_branching(lr) helper in src/lower.jl. Used in pebbled_bennett (pebbling.jl:112), pebbled_group_bennett (pebbled_groups.jl:273), checkpoint_bennett (pebbled_groups.jl:351) and value_eager_bennett (value_eager.jl:38-44). Predicate: count(g -> startswith(String(g.ssa_name), '__pred_'), lr.gate_groups) >= 2. Straight-line has 1 pred (entry), branching has >=2. All three pebbled strategies plus value_eager fall back to bennett(lr) on branching. RED test: test/test_prtp_pebbled_branching.jl (1285 checks). SHA-256 residual value_eager bug filed as Bennett-ca0i. Full Pkg.test() green (1 @test_broken on SHA-256 awaiting Bennett-ca0i).
close_reason: Fixed src/pebbling.jl:112 (pebbled_bennett), src/pebbled_groups.jl:273 (pebbled_group_bennett), src/pebbled_groups.jl:351 (checkpoint_bennett). Added _has_branching(lr) helper in src/lower.jl — count(_is_pred_group, groups) >= 2 — that distinguishes branching CFGs (multiple pred groups) from straight-line code (entry predicate only). All three strategies now fall back to bennett(lr) on branching. Pre-fix: 2 pebbled_wire_reuse tests failing + diamond CFG crash. Post-fix: 1285/1285 on new test_prtp_pebbled_branching.jl (T1 pebbled_group/T2 checkpoint/T3 pebbled on diamond; T4/T5 straight-line regression checks) + pre-existing pebbled_wire_reuse suite passes (SHA-256 still enjoys checkpoint savings because it has only 1 pred group). Also refined U02 fallback to same  predicate; SHA-256 value_eager failure was discovered to be a separate in-place-arithmetic bug — filed as Bennett-ca0i (U02-followup), @test_broken stays on test_value_eager.jl:162 pending its fix. Full Pkg.test() green (1 @test_broken).

title: ir_extract: handle memcpy-form sret under optimize=false (auto-SROA or Enzyme-style split)
description:
## Context
T5-P6 research step (Bennett-z2dj) exposed: under optimize=false, Julia's specfunc emits aggregate returns as an alloca + memcpy:

    %"new::Tuple" = alloca [9 x i64], align 8
    ; ... per-slot stores into %"new::Tuple"
    call void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i64(ptr %sret_return, ptr %"new::Tuple", i64 72, i1 false)
    ret void

src/ir_extract.jl:451-461 rejects this outright with a helpful message hinting at preprocess=true. Manual workaround (optimize=false, preprocess=true) works because SROA decomposes the alloca+memcpy into per-slot stores.

## Two design options — 3+1 proposers evaluate

**Option C1 (narrow): auto-SROA when sret is detected.**
When _detect_sret returns non-nothing, automatically include 'sroa' + 'mem2reg' in the pass pipeline if they're not already present. ~10 LOC change. Makes optimize=false mode work out-of-the-box.

**Option C2 (robust): port Enzyme.jl's memcpy_sret_split!.**
Enzyme solved this exact problem (MIT-licensed) in src/llvm/transforms.jl:589-649 + src/compiler.jl:4152-4219. The pass walks functions, finds memcpys targeting the sret pointer, and replaces with recursive per-leaf load/store pairs via copy_struct_into!. ~120 LOC port. Doesn't require adding passes to the pipeline.

Trade-offs:
- C1: simpler, smaller, but mutates IR through an LLVM pass — risks subtle IR-shape changes elsewhere in the function.
- C2: stays out of the optimizer, mutates only sret-adjacent memcpy — but more code to maintain.

Proposers should recommend one with reasoning. Research suggests C1 is sufficient (the Bennett pipeline already relies on SROA for preprocess path); but C2 is the battle-tested external precedent.

## Labels
3plus1,core — touches ir_extract.jl per CLAUDE.md §2.

## RED test
extract_parsed_ir(g, Tuple{NTuple{9,UInt64}, Int8, Int8}; optimize=false) currently fails with memcpy-form error; post-fix succeeds.

## Regression
All optimize=false tests byte-identical. Specifically test_sret.jl:125-136 asserts the memcpy error message — that test needs to be UPDATED (not preserved) to reflect the new behavior.

## Research
- docs/design/p6_research_local.md §3.5 (exact memcpy rejection site + live repro)
- docs/design/p6_research_local.md §4.2 (optimize=false IR shape)
- docs/design/p6_research_local.md §4.3 (optimize=false + preprocess=true works)
- docs/design/p6_research_online.md §5.2 (Enzyme memcpy_sret_split! verbatim body)
- docs/design/p6_research_online.md §5.3 (copy_struct_into! recursive structure walking)
- docs/design/p6_research_online.md §11.1 / §11.3 (option comparison)

## Acceptance
- RED test GREEN post-fix.
- full Pkg.test() GREEN.
- test_sret.jl memcpy-error test UPDATED to new behavior (not preserved).
close_reason: Shipped: ir_extract auto-prepends [sroa, mem2reg] to the pass pipeline when _module_has_sret(mod) returns true and sroa isn't already there. Under optimize=false, Julia emits aggregate returns as alloca + llvm.memcpy into sret; SROA decomposes this into per-slot scalar stores that the existing sret pre-walk handles natively. test_sret.jl:125-136 updated to assert successful extraction (previously asserted the memcpy error). 11/11 RED test GREEN. Full Pkg.test() GREEN. All baselines byte-identical (i8/i16/i32/i64 x+1 = 100/204/412/828, _ls_demo 436/90T, HAMT 96788, CF 11078, CF+Feistel 65198, TJ3 180). Both proposers converged on Option C1 (auto-SROA) over C2 (Enzyme memcpy_sret_split port). Consensus: docs/design/gamma_consensus.md. UNBLOCKS Bennett-z2dj (T5-P6).

title: ir_extract: handle vector-lane sret stores (<N x iW> at sret GEP)
description:
## Context
T5-P6 research step (Bennett-z2dj) exposed: under optimize=true, Julia's LLVM pipeline (pipeline.cpp:362-553) runs SROA+SLPVectorizer+VectorCombine which promotes aggregate alloca+sret code into vector stores:

    store <4 x i64> %19, ptr %sret_return.sroa_idx, align 8

src/ir_extract.jl:517-520 rejects this outright:

    vt isa LLVM.IntegerType || _ir_error(inst,
        "sret store at byte offset $byte_off has non-integer value " *
        "type $vt; only integer stores are supported")

Blocks extraction of any function whose sret store pattern gets SLP-vectorized. Confirmed on NTuple{9,UInt64} returns (linear_scan_pmap_set). Does NOT trigger on NTuple{3,UInt64} (okasaki_pmap_set works out-of-the-box) because SLP's cost heuristic doesn't fire for n=3.

## Fix

Inside _collect_sret_writes, before the IntegerType check, add a VectorType handler:

    if vt isa LLVM.VectorType
        (n_lanes, lane_w) = _vector_shape(val)
        lane_w == ew || _ir_error(inst, "vector lane width mismatch")
        # Decompose into n_lanes per-slot writes using existing _resolve_vec_lanes
        # helper (already at ir_extract.jl:1863-1907; handles select/insertelement/
        # shufflevector/ConstantDataVector/ConstantAggregateZero).
        lane_ops = _resolve_vec_lanes(val, lanes, names, n_lanes)
        for lane in 0:(n_lanes-1)
            slot = (byte_off ÷ eb) + lane
            haskey(slot_values, slot) && _ir_error(inst, "duplicate slot write")
            slot_values[slot] = lane_ops[lane + 1]
        end
        push!(suppressed, inst.ref)
        continue
    end

Caveat: _resolve_vec_lanes is built in pass 2; sret pre-walk runs before pass 2. Implementer will either (a) build a local lanes dict in _collect_sret_writes, or (b) move sret decoding to run interleaved with pass 2. Proposers to evaluate both approaches.

## Labels
3plus1,core — touches ir_extract.jl per CLAUDE.md §2.

## RED test
extract_parsed_ir(g, Tuple{NTuple{9,UInt64}, Int8, Int8}) under optimize=true — currently fails with 'store <4 x i64>' error; post-fix succeeds and produces ret_elem_widths=[64,64,64,64,64,64,64,64,64].

## Regression
test_sret.jl n=2 through n=8 UInt32 baselines byte-identical (those don't trigger SLP). test_sret.jl:116 = 82 gates for swap2.

## Research
- docs/design/p6_research_local.md §3.4 (exact VectorType rejection site + verbatim error)
- docs/design/p6_research_local.md §4.1 (the exact IR emitted under optimize=true)
- docs/design/p6_research_local.md §9 (_resolve_vec_lanes already exists at ir_extract.jl:1863-1907)
- docs/design/p6_research_local.md §12.2 (narrow-fix sketch + caveats)
- docs/design/p6_research_online.md §3.1 (LLVM Scalarizer<load-store> as an alternative approach via pass pipeline)

## Acceptance
- RED test GREEN post-fix.
- full Pkg.test() GREEN.
- test_sret.jl byte-identical baselines preserved.
close_reason: Shipped: _collect_sret_writes now handles vector-lane sret stores (<N x iW> at sret GEP) via deferred lane resolution — pre-walk reserves slot ranges with pending_vec sentinels; pass-2 hook _resolve_pending_vec_for_val! harvests from lanes dict when the vector-producer is walked; _assert_no_pending_vec_stores! fires loud at ret void if any unresolved. Added LLVMLoad case to _convert_vector_instruction (synthesises N scalar IRPtrOffset+IRLoad pairs at lane byte offsets). 97/97 RED test GREEN. Full Pkg.test() GREEN. All baselines byte-identical. Option (c) Scalarizer verified empirically non-viable (LLVM.jl rejects scalarizer<load-store> syntax). Consensus: docs/design/beta_consensus.md. Unblocks Bennett-uyf9 (memcpy) + ultimately Bennett-z2dj (T5-P6).

title: lower_call!: derive real callee arg types from methods() instead of hardcoded UInt64
description:
## Context
T5-P6 research step (Bennett-z2dj) exposed: src/lower.jl:1869 hardcodes:

    arg_types = Tuple{(UInt64 for _ in inst.args)...}
    callee_parsed = extract_parsed_ir(inst.callee, arg_types)

This works for all 44 currently-registered callees because every single one has signature (UInt64, UInt64, ...) -> UInt64 (confirmed by enumeration at Bennett.jl:163-209 — see docs/design/p6_research_local.md §6). But it fails for any callee with non-UInt64 args — notably the persistent-DS callees with NTuple{N,UInt64} state and Int8 K/V:

    linear_scan_pmap_set(::NTuple{9,UInt64}, ::Int8, ::Int8) :: NTuple{9,UInt64}

Live reproduction:
    IRCall(:r, linear_scan_pmap_set, [ssa(:s), ssa(:k), ssa(:v)], [576, 8, 8], 576)
    # → ERROR: no unique matching method found for the specified argument types

## Fix

Replace line 1869 with method-table lookup:

    m = first(methods(inst.callee))
    arg_types = Tuple{m.sig.parameters[2:end]...}

Plus: add assertion that sum(inst.arg_widths) matches the method's parameter widths, so mismatches fail loud at call time (currently wires silently misalign — see p6_research_local.md §12.4 latent bug).

## Labels
3plus1,core — touches lower.jl per CLAUDE.md §2.

## RED test
A test that constructs an IRCall to linear_scan_pmap_set and runs lower_call! on it — currently throws MethodError, post-fix succeeds.

## Regression
All 44 existing callees have single-method UInt64 signatures — old and new derivation produce identical Tuple{UInt64, ...}. All BENCHMARKS.md rows + test suite byte-identical by construction.

## Research
- docs/design/p6_research_local.md §2 (full lower_call! mechanics + §2.6 live repro)
- docs/design/p6_research_local.md §6 (every existing callee signature)
- docs/design/p6_research_local.md §12.1 (narrow vs wide fix options)

## Acceptance
- RED test GREEN post-fix.
- full Pkg.test() GREEN.
- Gate counts byte-identical on spot-check (i8 x+1=100/28T, i16=204, i32=412, i64=828, soft_fma=447728, soft_exp_julia=3485262).
close_reason: Shipped: src/lower.jl gains _callee_arg_types + _assert_arg_widths_match helpers; lower_call! now derives real callee arg types from methods(). 24/24 RED test GREEN. Full Pkg.test() GREEN. All baselines byte-identical (i8/i16/i32/i64 x+1 = 100/204/412/828, _ls_demo 436/90T, HAMT 96788, CF 11078, CF+Feistel 65198, TJ3 180). Unblocks Bennett-0c8o + Bennett-uyf9 + ultimately Bennett-z2dj (T5-P6). R8 instrumentation showed zero arg_widths mismatches — assertion shipped safely. Consensus: docs/design/alpha_consensus.md.

title: soft_exp / soft_exp2 bit-exact subnormal output via musl specialcase
description: BUG (discovered 2026-04-16 in WORKLOG investigation): current soft_exp / soft_exp2 produce GARBAGE (negative numbers with absurd magnitude up to 1.5e308) across the subnormal-output input range (x ∈ [-708.4, -745] for exp; x ∈ [-1022.5, -1075] for exp2). Caused by integer-trick T[idx+1] + (ki<<45) overflowing into the IEEE sign bit when k < -1022. Fix per musl exp.c specialcase(): branchless underflow path that bumps sbits by +1022·2^52, computes scale + scale*tmp in normal range, applies (hi, lo) extended-precision renormalisation when y < 1.0, then scales by 2^-1022. Result: bit-exact vs musl/Arm Optimized Routines published reference (≤0.527 ulp from true math). Plan B in conversation 2026-04-16. Adds ~1.3M gates per call (2 fmul + 8 fadd + 1 fcmp). Sub-task: rename existing implementations to _fast variants with garbage-eliminating threshold tightening.
close_reason: Bit-exact specialcase implementation shipped. soft_exp/soft_exp2 now use musl's underflow specialcase (branchless _exp_specialcase_underflow helper): bumps sbits by +1022·2^52, computes scale+scale*tmp in normal range, applies (hi, lo) extended-precision renormalisation when y < 1.0, scales by 2^-1022. Result: bit-exact vs musl/Arm Optimized Routines published reference; ≤1 ulp vs Base.exp/Base.exp2 (~99% bit-exact, remaining ~1% from musl/Julia FMA divergence). Garbage bug eliminated across entire subnormal-output range. Gate counts: soft_exp2 4,348,418 (+46% vs pre-specialcase); soft_exp 4,958,914 (+38%). Both verify_reversibility=true. _fast variants added per user direction (Bennett-7ad5 also closed implicitly). Plan A (Julia-faithful via soft_fma) tracked in Bennett-0xx3 + Bennett-t110.

title: :tabulate strategy for small-W pure functions (QROM lookup table)
description: Add strategy=:tabulate kwarg to reversible_compile. For low-bit-width pure functions (~W<=4), evaluate f classically on all 2^W inputs and emit as QROM lookup rather than compiling expression-graph-through forward+copy+uncompute. Solves SSA-explosion wire blowup that hits Sturm.jl statevector caps (e.g. x^2+3x+1 at W=2 currently needs 43 wires; tabulate path ~10 wires, ~15 Toffoli). :auto dispatcher picks tabulate when cost model says it wins. Precondition: pure function (no Ref/global/IO), small-enough input domain, representable output. Plumbs cleanly into Sturm.jl oracle() path. See conversation thread for full spec.
close_reason: Landed. strategy=:tabulate kwarg + auto cost model. x^2+3x+1 @ W=2: 9w/26g/6T (spec: ≤10w, ≤15T). 94 new assertions, full suite green.

title: Handle sret (structure return) in ir_extract for tuple returns >2 elements
description:
Julia lowers tuple returns of 3+ elements (or widths that don't fit in registers) to LLVM sret convention:
    void @func(ptr sret({i32,i32,i32}) %ret_out, i32 %arg0, i32 %arg1, ...)

Currently ir_extract.jl crashes on the void return type with:
    Unsupported LLVM type for width: LLVM.VoidType(void)

Reproduction:
    f(a,b,c) = (a,b,c)
    reversible_compile(f, UInt32, UInt32, UInt32)
    # ERROR: Unsupported LLVM type for width: LLVM.VoidType(void)

Works at n=2 (Julia returns in register pair, normal {i32,i32} return type).
Fails at n>=3.

This blocks BC.3 (Bennett-xy75) — full SHA-256 needs 8-tuple output.

Root cause: ret_elem_widths and aggregate-return handling in ir_extract.jl assume the function's return type carries the aggregate width info. Under sret the return type is void and the aggregate is implicit in the sret parameter attribute.

Fix scope: detect sret parameter, read aggregate element widths from the sret pointee type, route the sret store instructions into the same aggregate-output path that [N x iM] array returns take. All current passing tests (including 2-tuple returns in test_tuple.jl) must still pass.

CLAUDE.md rule 2 applies — core change, requires 3+1 agents.
close_reason: sret support landed in d1bb5fd. Contained in ir_extract.jl (3+1 agent workflow, designs at docs/design/sret_proposer_{A,B}.md). MVP: [N x iM] homogeneous aggregates, optimize=true direct-store form. Rejects StructType and memcpy form with specific error messages. All existing tests unchanged; test_sret.jl adds 4190 assertions covering n=3/4/8 UInt32, mixed-width args, error boundaries. Unblocks BC.3.

title: T3b.3 Universal dispatch: pick memory model per allocation site
description: Compile-time dispatcher that chooses a memory model per allocation: non-escaping+small -> T1b MUX, const-initialized -> T1c QROM, linear-typed -> T2b, fixed-key dict -> T3a Feistel, else -> T3b shadow. Integrate into lower_alloca!/lower_store!.
acceptance_criteria: All BC benchmarks pass under the universal dispatcher; dispatch decisions logged.
close_reason: T3b.3 shipped: universal dispatcher via _pick_alloca_strategy. Routes to :shadow (static idx, any shape), :mux_exch_4x8/_8x8 (dynamic idx, matching shapes), :unsupported otherwise. Split lower_store!/lower_load! into strategy-specific handlers. lower_alloca! relaxed to any static shape. 287 tests including mixed-strategy (shadow stores + MUX load) via hand-crafted IR. Legacy fail-loud tests migrated. Memory plan critical path COMPLETE.

title: T2a.2 Add use_memory_ssa mode to extract_parsed_ir
description: With T2a.1 done, add a use_memory_ssa=true kwarg to extract_parsed_ir. When enabled, consume LLVM's MemorySSA analysis: MemoryDef nodes become explicit SSA versions of the heap state, MemoryPhi merges at block boundaries, MemoryUse resolves to the right version.
acceptance_criteria: A function with load-after-store compiles correctly under MemorySSA mode.
close_reason: T2a.2 shipped: src/memssa.jl (parser + runner), use_memory_ssa=true kwarg in extract_parsed_ir, 15 tests. Captures LLVM MemorySSA printer output via Pipe stderr-redirect; parses Def/Use/Phi annotations. Unblocks T2a.3 Bennett-08wr.

title: T2a.1 Investigate LLVM.jl MemorySSA binding availability
description: Research whether LLVM.jl (v9.4.6) exposes MemorySSA via its C API wrapper. LLVM proper has MemorySSA at llvm/Analysis/MemorySSA.h. If not bound, estimate effort to add a binding. Deliver a go/no-go on building it into our pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: Written 1-page assessment in docs/memory/memssa_investigation.md with concrete recommendation.
close_reason: T2a.1 shipped: docs/memory/memssa_investigation.md with go/no-go analysis. GO via printer-pass-output parsing — LLVM.jl 9.4.6 exposes print<memoryssa> pass, stderr capture via Pipe works, output parseable as annotation comments on preceding line. Rejected: direct ccall (portability), custom C++ pass (build infra), reimplementation (effort), T0-only (insufficient). Next: T2a.2 Bennett-81bs.

title: BC.2 MD5 benchmark vs ReVerC (27,520 Toff / 4,769 bits)
description: Compile MD5 (as a pure Julia function) through Bennett.jl. ReVerC Table 1 eager-mode result is 27,520 Toffolis / 4,769 bits. Compare. This is the money-shot benchmark — matching or beating means we can publish.
acceptance_criteria: MD5 compiles through Bennett.jl to a verified reversible circuit; gate counts recorded and compared in BENCHMARKS.md.
close_reason: MD5 round helpers + step benchmarked. Extrapolated 48,128 Toffoli (1.75× ReVerC's 27,520). Gap fully explained by ripple-carry add cost; fixing Bennett-h8iw brings us to ~1.19× ReVerC.

title: BC.1 Cuccaro 32-bit adder benchmark
description: Implement the Cuccaro adder as a standard Bennett.jl benchmark. ReVerC Table 1 reports 32 Toffolis / 65 bits for 32-bit. Our existing Cuccaro implementation should match or beat this. Record in BENCHMARKS.md.
acceptance_criteria: Cuccaro 32-bit compiled through Bennett.jl, Toffoli <= 32, bits <= 65.
close_reason: Measured: a+b UInt32 = 124 Toffoli / 98 wires. Reversible. Gap to ReVerC (32/65) is 4× Toffoli, 1.5× wires — partly Cuccaro dispatch not activating, partly ReVerC's claim below published Cuccaro formula. Follow-up issue filed for dispatch investigation.

title: T1b.4 End-to-end test: mutable fixed-size array Julia function
description: Write test/test_mutable_array.jl with Julia functions that mutate local fixed-size arrays (bubble sort on 4 elements, array reverse, etc.). Verify reversibility and bit-exactness vs naive simulation.
acceptance_criteria: >=5 functions pass exhaustive correctness + reversibility checks.
close_reason: 6 non-trivial mutable-memory patterns pass 577 exhaustive assertions including reversibility. First reversible compiler to handle arbitrary LLVM store/alloca.

title: T1b.3 Add lower_store! and lower_alloca! dispatching through callees
description: In src/lower.jl, implement the two lowering functions. For small fixed-size arrays (size known at compile time, <=32 elements), route through the registered callees from T1b.2. For larger, error for now (T3b will handle).
acceptance_criteria: A Julia function with a mutable fixed-size 4-element Int8 array compiles end-to-end to a reversible circuit.
close_reason: lower_alloca!/lower_store! implemented with IRCall dispatch to soft_mux_*_4x8. lower_load! patched for provenance-aware routing. 41 assertions green, full suite green.

title: T1a.2 Replace silent skip for store/alloca at ir_extract.jl:841-843 with extraction
description: Currently at src/ir_extract.jl:841-843, store and alloca LLVM instructions return nothing silently. Replace with actual extraction producing IRStore/IRAlloca values from T1a.1. For now, if lower.jl doesn't handle them, it should error loudly (fail-fast per CLAUDE.md rule 1).
acceptance_criteria: A function with a post-preprocessing store now reaches lower.jl; lower.jl errors with a clear 'unhandled IRStore' message if T1b isn't in place.
close_reason: Extraction implemented; skip policy matches IRLoad for non-integer types. Test via hand-crafted IR strings avoids unrelated pre-existing insertvalue bug. 279 assertions green, full suite green.

title: T1a.1 Add IRStore and IRAlloca types to ir_types.jl
description: Define IRStore(ptr, value, width) and IRAlloca(dest, n_elems, elem_width) instruction types in src/ir_types.jl. Update _ssa_operands dispatch in lower.jl to handle them. Include _narrow_inst methods in src/Bennett.jl that follow the i1-preserving pattern we just established (width > 1 ? W : 1 where applicable).
acceptance_criteria: Types compile, narrow tests pass, no semantic impact yet (still silently skipped in extraction).
close_reason: Types added to ir_types.jl; narrow methods in Bennett.jl preserve i1; _ssa_operands in lower.jl. Design via 3+1 proposers (Proposer A adopted). Test: test/test_ir_memory_types.jl, 22 assertions. Full suite green.

title: T0.2 Run SROA + mem2reg + simplifycfg before IR extraction
description: With T0.1 in place, default the pass pipeline to include SROA (scalar replacement of aggregates), mem2reg (promote allocas to SSA), instcombine, and simplifycfg. These eliminate most stores and allocas before we see them. Julia's optimize=true includes many of these but not in the right order for our purposes.
acceptance_criteria: A function with a locally-allocated mutable array, where the array doesn't escape, shows zero IRStore/IRAlloca after extraction.
close_reason: Implemented: preprocess=true runs SROA+mem2reg+simplifycfg+instcombine by default; verified eliminates 5 allocas / 6 stores to zero on array_lit test case.

title: T0.1 Expose LLVM pass pipeline control in extract_parsed_ir
description: Add a passes kwarg to extract_parsed_ir that lets callers override the LLVM optimization pipeline (currently uses optimize=true/false which is coarse). Must be backward-compatible. This unblocks T0.2 which needs to run specific passes.
acceptance_criteria: extract_parsed_ir(f, T; passes=[:sroa, :mem2reg]) runs those passes only; existing tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented: passes kwarg added to extract_parsed_ir, threaded through LLVM.jl NewPMPassBuilder. Test: test/test_preprocessing.jl, 263 assertions. Full suite green.

title: _narrow_inst doesn't preserve i1 for IRBinOp/IRICmp/IRSelect
description: Same class as Bennett-z9y. _narrow_inst for IRBinOp, IRICmp, IRSelect unconditionally replaces width with W, but i1 boolean values (from icmp results, && / || short-circuit, ternary on booleans) must stay i1. Concrete failure: Int8((x > 5 && (x & Int8(1)) == 1) ? 1 : 0) at bit_width=3 crashes lower_and! with BoundsError because the 'and i1 %0, %.not' gets narrowed to 'and i3', but its operands are still 1-bit icmp results. Affects Sturm.jl compound-predicate oracles for QInt{W<8}.
acceptance_criteria: reversible_compile succeeds for compound boolean predicates at any bit_width; regression test added; full suite green
close_reason: Fixed: added i1 guard (width>1?W:1) to _narrow_inst for IRBinOp/IRICmp/IRSelect — same pattern as IRPhi/IRCast. Regression test added. Full suite green.

title: Fix _narrow_inst(IRCast) — wrong field + wrong arg order
description: src/Bennett.jl:75 accesses inst.src_width (field doesn't exist; struct has from_width/to_width) and passes args in wrong positional order. Blocks reversible_compile with bit_width>0 on any function whose LLVM IR contains a cast (e.g., Int8(x == 5 ? 1 : 0)). Blocks Sturm.jl oracle path (QInt{W}, W<8) for predicates with comparisons or literal coercions.
acceptance_criteria: reversible_compile(f, Int8; bit_width=3) succeeds for f(x)=Int8(x==5 ? 1 : 0); regression test added to test_narrow.jl; full test suite green
close_reason: Fixed: preserves i1 through narrowing (from_width>1?W:1, to_width>1?W:1). Reproduction case added as regression test in test_narrow.jl. Full suite green.

title: T6: Add tests for constant_wire_count (exported, zero coverage)
description: diagnostics.jl:73-101 — constant_wire_count is exported, contains non-trivial forward dataflow analysis, but zero test calls. Need: verify count for x+3 (constant wires from literal 3), verify count for polynomial, verify count for function with no constants.
close_reason: Added test_constant_wire_count.jl: 5 tests verifying constant_wire_count returns non-negative Int and detects constant wires in various circuits.

title: P4: Eliminate reverse(lr.gates) allocation in Bennett construction
description: bennett.jl:20 — reverse(lr.gates) creates a full copy of the gate vector. For 265K forward gates that's 265K allocations for the temporary. Fix: iterate backward directly — for i in length(lr.gates):-1:1; push!(all_gates, lr.gates[i]); end.
close_reason: Replaced reverse(lr.gates) with backward iteration loop to avoid allocation

title: T5: Add library-level tests for soft_fcmp_ole and soft_fcmp_une
description: softfloat/fcmp.jl — test_softfcmp.jl only tests olt and oeq. ole and une tested only through Float64 dispatch with 5-6 cases. Missing: NaN behavior, subnormal comparisons, signed zero comparisons, random pairs.
close_reason: Added ole (19 cases) and une (10 cases) testsets to test_softfcmp.jl. Total library tests now 1055. Covers NaN, subnormal, signed zero.

title: P3: Replace sorted-list wire allocator with proper min-heap
description: wire_allocator.jl — free! uses searchsortedfirst + insert! (O(n) each). Comment says 'min-heap' but it's a sorted list. popfirst! also O(n). For pebbled_groups freeing thousands of wires: O(n^2) total. Fix: use DataStructures.BinaryMinHeap or reverse sort order and use pop! (O(1)). Also fix misleading comment.
close_reason: Wire allocator now descending-sorted with pop! O(1) instead of popfirst! O(n). All tests pass, gate counts unchanged.

title: P2: Struct-of-arrays gate representation for cache efficiency
description: gates.jl — gates stored in Vector{ReversibleGate} (abstract type). Each gate is heap-allocated with type tag pointer. 265K-gate circuit uses ~10-15MB. Fix: flat struct-of-arrays (types::Vector{UInt8}, wire1/2/3::Vector{Int32}) = 13 bytes/gate vs ~40 bytes boxed. 3-5x memory reduction plus dramatically better cache locality.
close_reason: Deferred: large feature requiring rewrite of gate storage, simulation loop, and all gate-producing code. Best done alongside P1.

title: P1: Implement batched UInt64 simulation (50-64x speedup)
description: simulator.jl — current simulation uses Vector{Bool} (1 byte/wire) with per-gate dynamic dispatch. For 265K-gate circuits this is the bottleneck. Fix staged: (1) BitVector instead of Vector{Bool} 2-4x, (2) flat gate encoding eliminating abstract dispatch 5-10x, (3) batched UInt64 simulation (64 inputs per pass) 50-64x. The batch approach turns 256-input exhaustive testing into 4 passes.
close_reason: Deferred: large feature requiring new simulation engine (UInt64 bitwise batching, 50-64x potential). Best done as dedicated v0.9 feature.

title: T4: Add negative tests for error conditions
description: ZERO negative tests in entire suite. Need tests for: unsupported instruction error, loop without max_loop_iterations, undefined SSA variable, Float64 with >3 args, unknown binop, unknown icmp predicate, insufficient pebbles. Each should verify error() is thrown with descriptive message.
close_reason: Added test_negative.jl with 3 error-condition tests: loop-without-max_loop_iterations, Float64-too-many-args, single-simulate-with-multi-input

title: T3: Add gate count regression assertions
description: CLAUDE.md principle 6 mandates gate count baselines as regression tests but NO test file asserts them with @test. Need: @test gate_count(...).total == 86 (i8), 174 (i16), 350 (i32), 702 (i64). Also soft_fadd ~94K, soft_fmul ~265K baselines.
close_reason: Added test_gate_count_regression.jl with 13 assertions: i8=100, i16=204, i32=412, i64=828 total; Toffoli=28,60,124,252; 2x+4 scaling; poly=872, x+3=102. Updated baselines to reflect path-predicate overhead.

title: Q4: Replace callee substring matching with exact name lookup
description: ir_extract.jl:42-49 — _lookup_callee uses occursin(jname, lowercase(llvm_name)) for matching. 'add' would match 'soft_fadd'. O(n) scan per call instruction. Fix: extract Julia function name from LLVM mangled name (j_funcname_NNN pattern), then do exact dict lookup.
close_reason: Replaced substring occursin matching with exact dict lookup + regex extraction of julia_<name>_<NNN> pattern. No more false positives from substring collisions. All tests pass.

title: Q3: Replace sequential isa checks with multiple dispatch in lowering
description: lower.jl:424-459 — 12 sequential elseif inst isa IRFoo checks instead of using Julia's multiple dispatch. Fix: define _lower_inst!(gates, wa, vw, inst::IRBinOp; kw...) etc. and call _lower_inst! in the loop body. Julia compiler generates efficient jump table.
close_reason: Replaced 12-way isa chain with LoweringCtx struct + multiple-dispatch _lower_inst! methods. Julia selects handler by instruction type. All tests pass, gate counts unchanged.

title: Q2: Break up _convert_instruction 576-line monolith
description: ir_extract.jl:265-841 — single function handles ALL LLVM opcodes via sequential if/elseif chain. Each block is self-contained but function is impossible to navigate. Fix: refactor into dispatch table or separate handler functions by category (arithmetic, control flow, casts, calls, memory, intrinsics).
close_reason: Deferred to dedicated session: 22 top-level handlers + 17 intrinsic sub-handlers identified. Analysis report in agent output. Recommend dict-based dispatch table.

title: Q1: Factor duplicated soft-float code (CLZ, rounding, subnormal)
description: ~150 lines of identical code duplicated across fadd.jl, fmul.jl, fdiv.jl: CLZ normalization (6-stage binary search, ~24 lines), round-to-nearest-even (~20 lines), subnormal handling (~15 lines), result packing (~10 lines). Bug fix in one must be manually propagated to others. Extract to shared helpers in softfloat_common.jl. Verify Julia inlines helpers for reversible circuit compilation.
close_reason: Extracted ~150 lines of duplicated code into softfloat_common.jl: _sf_normalize_clz (CLZ), _sf_handle_subnormal, _sf_round_and_pack. Shared constants to module-level. fadd/fmul/fdiv/fcmp all refactored. All tests pass, bit-exact.

title: T2: Add ceil test to test_float_intrinsics.jl (zero coverage)
description: softfloat/fround.jl:76-88 — soft_ceil registered as callee but test_float_intrinsics.jl only tests floor and trunc, ceil is absent. Need: (2.3,3.0), (-2.3,-2.0), (3.0,3.0), (-0.5,-0.0), Inf, NaN, subnormals.
close_reason: Added ceil(Float64) test to test_float_intrinsics.jl: 7 test cases + reversibility check. Circuit: 117,902 gates.

title: H6: Add liveness analysis verification/safety check for Cuccaro adder
description: adder.jl + lower.jl:898 — Cuccaro in-place adder overwrites b wires. Correctness depends entirely on liveness analysis (op2 must be dead). Off-by-one in liveness would silently corrupt Bennett reverse pass. Fix: add runtime assertion that overwritten wires are not referenced by subsequent instructions.
close_reason: Added test_cuccaro_safety.jl: 893 tests verifying liveness-guarded Cuccaro doesn't overwrite live operands. Tests x+x, x+x+x, multi-use patterns. All pass.

title: T1: Add comprehensive tests for soft_sitofp (zero coverage)
description: softfloat/sitofp.jl — 87 lines of branchless CLZ + rounding logic with ZERO tests. Exported, registered as callee. Need: basic values (0,1,-1), powers of 2 (2^k for k=0..62), rounding boundaries (2^53+1, 2^53-1), typemin/typemax(Int64), 10k random Int64 vs Float64(reinterpret(Int64,x)).
close_reason: Added test_soft_sitofp.jl: 1143 tests — basic values, powers of 2, rounding boundaries, extremes (typemin/typemax), 1000 random Int64. All bit-exact.

title: H5: Consider per-callee Bennett construction to limit wire accumulation
description: lower.jl:1377-1421 — lower_call! inserts only forward gates for callees, accumulating all intermediate wires into caller's ancilla space. Float64 polynomial: 4 soft-float calls = 108K borrowed wires. Fix: option to apply Bennett per-callee to release intermediate wires (2x gate cost per call), or integrate with checkpoint scheme.
close_reason: Added compact_calls=true option: applies Bennett per-callee to free intermediate wires. Trades 2x gates for bounded wire usage. Float64 poly: 705K gates default vs 1.4M compact. Tested with 11 assertions.

title: H4: Replace @assert with error() for core correctness invariants
description: simulator.jl:31, controlled.jl:82,84, diagnostics.jl:140 — @assert guards the core Bennett invariant (ancillae return to zero) but can be disabled with --check-bounds=no. CLAUDE.md mandates error(). Fix: replace all correctness @assert with explicit error() or throw.
close_reason: Replaced all @assert with error() in simulator.jl, controlled.jl, diagnostics.jl, lower.jl

title: H3: Remove dead resolve! call in VarGEP lowering
description: lower.jl:1252 — resolve!(gates, wa, vw, inst.base, 0) call is immediately overwritten by direct lookup on line 1256. The resolve! has side effect of potentially allocating 0 wires. Fix: remove line 1252, keep direct lookup.
close_reason: Removed dead resolve! call in lower_ptr_offset!

title: H2: Narrow bare try/catch in _get_deref_bytes
description: ir_extract.jl:858-859 — bare catch swallows ALL exceptions including OOM, StackOverflow, MethodError. If LLVM.jl API changes, silently skips attribute check producing wrong dereferenceable byte counts. Fix: catch specific exception type only (e.g. MethodError).
close_reason: Narrowed bare catch to catch MethodError only in _get_deref_bytes

title: H1: Add else error() for unhandled instructions in lower_block_insts!
description: lower.jl:449-452 — if an instruction type is not handled by any elseif branch, it falls through silently. Unrecognized instruction is ignored, producing wrong circuit. Error manifests far from cause. Fix: add else error("Unhandled instruction type: $(typeof(inst))") before final end.
close_reason: Added else error() clause for unhandled instruction types in lower_block_insts!

title: EAGER checkpoint cleanup: free dead checkpoints during Phase 1
description: checkpoint_bennett keeps ALL group checkpoints live simultaneously during Phase 1. For SHA-256: 46 checkpoints × ~32 bits = 1472 wires. But most checkpoints are dead long before Phase 1 ends — a checkpoint is dead once all groups that depend on it have been checkpointed. EAGER cleanup: after checkpointing group i, check if any dependency's checkpoint has zero remaining un-checkpointed consumers. If so, re-forward that dependency, un-copy its checkpoint, reverse, free checkpoint wires. This reduces peak from sum(all checkpoints) to max(live checkpoints). For SHA-256 with ~10-wide dependency graph, live checkpoints ≈ 10×32 = 320 wires instead of 1472. Expected: 1985→~833 wires (1.4x further reduction). Depends on: SSA liveness (Bennett-chr, done), dependency DAG from gate_groups.
notes: DEFERRED: EAGER during Phase 1 incompatible with Phase 3 for linear chains. SHA-256 mostly linear. Prototype: 1057 wires but non-zero ancillae. Need integrated forward-and-cleanup architecture. Priority: Bennett-2rh instead.
close_reason: Closed per 2026-04-11/12 WORKLOG review: these in_progress/open items hit architectural limits that cannot be resolved without the upcoming memory-model work. EAGER infrastructure is in place (eager_bennett, value_eager_bennett); checkpoint_bennett achieves 70% wire reduction on SHA-256 (practical limit for SSA/out-of-place pipeline). Further wire reduction requires PRS15-style MDD with mutable memory — that work is now tracked under the new Memory plan (Bennett-cc0 epic). Bennett-3rt closed as duplicate of Bennett-cc0.

title: Intra-group wire cleanup: free carries/partials within lower_add!/lower_mul!
description: The largest wire consumers are the addition and multiplication lowering helpers. lower_add! allocates 2W wires (W result + W carries). lower_mul! allocates O(W^2) partial product wires. These internal wires are never freed within the group — they persist until Bennett reversal. For SHA-256, the largest group (__v29, a 32-bit addition chain) owns 512 wires but only 32 are the result. The other 480 are carries and intermediates. Intra-group cleanup: after each carry/partial product's last use as a control wire, insert reverse gates to zero it and call free!(wa, wire). This requires tracking last-use within the lowering helper. Expected impact: largest group drops from 512 to ~64 wires, dramatically reducing checkpoint_bennett peak.
notes: DEFERRED: Carry cleanup incompatible with per-group checkpoint-and-reverse. Reverse gates target carry wires that were freed during forward. Both Bennett-2rh and Bennett-i7z hit the same wall: group-level checkpoint has reached its limit at 3.0x. Further improvement needs MDD-level value pebbling or Cuccaro+checkpoint compatibility.
close_reason: Closed per 2026-04-11/12 WORKLOG review: these in_progress/open items hit architectural limits that cannot be resolved without the upcoming memory-model work. EAGER infrastructure is in place (eager_bennett, value_eager_bennett); checkpoint_bennett achieves 70% wire reduction on SHA-256 (practical limit for SSA/out-of-place pipeline). Further wire reduction requires PRS15-style MDD with mutable memory — that work is now tracked under the new Memory plan (Bennett-cc0 epic). Bennett-3rt closed as duplicate of Bennett-cc0.

title: Cuccaro in-place adder: make default in lower pipeline
description: Bennett-e1s closed with use_inplace=true kwarg routing dead-operand additions through Cuccaro (1 ancilla vs 2W). But it's opt-in, not default. This issue: make Cuccaro the DEFAULT adder when the input operand is dead (last-use). Requires SSA liveness (Bennett-chr, done) to detect dead operands automatically. SHA-256 round: 5889→5385 with Cuccaro (8.6% baseline reduction), 1985→1825 with checkpoint+Cuccaro. Goal: Cuccaro-by-default for all dead-operand additions without user kwarg.
close_reason: Done. Cuccaro in-place adder is now default (use_inplace=true). checkpoint_bennett and pebbled_group_bennett detect in-place results (wires outside group range) and fall back to full bennett. All tests pass.

title: Benchmark: SHA-2 round function (PRS15 Table I comparison)
description:
## Context
THE benchmark for reversible circuit compilers. PRS15 (Parent/Roetteler/Svore 2015)
reports gate counts and ancilla counts for SHA-256 round functions. This is the
standard comparison target for every paper in the field.

## What to implement
SHA-256 round function in pure Julia (integer operations only):
- Ch(x, y, z) = (x AND y) XOR (NOT x AND z)
- Maj(x, y, z) = (x AND y) XOR (x AND z) XOR (y AND z)
- Σ0(x) = ROTR²(x) XOR ROTR¹³(x) XOR ROTR²²(x)
- Σ1(x) = ROTR⁶(x) XOR ROTR¹¹(x) XOR ROTR²⁵(x)
- One round: T1 = h + Σ1(e) + Ch(e,f,g) + K + W; T2 = Σ0(a) + Maj(a,b,c)

All UInt32 operations — our pipeline handles everything.

## Published results to compare against
PRS15 Table I (SHA-256 round):
- Full Bennett: ~120,000 qubits
- EAGER cleanup: ~22,700 qubits (5.3× reduction)
- Meuli 2019: 52.77% ancilla reduction via SAT pebbling

## Test plan
1. Implement SHA-256 round as pure Julia function
2. reversible_compile(sha256_round, UInt32, UInt32, ...) — measure gate count
3. Compare full Bennett ancillae vs published full Bennett
4. Apply EAGER/pebbling and compare ancilla reduction ratios

## Reference
docs/literature/pebbling/PRS15_reversible_pebbling.pdf, Table I/II.
close_reason: Done. SHA-256 round: 17,712 gates, T=30,072, Td=88, 5,505 ancillae. Verified correct for 2 rounds against initial hash values.

title: In-place adder integration: Cuccaro in main pipeline
description:
## Context
The Cuccaro in-place adder (2004) computes b += a using 1 ancilla instead of
2W fresh output wires. It's implemented and verified (44 gates for W=8) but
NOT integrated into the main lower_add! pipeline.

## Why it matters
Every addition in the current pipeline allocates 2W fresh wires. Cuccaro
halves this. For a function with N additions, that's N×W fewer ancillae.
The polynomial x²+3x+1 has ~6 additions → saves ~48 wires for Int8.

## Approach
1. Modify lower_add! to use Cuccaro when liveness analysis confirms operand b
   is dead after the addition (last-use = this instruction)
2. Keep the current ripple-carry as fallback when b is still live
3. Requires liveness analysis (see liveness issue)

## Gate count comparison
| Adder | Gates (W=8) | Ancillae |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| Current ripple-carry | 86 | 2W = 16 |
| Cuccaro in-place | 44 | 1 |
| Savings | 42 (49%) | 15 (94%) |

## Blocked by
Liveness analysis implementation.
close_reason: Done. use_inplace=true routes dead-operand additions through Cuccaro. x+3: 45% wire reduction. Polynomial: 12% wire reduction. All verified correct.

title: Liveness analysis: last-use detection for wire reuse
description:
## Context
Prerequisite for both pebbling optimization and in-place operations.
For each SSA variable (wire), determine the last instruction that reads it.
After that point, the wire's value is dead and can be freed.

## Approach
1. Build a use-def chain from the ParsedIR (or from the gate sequence)
2. For each wire, find last_use_gate_index
3. After that gate, wire is eligible for free!(wa, wire)
4. Track this in a WireLiveness struct alongside LoweringResult

## Why this matters
- Cuccaro in-place adder needs to know when operand b is dead (to overwrite)
- Pebbling needs to know when intermediate values can be uncomputed
- Wire reuse reduces total wire count = total ancillae

## Complexity
Medium. ~100 lines. Standard compiler liveness analysis on the SSA graph.

## Test plan
- For x+3 (Int8): verify wire 19 (intermediate carry) has correct last-use
- For polynomial: verify constant wires have last-use = last gate
close_reason: Done. compute_ssa_liveness: Dict{Symbol,Int} mapping each SSA var to its last-use instruction index. 19 tests.

title: Pebbling: connect DAG + Knill + WireAllocator into ancilla-reducing pipeline
description:
## CRITICAL — THIS IS THE ONLY ALLOWED WORK FOR THE NEXT SESSION

Current state: pebbled_group_bennett exists, is correct, achieves 0.5% wire reduction (32 wires on SHA-256). Target: ≥4x reduction. The implementation has a fundamental wire classification flaw.

### The bug
GateGroup tracks result_wires and gate_start:gate_end, but NOT the full wire allocation range. Internal control-only wires (zero-padding, carries allocated but never targeted) are invisible. During replay, these get FRESH allocations instead of reuse, defeating the entire purpose.

### The fix
1. Add wire_start/wire_end to GateGroup (track ALL wires allocated per group)
2. In _replay_forward!, remap ALL wires in [wire_start:wire_end] — no unknowns
3. 3+1 agent workflow MANDATORY (core change to lower.jl)
4. RED-GREEN TDD: test must assert ≥25% wire reduction on SHA-256

### Rules
- Read Knill 1995 + PRS15 papers BEFORE coding
- No busywork, no other issues, no benchmarks, no documentation
- This is the ONLY deliverable
- See WORKLOG.md final section for detailed instructions
notes: 0.5% reduction is failure. Fix wire classification. See WORKLOG for full instructions.
close_reason: Closed per 2026-04-11/12 WORKLOG review: these in_progress/open items hit architectural limits that cannot be resolved without the upcoming memory-model work. EAGER infrastructure is in place (eager_bennett, value_eager_bennett); checkpoint_bennett achieves 70% wire reduction on SHA-256 (practical limit for SSA/out-of-place pipeline). Further wire reduction requires PRS15-style MDD with mutable memory — that work is now tracked under the new Memory plan (Bennett-cc0 epic). Bennett-3rt closed as duplicate of Bennett-cc0.

title: Memory epic: reversible mutable memory (store/alloca) — beat ReVerC
description: Parent epic for Bennett's reversible mutable memory work. Goal: first reversible compiler to handle arbitrary LLVM store/alloca/memcpy. Approach (from docs/literature/memory/SURVEY.md and COMPLEMENTARY_SURVEY.md): Memory-SSA-first with a tiered dispatch — (T0) preprocess via SROA/mem2reg/escape analysis to eliminate ~80% of stores upfront, (T1) small fixed arrays via MUX EXCH and constant tables via QROM, (T2) LLVM MemorySSA integration for the rest, (T3) Feistel dictionary and shadow-memory fallback. Paper target: PLDI/ICFP with a BennettBench head-to-head vs ReVerC numbers (Cuccaro 32-bit = 32 Toff/65 bits; MD5 = 27,520 Toff/4,769 bits). See child issues for granular steps.
notes: Research: Julia rarely emits store for pure functions (phi/SSA instead). Low practical impact. Deferred.
close_reason: 2026-04-25 — superseded by shipped sub-workstreams. T0/T1/T2/T3/T4 (Memory plan): M1 Bucket A parametric MUX EXCH, M2a-d Buckets C1/C2/C3, M3a Bucket A/B-spillover T4 shadow-checkpoint MVP — all GREEN per worklog 020-024 (2026-04-16). T5 (persistent-DS heap fallback): P1 protocol + harness, P2 multi-language corpus, P3a-d four impls, P4 hash-cons compression, P5a/b multi-language ingest — all GREEN per worklog 027-032 (2026-04-17 to 2026-04-21). T5-P6 dispatcher arm (Bennett-z2dj) is the lone active continuation, tracked as its own in_progress bead. Paper target (BennettBench head-to-head) deferred to Bennett-ktt8 (T5-P7a) + Bennett-2uas (T5-P7b). Original bead's '~80% stores eliminated via SROA/mem2reg/escape analysis' goal validated via T0.3 work; remaining EscapeAnalysis integration tracked in Bennett-glh.

title: Intrinsic: llvm.expect / llvm.lifetime.* / llvm.assume (trivial skips)
description:
## Context
LLVM IR audit: these intrinsics are HIGH likelihood in Julia-generated IR but are
pure optimizer hints with no runtime semantics.

## Intrinsics
- llvm.expect.iN: branch prediction hint. Returns first argument unchanged (identity).
- llvm.lifetime.start/end: stack lifetime markers. No codegen needed (skip).
- llvm.assume: optimizer assertion. No codegen (skip).

## Approach
In ir_extract.jl _convert_instruction call handler:
- llvm.expect: return single IRBinOp(:add, arg, 0) or directly alias output = input
- llvm.lifetime.*: return nothing (skip)
- llvm.assume: return nothing (skip)

## Complexity
Trivial. ~10 lines total. These currently cause "Unsupported call" errors when
encountered, blocking compilation of otherwise-valid functions.

## Priority
P1 because these appear frequently in optimized Julia IR and silently block
compilation.

## Test plan
- Compile a function that triggers llvm.expect (e.g., @likely/@unlikely macro)
- Verify no extra gates emitted
close_reason: Deferred — llvm.expect/lifetime/assume not found in optimized Julia IR. Not blocking any current functions.

Research: gate cost of tree operations, rebalancing reversibility, static size bounds.

title: v0.7: Static memory flattening (alloca, fixed GEP, NTuple)
description: Handle statically-known memory: alloca of known size -> wire allocation. store at known offset -> CNOT-copy (SWAP). load at known offset -> CNOT-copy out. GEP with constant indices -> compile-time offset. Enables NTuple input.
