title: sr8v test is 270s — uses 2.4M-gate fixture where tiny suffices
description: HOTFIX. test/test_sr8v_compile_cache.jl uses test/fixtures/ll/eq9p_acosh_intrinsic.ll as the cache-test fixture in testsets 1-3 (5 compiles total of a 2.4M-gate circuit) — 270.2s wall-time in the full suite, making it the single biggest hot file. Cache identity semantics are circuit-size-independent: a tiny x+1 Int8 fixture (58 gates) proves c1 === c2 just as well. Surfaced by the 2026-05-23 post-{hybr,sr8v,uiaq} full-suite re-run: total went 27m59s → 35m06s (+7 min), almost entirely due to this single file. Fix: replace the eq9p fixture path with Bennett._extract_parsed_ir_cached(x -> x + Int8(1), Tuple{Int8}) in testsets 1-3, drop the >1000 gate_count threshold to the x+1 baseline (==58 or > 30). Expected post-fix: sr8v test drops to <5s; suite returns to ~28 min (matching baseline or slightly faster from hybr gains).
close_reason: Closed

title: Optimize 12 test_*_llvm_*_dispatch.jl files (65% of suite wall-time)
description: Bennett-2mj3 verification run (2026-05-23, JULIA_NUM_THREADS=32) revealed 12 test_*_llvm_*_dispatch.jl files consume ~1100s (~18 min) of the 28-min Pkg.test suite — 65% of total wall-time. Each is compile-bound (reversible_compile() builds 2.4M+-gate circuits for transcendental LLVM dispatch); threading individual simulate sweeps does NOT help. Targets for optimization: (1) Share compiled circuits across testsets within a file (each testset currently re-compiles). (2) Batch-compile all transcendental dispatches in one pass. (3) Evaluate whether the dispatch tests duplicate coverage with the corresponding soft_* primitive tests — if so, prune. Suite hot list (sorted): eq9p 125.9s, sfx9 123.3s, s1zl 121.5s, g82n 116.4s, m2bv 115.2s, 3mo 107.0s, emv 101.0s, ky5n 60.4s, bybh 59.2s, 0ulc 56.6s, 582 47.1s, o7cy 37.5s. See worklog/075 (2026-05-23 entry) for full timing map.

title: Multithread heaviest softfloat test sweeps (5 files)
description: Heavy softfloat test files (test_softfexp, test_softfpow, test_softflog, test_softfpow_julia, test_softfma) run 256-input and 10k random sweeps serially. simulate() is pure (only mutates a local Vector{Bool}, no globals), register_callee! is already thread-safe (Bennett-7stg / U26 ReentrantLock), and Julia 1.10's DefaultTestSet.record uses a lock — so Threads.@threads over sweep loops is safe. Aim: 5-10x cut on those files, ~2x on full Pkg.test wall-clock under JULIA_NUM_THREADS=32 (user has 64 threads). Test-only change, no 3+1.

title: Per-file test validation must use --check-bounds=yes to match Pkg.test() — workflow gap
description: Pkg.test() launches the test subprocess with --check-bounds=yes; a bare 'julia --project test/<file>.jl' uses default check-bounds (auto). The two produce materially different LLVM IR (forced bounds-checks block SROA / alloc-elision). The heap-memory epic (Bennett-gf3n M1-M4, commits bdce399..3ea36ca) was validated per-file with the default mode and looked green, but is red under Pkg.test() — root cause of Bennett-2mj3. Fix: (1) document in CLAUDE.md §8 that per-file green claims must run 'julia --project --check-bounds=yes test/<file>.jl' (or via the runtests.jl harness) to match Pkg.test(); (2) consider a thin scripts/ wrapper that runs a single file with the Pkg.test() flags. Filed from the Bennett-2mj3 diagnosis 2026-05-22.

title: Full Pkg.test red on main — 9 heap-memory test failures surfaced during s0tn work
description: Running the full Pkg.test() suite on clean main (commit 3ea36ca, before any s0tn change) yields 5 failed + 4 errored across the heap-memory tests: test_t5_corpus_julia.jl, test_gps7_heap_m1.jl, test_5ikt_heap_m3.jl, test_bd5f_heap_m4.jl. The heap-memory epic (gf3n, M1-M4, commits bdce399..3ea36ca) was committed with per-file green runs but the full suite is red — likely test-ordering / shared-state pollution (worklog/074 M4 entry claims per-file M1 519/519, M2 9734/9734, M3 529/529, M4 13/13 all green). Needs root-cause: is it genuine breakage or cross-file state pollution under the runtests.jl registration order? Surfaced 2026-05-22 by the Bennett-s0tn implementer, who ran Pkg.test() on stashed-clean main to establish a baseline. NOT caused by s0tn (s0tn adds zero new failures). CLAUDE.md §8 requires green Pkg.test per commit — main is currently violating that.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE (diagnosed 2026-05-22): NOT pollution. Pkg.test() runs the test subprocess with --check-bounds=yes, which forces every @boundscheck ON. Under that mode the heap functions' IR carries extra @ijl_bounds_error_int calls + many more @ijl_gc_small_alloc (the optimizer cannot elide redundant Memory reallocs across push!), so the heap recogniser correctly (fail-loud) rejects. The 4 heap test files assert happy-path compiles / specific reject substrings that only hold under DEFAULT check-bounds — the mode the heap epic validated with per-file. The recogniser is correct; the TESTS are wrong. Fix is test-only (no 3+1). 9 failures: test_t5_corpus_julia.jl TJ1, test_gps7_heap_m1.jl (f1 + cond_pair), test_5ikt_heap_m3.jl (f_tj1, f_tj1_2push, 3 reject tests), test_bd5f_heap_m4.jl (1 reject). Also a real latent bug: test_gps7_heap_m1.jl cond_pair calls _gps7_cond_pair((x,)) — typo, should be _gps7_cond_pair(x).

STATUS: WIP committed UNVERIFIED. A subagent implemented the fix (convert happy-path + reject tests to .ll-fixture-driven via scripts/gen_heap_fixtures.jl + test/fixtures/heap_*.ll; fix cond_pair) but was STOPPED mid-verification. Work recovered from transcript + committed: 4 test files, scripts/gen_heap_fixtures.jl, 6 .ll fixtures, worklog/075. NEXT SESSION: run full Pkg.test(), confirm 0 failed/0 errored, fix any residual, THEN close. Fixtures regenerate with 'julia --project scripts/gen_heap_fixtures.jl' (default check-bounds). See worklog/075.
close_reason: Closed

title: Reversible-VM backend — emit a reversible program for a reversible abstract machine (next-version north star)
description: NORTH STAR for the next version of Bennett.jl. See Bennett-ReversibleVM-PRD.md. Add a SECOND lowering target — a reversible abstract machine (reversible ISA/VM with a program counter) — selected via the existing target= dispatch kwarg on lower(). NOT a fork: the front-end (extract_parsed_ir, ParsedIR) is fully shared; the targets diverge only at the lower/bennett stage. On the reversible-VM target a terminating loop of statically-unknown length just RUNS dynamically — reversibility from logged transitions + the existing Bennett pebbling/checkpoint machinery for space. This is the Enzyme-parity architecture (emit a program, not a circuit); it removes the loop / unbounded-memory limitation intrinsic to the fixed-circuit target. The circuit target STAYS — a quantum oracle needs a fixed gate sequence. RESEARCH ANGLE: a reversible VM with dynamic classically-controlled execution maps naturally onto dynamic quantum circuits (mid-circuit measurement + classical feedforward) and quantum architectures with classical control. Requires a research + 3+1 design phase first (design brief -> consensus design -> milestone roadmap), mirroring the heap-memory epic. Only true remaining wall: non-termination (undecidable). Filed from the 2026-05-22 loop-architecture discussion (Solution-set thread 4 / Solution D).

title: Auto-infer loop bounds by classical evaluation — remove the max_loop_iterations guess
description: Today a data-dependent loop requires the user to pass max_loop_iterations=K, and too small a K silently miscompiles (Bennett-s0tn). But Bennett compiles a Julia function, so it can also RUN it: classically evaluate f to discover the worst-case iteration count and unroll to exactly that — no guess. Exhaustive for narrow input types (Int8/Int16: run all 2^W inputs, take the max trip count); sampling + the Bennett-s0tn runtime halt-check as the soundness backstop for wider types. General form = iterative deepening (try T=1,2,4,8,... until the halt flag fires). strategy=:tabulate is the degenerate limit. Removes the principal loop footgun; makes data-dependent loops (incl. a Dict probe/rehash on narrow inputs) compile with no user knob. Relates-to Bennett-s0tn, Bennett-jefu. Filed from the 2026-05-22 loop-architecture discussion (Solution-set thread 2).

title: Document the fixed-circuit architectural constraint (no loops / no runtime-sized memory) in README + docs
description: Bennett compiles to a circuit: a fixed, finite, loop-free, branch-free gate sequence decided entirely at compile time. Consequence: every loop must be unrolled to a compile-time-constant count, every memory must be a fixed compile-time size. This is THE defining limitation (contrast Enzyme, whose output is a program — loops + runtime-sized memory allowed — so it never needs bounds). Currently scattered/implicit. Add a clear 'Architectural limits' section to README.md and a fuller treatment in docs/. Surfaced in a 2026-05-22 design discussion.

title: gf3n M4 — fail-loud scope hardening + corpus/test reconciliation
description: gf3n Milestone M4 (of 4). Depends on M3. Consensus design §4 M4. Precise fail-loud rejections for: runtime-loop push! (f5), Dict (TJ2 — see Bennett-800b), runtime-N Array, nested/non-bits element types, mid-array insert!/deleteat!, C/Rust malloc heap. title: gf3n M2 — Array{T}(undef,const_N): synthetic const-N IRAlloca
description: gf3n Milestone M2 (of 4). Depends on M1. Consensus design §4 M2. Recognise the f4 shape: Array{T}(undef,const_N) with no growend! — the two @ijl_gc_small_alloc calls (Memory backing + Array wrapper). Synthesise a const-N IRAlloca (capacity inferred from use sites / the undef,N constant — NOT from the i32 size operand, which is the 32-byte header, a known cc0.5-class trap). Route constant-offset stores + runtime-indexed loads through the EXISTING :shadow/:mux/:checkpoint strategies (no lowering change). Model only the one data-pointer indirection needed to resolve element GEPs. Test: f4-shape with DISTINCT store/load indices, oracle-verified. Makes redesigned TJ4 (Bennett-890r) reachable. 3+1 REQUIRED. Difficulty high.

Array{T}(undef,const_N) compiles under mem=:heap. test_kuza_heap_m2.jl 9734/9734; M1 519/519; 3+1. See worklog/073. Follow-up Bennett-5006 (runtime-index stores).

title: gf3n M1 — GC-preamble recognition + dead-skeleton infrastructure
description: gf3n Milestone M1 (of 4). Consensus design: docs/design/heap_memory_consensus_design_2026-05-21.md §3-4; brief: heap_memory_design_brief_2026-05-21.md. Implement: (1) _detect_gc_preamble! — extraction pre-pass recognising the stereotyped x86-64 TLS idiom (call ptr asm 'movq %fs:0,$0' + -8 GEP + load + GC-frame chaining) and dropping it; (2) _GC_TLS_ASM_ALLOWLIST — 1-entry const set in src/extract/instructions.jl, the ONLY inline-asm exempt from the U15 fail-loud guard; (3) the conservative, bail-loud dead-skeleton taint/liveness proof — compute taint set of everything derived from %thread_ptr, assert it reaches user data ONLY via the @ijl_gc_small_alloc ptls arg, reject loud on any ambiguity; (4) mem=:heap opt-in mode (default mem=:auto unchanged). New file src/extract/heap.jl. Smallest green result: f1 (v=Int8[];push!(v,x);v[1], optimizer-reduces to ret %x) compiles green under mem=:heap. 3+1 REQUIRED — core extraction change; taint-proof soundness is the highest-risk part (a misjudged-dead skeleton feeding the return is a silent miscompile). Red-green TDD. Difficulty med-high.
close_reason: gf3n M1 landed — src/extract/heap.jl: non-mutating GC-preamble recogniser + 6-part bail-loud dead-skeleton liveness proof + mem=:heap opt-in. f1 compiles green; test_gps7_heap_m1.jl 519/519; peer regressions green. 3+1 protocol. See worklog/073.

title: Julia heap-memory support (Vector/Array) — extraction-only skeleton-stripping
description:
Implementation epic from the 2026-05-21 heap-memory design effort (3+1: 2 architects + orchestrator + validation spike). Design doc: docs/design/heap_memory_consensus_design_2026-05-21.md; problem brief: docs/design/heap_memory_design_brief_2026-05-21.md.

GOAL: compile Julia functions using statically-shaped heap Vector/Array into reversible circuits. Currently they fail at extraction (inline-asm TLS wall). TJ1 (Vector push x3 + reduce) goes genuinely green.

VALIDATED APPROACH (extraction-only, no heap modelling): the empirical spike confirmed the 'dead-skeleton hypothesis' across 5 IR shapes — for statically-shaped heap usage the optimizer has already compiled the collection into plain constant-offset store/load; the GC machinery (inline-asm TLS read, @ijl_gc_small_alloc, j_#_growend! calls, size-counter stores, memoryref arithmetic) is DEAD w.r.t. the return value. Design: recognise the skeleton, prove it dead (conservative, bail-loud), strip it, re-root surviving stores/loads onto a synthetic constant-capacity IRAlloca compiled by the EXISTING :shadow/:mux/:checkpoint lowering. No new IR node, no lowering changes, no register_callee! change — all logic in IR extraction (new src/extract/heap.jl).

ROADMAP — 4 milestones, each a delegated coding task, each red-green TDD:
 M1 — GC-preamble recognition + dead-skeleton infrastructure (_detect_gc_preamble!, _GC_TLS_ASM_ALLOWLIST, the taint/liveness proof, mem=:heap opt-in mode). Smallest result: f1 (v=Int8[];push!(v,x);v[1]) compiles green. 3+1 REQUIRED (core extract change; taint-proof correctness is subtle). Difficulty med-high.
 M2 — Array{T}(undef,const_N) / f4 shape, no growend!: recognise the two @ijl_gc_small_alloc, synthesise const-N IRAlloca, route const-offset stores + runtime-index loads to existing strategies. 3+1 REQUIRED. Difficulty high. Makes redesigned TJ4 (Bennett-890r) reachable.
 M3 — push!-built Vector with statically-inferable count: handle growend!/size-counter/capacity-diamond as dead skeleton, infer capacity from the const-offset store set. Makes TJ1 genuinely green. 3+1 REQUIRED (conceptual core). Difficulty high.
 M4 — fail-loud scope hardening: reject runtime-loop push! / Dict / runtime-N Array / nested element types with precise messages; flip TJ1 to green, keep TJ2 rejected, rewrite TJ4. Reconcile cond_pair: under --check-bounds=yes it emits an f4-identical skeleton, currently @test_throws via U15 — once M1/M2 land the recogniser engages and it should compile correctly; that assertion flips. Difficulty low-med, standard review.

SCOPE: Vector/Array of concrete integer T, statically-known shape. NOT supported (fail-loud): Dict (j_setindex! hashing is live, not dead skeleton — separate research program), unbounded/runtime-count push!, runtime-N Array, nested/non-bits elements, C/Rust malloc heap.

BIGGEST RISK: the dead-skeleton liveness proof must be sound — a misjudged-dead skeleton that feeds the return is a silent miscompile. Proof must be conservative + bail-loud; M1's 3+1 must stress shapes the spike did not audit. Julia-version fragility: the idiom is Julia-1.12-specific; recogniser must fail-loud on unrecognised preamble shapes.

When scheduling: split M1-M4 into child beads. The validation spike (the cc0.5-style de-risk gate both architects demanded) is DONE and passed — M1 may be scheduled.
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE — all 4 milestone children closed: M1 Bennett-gps7 (drop a dead heap collection), M2 Bennett-kuza (Array{T}(undef,const_N) re-rooting), M3 Bennett-5ikt (push!-built Vector, static count — TJ1 green), M4 Bennett-bd5f (fail-loud scope hardening). Julia heap arrays compile to reversible circuits under mem=:heap; out-of-envelope shapes reject loud with precise signposts. All extraction-only in src/extract/heap.jl — no IR node, no lowering change. Dict (Bennett-800b) was always out of gf3n scope (separate research program).

title: 9nwt-volatile: Julia GC-frame volatile memset blocks TJ1/TJ2/TJ4 corpus
description:
T5-P6 + Bennett-smjd are wired but the Julia-source dynamic-memory T5 corpus (TJ1 Vector, TJ2 Dict, TJ4 Array{Int8}(undef)) cannot reach the persistent dispatcher under reversible_compile. Failure point: Bennett-9nwt Phase 2 predicate 3 in src/extract/instructions.jl:771-776 rejects the volatile (i1 true) llvm.memset.p0.i64 that Julia emits to zero-init the GC frame (%gcframe1) at function entry.

Filed from Bennett-25dm triage 2026-05-18. Old TJ1/TJ2 root cause (LLVMGlobalAliasValueKind) and TJ4 root cause (thread_ptr GEP) still exist but are now downstream of this earlier wall.

Options to investigate:
  (a) Whitelist 'volatile memset of fresh alloca with c=0' in the 9nwt cascade — same allocation-fresh logic as the c=0 silent-drop fast path at predicate 8 should justify dropping it for volatile too, since the reversible model has no side-effect ordering anyway.
  (b) Bump the silent-drop fast path (predicate 8) to fire BEFORE the volatile check (predicate 3).
  (c) Special-case the gcframe alloca tag at extraction time.

Acceptance: TJ1/TJ2/TJ4 in test/test_t5_corpus_julia.jl progress past the volatile-memset error.  (TJ1/TJ2 then need LLVMGlobalAlias support — Bennett-cc0.x family — to fully green; TJ4 then needs Bennett-cc0.5 thread_ptr.  Out of scope here.)

Parent: Bennett-9nwt. Related: Bennett-25dm, Bennett-cc0.5.
close_reason: Volatile c=0 memset accepted via predicate relocation (3+1 protocol). _handle_memset_arm: malformed-IR guard kept early, volatile-value check moved to after the c==0 silent-drop. TJ1/TJ2/TJ4 now progress past the volatile-memset wall to the inline-asm TLS wall (Bennett-5oyt/U15). 24/24 new test (test_8su4_volatile_c0_memset.jl); regressions green (9nwt 87/87, ixiz, munq, q04a, 37mt, lqif, t5_corpus 258/258). De-risking finding: SROA-dissolution fear REFUTED — Vector/Dict/Array survive as live @ijl_gc_small_alloc heap allocs (SROA only hits NTuple value-types). See worklog/072.

title: soft_minimumnum/maximumnum + llvm.minimumnum/maximumnum dispatch (final kh6n stub)
description:
Closes the third and last Bennett-kh6n future-work stub. LLVM 19+ added llvm.minimumnum.f64 and llvm.maximumnum.f64 implementing IEEE 754-2019 minimumNumber/maximumNumber semantics. Per LLVM 19 langref:
  - minimumnum: NaN absorbed (return non-NaN); both-NaN -> NaN; -0.0 < +0.0 in tie (specified, not unspecified like minnum).
  - maximumnum: symmetric with +0.0 > -0.0 tie.

This is essentially soft_fmin/soft_fmax (Bennett-k2w6, NaN-absorbing) with the +-0 sign-tie-break specified rather than unspecified. The existing soft_fmin/soft_fmax IMPLEMENTATIONS already make the same +-0 tie-break choice (matches Base.min), so the behavior is bit-identical. Two design options:

  (a) Add thin aliases: soft_minimumnum = soft_fmin, soft_maximumnum = soft_fmax. Register both names so the LLVM dispatch can pick whichever matches the intrinsic name. Pros: clarity in the callee registry; symmetric with soft_fminimum vs soft_fmin distinction. Cons: +2 callees that are functionally identical.
  (b) Dispatch llvm.minimumnum.f64 directly to soft_fmin (no new primitive, just route the new LLVM intrinsic to the existing one). Pros: no duplicate code/register. Cons: callsite documentation has to explain why minimumnum routes to fmin.

Recommendation: (a) for clarity. Use trivial @inline aliases in src/softfloat/fmin.jl.

Implementation:
1. Add soft_minimumnum/soft_maximumnum (or aliases) to src/softfloat/fmin.jl.
2. Register in _CALLEES_FP_MINMAX (extended +2).
3. Update SoftFloatLib export list (+2).
4. Replace the kh6n explicit-reject for llvm.minimumnum./llvm.maximumnum. in src/extract/instructions.jl with native IRCall dispatch (f32 still rejected per CLAUDE.md §13).
5. Update test_kh6n_prefix_discipline.jl: invert the kh6n minimumnum/maximumnum reject testsets to assert clean dispatch (mirrors what mq6f did for roundeven).
6. New test/test_???_minimumnum_maximumnum.jl with .ll fixtures + bit-level + IR-level + regression coverage.

This finishes the kh6n future-work pipeline. After this: every LLVM round/min/max intrinsic dispatches natively; no explicit-reject stubs remain in instructions.jl from kh6n.
acceptance_criteria: src/softfloat/fmin.jl extended with soft_minimumnum/soft_maximumnum (alias or thin wrapper). _CALLEES_FP_MINMAX extended +2. Export list +2. LLVM dispatch: llvm.minimumnum./llvm.maximumnum. arms in src/extract/instructions.jl replace the kh6n explicit-reject with native IRCall. f32 forms still rejected per §13. New test file covers: bit-level vs reference, +-0 tie-break, NaN absorption (both/either), IR-ingest dispatch, f32 reject. test_kh6n_prefix_discipline.jl reject testsets for minimumnum/maximumnum inverted to assert clean dispatch. Regression sample green (test_k2w6_soft_fminmax must still pass, plus test_kh6n_prefix_discipline + test_intrinsics + test_float_intrinsics + test_ao66_vector_intrinsic_rescalarise). Worklog chunk 066 prepended.
close_reason: Native dispatch for IEEE 754-2019 minimumnum/maximumnum (LLVM 19+) via thin function-form aliases over soft_fmin/soft_fmax. Closes the third and FINAL kh6n future-work stub: every LLVM round/min/max intrinsic now dispatches natively, ZERO kh6n explicit-rejects remain in instructions.jl. _CALLEES_FP_MINMAX 4 to 6, soft-float primitives 37 to 39. 227,276 asserts green incl. alias-identity invariant, IEEE 754-2019 reference matrix vs 100k random pairs, 1074-binade subnormal sweep. f32 still rejected per CLAUDE.md §13. Worklog chunk 066 prepended; WORKLOG.md index updated.

title: Round-family cleanup: fix kh6n misstatement + llvm.round vs llvm.roundeven semantics
description:
Three-part cleanup arising from a misunderstanding in Bennett-kh6n:

(1) FIX FALSE STATEMENT: Bennett-kh6n's worklog chunk 066 + the explicit-reject error message in src/extract/instructions.jl claim soft_round 'implements round-half-AWAY-from-zero'. This is FALSE — soft_round IS banker's rounding (roundToIntegralTiesToEven), bit-exact vs Julia Base.round. Verified: soft_round(0.5)=0.0, soft_round(1.5)=2.0, soft_round(2.5)=2.0. Update the worklog entry and the kh6n explicit-reject error message.

(2) REAL SILENT MISCOMPILE: llvm.round.f64 (per LLVM langref: 'rounds halfway cases AWAY from zero') is currently silently dispatched via the no-op fallthrough to soft_round (which is banker's). For ties (0.5, 1.5, 2.5, ...) this produces the wrong answer. Two paths:
  (a) implement a new soft_round_away primitive (round-half-away-from-zero) and dispatch llvm.round.f64 to it
  (b) declare round-half-away as future work, fail loud on llvm.round.f64
  Recommendation: (a) — small primitive, mirrors soft_round structurally with one branch difference. The  rule changes from  to . About 30 LOC.

(3) ROUTE llvm.roundeven.f64 to soft_round: now that we know soft_round IS banker's, remove the kh6n explicit reject for llvm.roundeven.f64 and replace with native dispatch to soft_round. f32 still rejected per CLAUDE.md §13.

Optional bonus: rename soft_round → soft_roundeven for clarity. Add soft_round as a new round-half-away primitive (matches LLVM's llvm.round naming). This cleanup is high-churn (touches Base.round dispatch, callee registration, _CALLEES_FP_ROUND, every test that uses soft_round). Recommend: defer naming to a separate bead; in this bead, just add soft_round_away under the soft_*_away naming scheme.

Final dispatch matrix after this bead:
  - llvm.round.f64       → soft_round_away  (new, round-half-away)
  - llvm.roundeven.f64   → soft_round       (existing, banker's)
  - Base.round(::SoftFloat) → soft_round     (existing, banker's; matches Julia Base.round)
acceptance_criteria: src/softfloat/fround.jl gains soft_round_away (round-half-away-from-zero) following soft_round structure with one-branch difference. Bit-exact test sweep: soft_round_away(0.5)==1.0, soft_round_away(1.5)==2.0, soft_round_away(2.5)==3.0, soft_round_away(-0.5)==-1.0; matches Float64 LLVM @round on 100k random + tie matrix + ±0/±Inf/NaN/subnormal. _CALLEES_FP_ROUND extended +1. LLVM dispatch in src/extract/instructions.jl: llvm.roundeven.f64 routes to soft_round (kh6n explicit-reject removed); llvm.round.f64 routes to soft_round_away (no-op-fallthrough arm now does NOT match llvm.round.f64; explicit dispatch arm added). The kh6n error-message text + the worklog chunk 066 are corrected to say 'soft_round is banker's rounding (matches Base.round bit-exactly)'. Pkg.test green on regression sample (test_2hhx_soft_round, test_kh6n_prefix_discipline, test_intrinsics, test_float_intrinsics).
close_reason: Shipped soft_round_away (round-half-AWAY-from-zero, mirrors soft_round with tie arm folded into half-to-one) + native llvm.round./llvm.roundeven. dispatch arms in src/extract/instructions.jl (closes silent miscompile where llvm.round.f64 fell through no-op arm to banker's soft_round). Replaced kh6n explicit-reject for llvm.roundeven. with native dispatch to soft_round (both are banker's). Corrected kh6n misstatement (soft_round IS banker's per Bennett-2hhx, not round-half-AWAY) inline in worklog/066. Plus _CALLEES_FP_ROUND extension + module export + test/test_mq6f_round_away.jl with 7367 asserts (tie sweep, 1074-binade subnormal sweep, 5000 random samples vs RoundNearestTiesAway, IR ingest dispatch, f32 reject). Inverted one kh6n testset to assert positive dispatch instead of reject. Validation: mq6f 7367/7367, kh6n 6/6 (down from 7), 2hhx soft_round 5091/5091 (no regression), k2w6 434472/434472, broad regression sample 1348/1348. 3+1 protocol skipped per CLAUDE.md §2 mechanical-extension exception (documented in worklog).

title: soft_roundeven (banker's rounding) + llvm.roundeven dispatch + Base.round fix
description:
Closes the second of three kh6n future-work stubs. Implements soft_roundeven (IEEE 754 roundToIntegralTiesToEven / banker's rounding) as a fifth member of the rounding family alongside soft_round/soft_floor/soft_ceil/soft_trunc. The kh6n explicit reject in src/extract/instructions.jl currently fails loud on llvm.roundeven.f64 with the error 'Native soft_roundeven is filed as future work'.

Bonus drive-by: src/softfloat_dispatch.jl currently has Base.round(::SoftFloat) = SoftFloat(soft_round(x.bits)) which is INCORRECT — Julia Base.round(x::Float64) defaults to RoundNearest (banker's), but soft_round is round-half-AWAY-from-zero. So Bennett currently silently miscompiles Base.round(0.5) → 1.0 instead of 0.0. After this bead Base.round routes to soft_roundeven (matches Julia hardware semantics bit-exactly).

Implementation: add src/softfloat/froundeven.jl with soft_roundeven(a::UInt64) -> UInt64. Reference: existing soft_round in src/softfloat/fround.jl (line ~141), reusing the same exponent / mantissa / shift extraction; the only difference is the tie-break rule (round-to-even vs round-away). LLVM dispatch in src/extract/instructions.jl: replace the kh6n explicit-reject arm with IRCall to soft_roundeven (f32 still rejected per CLAUDE.md §13). Register in _CALLEES_FP_ROUND. Update Base.round(::SoftFloat) to soft_roundeven. Module export +1.
acceptance_criteria: src/softfloat/froundeven.jl implements soft_roundeven. Bit-exact vs Base.round on 100k random sweep + integer half-tie matrix (0.5, 1.5, 2.5, ..., -0.5, -1.5, ..., across all binades) + ±0/±Inf/NaN special cases + subnormal sweep. _CALLEES_FP_ROUND extended +1. LLVM dispatch in src/extract/instructions.jl replaces the kh6n explicit-reject arm with native dispatch (f32 still rejected). Base.round(::SoftFloat) override updated. Pkg.test green on regression sample (test_2hhx_soft_round, test_kh6n_prefix_discipline, test_intrinsics, test_float_intrinsics).
close_reason: Closed pre-work — bead premise was wrong. Verified soft_round IS already banker's rounding (round-half-to-even, matches Base.round bit-exactly): soft_round(0.5)=0.0, soft_round(1.5)=2.0, soft_round(2.5)=2.0. Re-filed as Bennett-{TBD} with the actual scope: (1) llvm.roundeven.f64 = banker's = soft_round (just route, no new primitive); (2) llvm.round.f64 is round-half-AWAY-from-zero per LLVM langref but is currently silently miscompiled to soft_round (banker's) via the no-op fallthrough — REAL bug; either implement soft_round_away or fail loud; (3) consider renaming soft_round to soft_roundeven to match its actual semantics. Also: kh6n worklog 066 contains the wrong claim that 'soft_round implements round-half-AWAY-from-zero' — needs correction.

title: soft_fmin/fmax/fminimum/fmaximum + llvm.minnum/maxnum/minimum/maximum dispatch
description:
Bennett-kh6n landed fail-loud rejects for scalar llvm.minnum.f64/llvm.minimum.f64/llvm.maxnum.f64/llvm.maximum.f64 because the existing scalar handler uses IRICmp(:slt) on the operand bit pattern, which mishandles +0/-0 and NaN per IEEE 754. This bead implements the four native soft-float primitives that close the kh6n future-work stub.

Four primitives, two pairs:
- soft_fmin / soft_fmax (≡ llvm.minnum / llvm.maxnum ≡ IEEE 754 minNum/maxNum): NaN-absorbing — if exactly one operand is NaN, return the other; if both NaN, return canonical qNaN.
- soft_fminimum / soft_fmaximum (≡ llvm.minimum / llvm.maximum ≡ IEEE 754-2008 minimum/maximum): NaN-propagating — if either operand is NaN, return NaN.

All four agree on -0.0 < +0.0 sign-aware tie-break (matches Julia Base.min/max bit-exactly).

Implementation: lean on soft_fcmp_olt (src/softfloat/fcmp.jl) for the ordered-compare core which already handles sign-aware ordering and ±0 equality. Add explicit ±0 sign-pick override and NaN dispatch on top. Register the four as callees, wire LLVM dispatch in src/extract/instructions.jl (replace the four float-rejects from kh6n), add Base.min/max(::SoftFloat, ::SoftFloat) overrides in src/softfloat_dispatch.jl. f32 rejected per CLAUDE.md §13. Test plan: 100k random pairs vs Base.min/max bit-exact, full special-case matrix (±0, ±Inf, NaN propagation contracts differ across the two pairs), subnormal-input sweep.
acceptance_criteria: src/softfloat/fmin.jl (or similar) implements all four primitives. Dispatch in src/extract/instructions.jl replaces the kh6n fail-loud rejects with IRCall to the four primitives. Per-bead test file covers: 100k random pair sweep bit-exact vs Base.min/max for fmin/fmax (NaN-absorbing semantics) and bit-exact vs minimum/maximum semantics for fminimum/fmaximum; full special-case matrix (±0/±Inf/NaN×4 per primitive); subnormal-input sweep across ±1074 binades. _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended +4 (or new _CALLEES list if min/max isn't transcendental). Existing kh6n reject tests updated or replaced.

title: Trailing-. prefix discipline for scalar llvm.* intrinsics in instructions.jl
description:
The vector handler in src/extract/vectors.jl (Bennett-7goc / chunks 063-064 trailing-. discipline) explicitly tightens llvm.minnum./llvm.minimum./llvm.maxnum./llvm.maximum. and rejects float-typed forms because the scalar handler uses integer compares on float bit patterns. The scalar handler in src/extract/instructions.jl has NOT been tightened: ~25 startswith(cname, "llvm.<name>") sites lack the trailing-. so future LLVM intrinsic siblings silently dispatch to the wrong handler. Concrete known siblings that are silently swallowed today: llvm.minimumnum / llvm.maximumnum (LLVM 19+, IEEE 754-2019 minimumNumber/maximumNumber semantics) currently swallowed by llvm.minimum/llvm.maximum prefix; llvm.roundeven currently swallowed by llvm.round prefix and dispatched to soft_round (round-half-away-from-zero) instead of banker's rounding. Plus the scalar min/max handler has the same float-bit-compare bug the vector handler rejects: integer slt/sgt on f64 bit patterns mishandles +0/-0 (treated as != 0) and NaN propagation (signed-int compare promotes NaNs to +Inf-equivalent).

Action: codemod every startswith(cname, "llvm.<name>") in src/extract/instructions.jl to include trailing dot, mirror the vectors.jl float rejection in the scalar min/max arm, and explicitly reject llvm.roundeven scalar with a clear error pointing at the missing soft_roundeven primitive (or implement it). Per CLAUDE.md trailing-. discipline rule from chunks 063-064.
acceptance_criteria: Every startswith(cname, "llvm.") in src/extract/instructions.jl ends with trailing dot. Scalar llvm.minnum/minimum/maxnum/maximum on float operands raises a clear _ir_error mirroring vectors.jl. llvm.roundeven explicitly rejected (or soft_roundeven implemented). Test fixtures cover llvm.minimumnum.f64, llvm.maximumnum.f64, llvm.roundeven.f64 reject paths. Pkg.test green.
close_reason: Tightened all 32 untightened llvm.* startswith() prefixes in src/extract/instructions.jl + drive-by in sret.jl. Added explicit fail-loud rejects for llvm.minimumnum, llvm.maximumnum, llvm.roundeven (previously silently swallowed by un-tightened llvm.minimum/maximum/round arms). Added float-operand reject on scalar llvm.minnum/minimum/maxnum/maximum arms mirroring src/extract/vectors.jl. 13/13 new + 6981 regression asserts green across 20 prefix-sensitive test files (ao66 vectors / g82n acosh / 37mt memcpy / 9nwt memset / 1pb sqrt-exp / h6f fma / emv pow / 2hhx round / s1zl-7goc trig family / 3mo sincos / o7cy expm1 / 0ulc log1p / lqif memmove-reject etc). Worklog chunk worklog/066_2026-05-15_kh6n_prefix_discipline.md filed. Native soft_fmin/fmax/minimumnum/maximumnum/roundeven flagged as future work in the error messages.

title: soft_expm1 + llvm.expm1 dispatch (Tier C2 — high-leverage primitive)
description: Tier C2.2 — second C2 transcendental, symmetric counterpart to log1p (Bennett-0ulc). expm1(x) = exp(x) - 1 accurate for small x. Three-regime branchless port: tiny |x| < 2^-54 → x bit-exactly; polynomial for |x| ≤ 0.5; exp(x) - 1 for |x| > 0.5 (handles ±Inf, NaN, large neg → -1, large pos → +Inf via natural propagation through soft_exp_fast). Symmetric high-leverage: would simplify the polynomial regimes in tanh/sinh/cosh (tier C1) just as log1p simplifies asinh/acosh/atanh. Register in _CALLEES_FP_TRANS (26 → 27).
design: Three-regime branchless port. (R1) tiny |x| < 2^-54: return x bit-exactly (expm1(x) ≈ x to ULP for tiny x; subnormal preserved per §13). (R2) polynomial |x| ≤ 0.5: K=15 Taylor expm1(x) = x + x²/2 + x³/6 + x⁴/24 + ... (residual at |x|=0.5: 0.5^16/16! ≈ 7.5e-19 << ULP). (R3) medium/large: exp(x) - 1 — no cancellation issue for |x| > 0.5 since exp(0.5) = 1.65 well above 1 and exp(-0.5) = 0.607 well below 1. Handles ±Inf, large negative (→ -1), large positive (→ +Inf), NaN naturally. Use soft_exp_fast (FTZ-on-output is correct semantics: x in [-745, -708] → exp ≈ subnormal, FTZ to 0 → 0-1 = -1, matches true expm1 for that range to ULP). Special cases via final cascade override. Tiny-threshold derivation: |expm1(x) - x| ≈ x²/2 ≤ ½ULP(x) requires x²/2 ≤ x·2^-53 → x ≤ 2^-52; pick 2^-54 for margin (same as log1p).
acceptance_criteria: (1) ≤2 ULP vs Base.expm1 on 100k random × 3 seeds × magnitude buckets; (2) expm1(±0) = ±0 bit-exact; (3) expm1(+Inf) = +Inf; (4) expm1(-Inf) = -1; (5) expm1(NaN) = NaN; (6) expm1(any subnormal) = subnormal bit-exact (§13); (7) llvm.expm1.f64 + libm @expm1 round-trip green; (8) f32 rejected; (9) regression-guard llvm.exp.f64 still routes to soft_exp; (10) _CALLEES_FP_TRANS 26→27.
notes: Symmetric to soft_log1p (Bennett-0ulc). Would benefit existing C1 hyperbolic primitives if they were rewritten: tanh/sinh/cosh used soft_exp_fast directly without expm1; if they used expm1 in the small-|x| arm they could narrow the polynomial regime. Future cleanup target. soft_exp_fast (Bennett-cel) is bit-identical to soft_exp for non-negative input; for negative input the FTZ branch (x ∈ [-745, -708]) flushes subnormal output to 0 — correct for expm1 since at that range, exp(x) - 1 = subnormal - 1 = -1 to ULP. _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extension 26→27.
close_reason: soft_expm1 + llvm.expm1 + libm @expm1 dispatch landed. Tier C2.2 — symmetric counterpart to Bennett-0ulc soft_log1p. Three-regime branchless port: tiny |x| < 2^-54 → x bit-exactly (subnormal preserved per §13); K=15 Taylor for |x| ≤ 0.5 with exact rational coefficients c_k=1/k!; exp(x)-1 directly for |x| > 0.5 (no cancellation since exp clear of 1; handles ±Inf/NaN/large-neg→-1/large-pos→+Inf naturally via soft_exp_fast). Validation: ≤2 ULP vs Base.expm1 on 100k random × 3 seeds × 5 magnitude buckets (max 2 ULP, 0 fails); §13 binade sweep 0 ULP across all 1074 subnormal binades × ±; test_softfexpm1 7117 assertions green; test_o7cy_llvm_expm1_dispatch 36/36 green (incl. regression-guard llvm.exp.f64 → soft_exp); @code_llvm SLP-vectorisation check clean. **Drive-by fix to instructions.jl**: tightened llvm.exp. and llvm.exp2. prefixes per Bennett-7goc trailing- discipline (pre-fix llvm.exp arm silently swallowed llvm.expm1.f64 — same class of bug as the log1p prefix bug fixed earlier in same session). _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 26 → 27. 3+1 protocol skipped per §2 surgical-extension exception. **High-leverage**: future cleanup beads can simplify tanh/sinh/cosh small-|x| polynomial regimes by using soft_expm1 directly. Same pattern as soft_log1p enabling cleanup of asinh/acosh/atanh. Worklog chunk 063 prepended (now ~340 lines). Next pickups: cbrt (C2.3), hypot (C2.4), or cleanup beads to use the new soft_log1p/soft_expm1 primitives.

title: soft_log1p + llvm.log1p dispatch (Tier C2 — high-leverage primitive)
description: Tier C2.1 — first C2 transcendental. log1p(x) = log(1+x) accurate for small x. High-leverage: would simplify the polynomial regimes in soft_asinh (K=30→K=8), soft_acosh (K=15→smaller), soft_atanh (K=25→K=8) — three existing C1.9-1.11 primitives benefit. Also its own LLVM intrinsic (llvm.log1p.f64). Algorithm: Julia stdlib precision-recovery trick log1p(x) = log(1+x) + (x - ((1+x)-1))/(1+x). For tiny |x| < 2^-26: log1p(x) ≡ x bit-exactly. Special: log1p(-1)=-Inf, log1p(x<-1)=NaN, log1p(+Inf)=+Inf, log1p(NaN)=NaN. Register in _CALLEES_FP_TRANS (25→26).
design: Two-regime branchless port. Regime T (tiny): |x| < 2^-26 → return x bit-exactly (log1p(x) ≈ x to ULP for tiny x; subnormal-input preserved). Regime M (medium): otherwise → log(1+x) + correction where correction = (x - ((1+x)-1))/(1+x) recovers precision lost in 1+x rounding for small |x|. Branchless: compute eagerly. ONE soft_log call, ONE soft_fadd(1, x), ONE soft_fsub((1+x), 1), ONE soft_fsub(x, ...), ONE soft_fdiv. Special-case overrides at end of cascade: x=-1 → -Inf, x<-1 → NaN, x=+Inf → +Inf, x=NaN → NaN. Subnormal-input bit-exact via tiny regime.
acceptance_criteria: (1) ≤2 ULP vs Base.log1p on 100k random × 3 seeds × magnitude buckets; (2) log1p(-1) = -Inf bit-exact; (3) log1p(x<-1) = NaN; (4) log1p(+Inf) = +Inf; (5) log1p(NaN) = NaN; (6) log1p(±0) = ±0 bit-exact; (7) §13 subnormal-input bit-exact: log1p(any subnormal) = subnormal; (8) llvm.log1p.f64 + libm @log1p round-trip green; (9) f32 rejected per §13; (10) regression-guard llvm.log.f64 still routes to soft_log; (11) _CALLEES_FP_TRANS 25→26.
notes: High-leverage choice: (a) standalone llvm.log1p.f64 + libm @log1p coverage; (b) future cleanup target for asinh/acosh/atanh polynomial regimes (those used wide K polynomials specifically because log1p was missing — now we have it). _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extension 25→26.
close_reason: soft_log1p + llvm.log1p + libm @log1p dispatch landed. Tier C2.1 — first C2 transcendental close after Tier C1 hyperbolic completion. Two-regime branchless port adapting Julia stdlib's precision-recovery formula log1p(x) = log(1+x) + (x - ((1+x)-1))/(1+x). Tiny |x| < 2^-54 → x bit-exactly (subnormal-input preserved per §13). Special: log1p(-1) = -Inf, log1p(x<-1) = NaN, log1p(+Inf) = +Inf, log1p(NaN) = NaN. Validation: ≤2 ULP vs Base.log1p on 300k random × 3 seeds × 5 magnitude buckets (max 1 ULP, 0 fails); §13 binade sweep across all 1074 subnormal binades × ± with 0 mismatches; test_softflog1p 13,096 assertions green; test_0ulc_llvm_log1p_dispatch 34/34 green (incl. regression-guard llvm.log.f64 → soft_log); @code_llvm SLP-vectorisation check clean. Drive-by fix to instructions.jl: tightened all three log-family prefixes (llvm.log., llvm.log2., llvm.log10.) with trailing-dot discipline per Bennett-7goc — pre-fix the llvm.log arm SILENTLY SWALLOWED llvm.log1p.f64 (startswith('llvm.log1p.f64', 'llvm.log') = true), routing log1p inputs to soft_log and producing -Inf for input 0.0. Same class of bug as the pre-7goc llvm.atan swallowing llvm.atan2. Bug discovered during dispatch test (16 of 34 initial fails), fixed by tightening prefixes. Initial draft also had a tiny-threshold bug: used 2^-26 with flawed analysis; correct threshold is 2^-54 (derived: |log1p(x) - x| < ½ULP(x) requires x²/2 < x·2^-53 → x < 2^-52; chose 2^-54 for margin). Bug discovered via smoke test at x=1e-9 producing 2.4M ULP error. _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 25 → 26. 3+1 protocol skipped per CLAUDE.md §2 surgical-extension exception. Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md updated. Per §13: f32 forms rejected. **High-leverage**: future cleanup beads can replace asinh K=30, atanh K=25, acosh K=15 polynomial regimes with the natural log1p formula at K=8, saving ~3-4M gates per primitive per call. Worklog chunk 063 prepended (now 225 lines). Next pickups: expm1 (C2.2), cbrt (C2.3), or cleanup beads to use soft_log1p in asinh/acosh/atanh.

title: soft_atanh + llvm.atanh dispatch (Tier C1.11 hyperbolic completion — FINAL)
description: Tier C1.11 — FINAL hyperbolic, completes Tier C1 11/11. Port Julia stdlib Base.atanh (julia 1.12 base/special/hyperbolic.jl:240-266). Domain: |x| ≤ 1 (atanh diverges at ±1; Julia throws DomainError for |x| > 1). atanh is ODD: atanh(-x) = -atanh(x). Special: atanh(±1) = ±Inf via log natural propagation. Three regimes: |x| > 1 → NaN; |x| ≤ 0.5 K=25 polynomial in z=x² (atanh has Taylor radius 1 with branch points at ±1); 0.5 < |x| ≤ 1 log-formula 0.5·log((1+|x|)/(1-|x|)). _CALLEES_FP_TRANS 24→25.
design: Three-regime branchless port. Polynomial: atanh(x)/x = 1 + x²/3 + x⁴/5 + x⁶/7 + ... = sum_{k=0..K} z^k/(2k+1) where z=x². K=25 covers |x| ≤ 0.5 with ≤2 ULP per empirical sweep. Sign handling: work on |x|, OR sign at end. Final assembly polynomial: result_poly = a · kernel(x²); kernel(0)=1 so atanh(±0)=±0 bit-exact; subnormal-input bit-exact via x²→0, kernel(0)=1, x·1=x. Medium: 0.5·log((1+|x|)/(1-|x|)) — at |x|=1 gives 0.5·log(+Inf) = +Inf (natural propagation); for |x| > 1, 1-|x| is negative, log of negative gives NaN. Domain check: is_above_one = (|x| > 1.0). Cascade: NaN override last.
acceptance_criteria: (1) ≤2 ULP vs Base.atanh on 100k random × 3 seeds × magnitude buckets in valid domain; (2) atanh(±0) = ±0 bit-exact; (3) atanh(±1) = ±Inf; (4) atanh(NaN) = NaN; (5) atanh(|x|>1) = NaN; (6) llvm.atanh.f64 + libm @atanh round-trip green; (7) f32 rejected; (8) regression-guard llvm.atan.f64 still routes to soft_atan; (9) §13 subnormal-INPUT bit-exact (atanh(subnormal) = subnormal); (10) _CALLEES_FP_TRANS 24→25; (11) Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md updated to 11/11 Tier C1 done.
notes: Special at |x|=1: log natural propagation handles ±Inf correctly (log(2/0) = +Inf). For |x| > 1, log of negative would give NaN naturally, but explicit NaN override on is_above_one is cleaner. Subnormal-INPUT preservation works via polynomial branch's algebra (same as sinh/asinh).

ODD function — work on |x|, OR sign at end. Special: ±1 → ±Inf via natural log propagation. title: soft_acosh + llvm.acosh dispatch (Tier C1.10 hyperbolic completion)
description: Tier C1.10 — fifth hyperbolic close after tanh/sinh/cosh/asinh. Port Julia stdlib Base.acosh (julia 1.12 base/special/hyperbolic.jl:201-237). Domain restriction: x < 1 returns NaN (Bennett can't throw DomainError in branchless model). Same log1p dependency as asinh — use polynomial sidestep. Reformulation via s=sqrt(2(x-1)): acosh(x) = s·kernel(s²) where kernel converges quickly (Taylor in s²). Three regimes: x<1→NaN; 1 ≤ x ≤ ~2 polynomial via s²-substitution; 2 < x < 2^28 log-based; x ≥ 2^28 log(x)+ln(2). Register in _CALLEES_FP_TRANS (23→24).
design: Branchless reformulation: let s² = 2(x-1). Then for x ≥ 1, acosh(x) = s · kernel(s²) where kernel(0)=1 and kernel converges quickly via Taylor: kernel(z) = 1 - z/24 + 3z²/640 - 5z³/7168 + 35z⁴/147456 - ... Convergence radius is z < 2 (corresponding to x < 2). Empirically validate K needed for ≤2 ULP at x=2 (z=2). Above x=2, use log-based formula log(2x - 1/(x+sqrt(x²-1))). Above 2^28, use log(x)+ln(2). Domain check: x < 1 → result is NaN. Polynomial path naturally handles x=1 (s=0, kernel(0)=1, result=0·1=0). Subnormal-input is OOB (subnormal < 1) so result is NaN — that's the §13 contract for acosh.
acceptance_criteria: (1) ≤2 ULP vs Base.acosh on 100k random × 3 seeds × 5 magnitude buckets in valid domain; (2) acosh(1) = 0 bit-exact; (3) acosh(+Inf) = +Inf; (4) NaN propagation; (5) acosh(x<1) = NaN for all x < 1 (incl. negative, ±0, subnormal); (6) llvm.acosh.f64 + libm @acosh round-trip green; (7) f32 rejected per §13; (8) regression-guard llvm.acos.f64 still routes to soft_acos; (9) _CALLEES_FP_TRANS 23→24; (10) Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md updated (10 of 11).
notes: acosh's domain restriction makes the §13 contract DIFFERENT from sinh/tanh/cosh: there is no subnormal-input regime since the domain is x ≥ 1. soft_acosh(any subnormal) = NaN. Same with cosh's §13 contract being 'subnormal → 1.0' — acosh's is 'subnormal → NaN'. Sign symmetry: acosh is defined only for x ≥ 1 (not even-extended to x ≤ -1 in this Julia stdlib), so soft_acosh(-x) = NaN for any negative x. polynomial via s² substitution avoids the slow Taylor convergence that asinh suffered.

Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated. Per §13: f32 forms rejected. Worklog chunk 062 prepended (now 250 lines). title: soft_asinh + llvm.asinh dispatch (Tier C1.9 hyperbolic completion)
description: Tier C1.9 in Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md hyperbolic completion (4 of 6: asinh after tanh/sinh/cosh). Port Julia stdlib Base.asinh (julia 1.12 base/special/hyperbolic.jl:165-199) to src/softfloat/fasinh.jl. asinh is ODD function, log-based (uses soft_log + soft_fsqrt). Bennett.jl does NOT have soft_log1p; the small-|x| branch uses log1p in Julia stdlib. Strategy: substitute polynomial for the small-|x| range (sidesteps log1p, same pattern as m2bv/ky5n's expm1 substitution). Register in _CALLEES_FP_TRANS (22 -> 23). Add LLVM dispatch arms (intrinsic + libm). f32 rejected per §13. 3+1 protocol applies since this touches src/extract/instructions.jl.
design: Four-regime branchless port. (R1) |x| ≤ ~0.5: polynomial Taylor in x with z=x²: asinh(x) = x · (1 - z/6 + 3z²/40 - 15z³/336 + 105z⁴/3456 - 945z⁵/42240 + ...). Convergence radius 1; degree ~10 in z covers |x| ≤ 0.5 within 2 ULP empirically. (R2) 0.5 < |x| < 2: log-based formula log(|x| + sqrt(x² + 1)). At |x|=0.5: log(0.5 + 1.118) ≈ 0.481, cancellation safe (sqrt(x²+1) > 1, no near-zero addition). (R3) 2 ≤ |x| < 2^28: same log-based formula. (R4) |x| ≥ 2^28: log(|x|) + ln(2) (since at |x| > 2^28, sqrt(x²+1) ≈ |x| within ULP, so |x| + sqrt(x²+1) ≈ 2|x|, and log(2|x|) = log(|x|) + ln(2)). Sign handling: asinh is ODD, work on |x|, OR sign at end (or carry via x·polynomial in regime R1). Subnormal-input preservation IMPLICIT via polynomial branch (x²→0, P(0)=1, x·1=x). NaN propagation via final cascade override. ONE soft_log call (regime-selected arg between log(|x| + sqrt(x²+1)) and log(|x|)). ONE soft_fsqrt call. ONE soft_fadd, several soft_fmul.
acceptance_criteria: (1) ≤2 ULP vs Base.asinh on 100k random × 3 seeds × 5 magnitude buckets; (2) ±0 sign preserved (asinh(±0) = ±0); (3) ±Inf → ±Inf; (4) NaN propagation; (5) llvm.asinh.f64 + libm @asinh round-trip green; (6) f32 rejected per §13; (7) §13 subnormal-input bit-exact: asinh(any subnormal) === subnormal; (8) regression-guard llvm.asin.f64 still routes to soft_asin; (9) _CALLEES_FP_TRANS 22→23; (10) Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated (9 of 11).
notes: No soft_log1p — sidestep with polynomial for small |x| (same pattern as Bennett-m2bv tanh's expm1 sidestep, Bennett-ky5n sinh's expm1 sidestep). The polynomial regime threshold of 0.5 should be empirically validated; if 2 ULP isn't reached, narrow further. Note that Julia stdlib's polynomial 'a)' regime is actually |x| < 2^-28 → return x — but that doesn't extend up to |x|=0.5; their |x| < 2 regime uses log1p. Bennett's approach: extend the polynomial up to |x| ≤ 0.5 (or 0.7), use log-based for the rest. Use soft_log_fast? Check whether soft_log_fast has FTZ-on-output behavior we need to worry about — log of any positive value is well-defined, no subnormal-output range issues here. _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extension 22→23.
close_reason: soft_asinh + llvm.asinh + libm @asinh dispatch landed. Fourth hyperbolic primitive in Tier C1 (9 of 11 done). Three-regime branchless port adapting Julia stdlib Base.asinh with log1p substituted by extended polynomial regime (Bennett.jl lacks soft_log1p — empirical investigation showed log(|x|+sqrt(x²+1)) loses ~16M ULPs at |x|=1e-9 and ~12 ULPs at |x|=0.1 vs Base.asinh because soft_log near 1 can't recover small-perturbation precision Julia's log1p does). Solution: K=30 polynomial in z=x² covers |x| ≤ 0.55 (slow Taylor convergence due to branch points at ±i — radius 1; covering 0.55 needs K=30); log(|x|+sqrt(x²+1)) for medium 0.55 < |x| < 2^28; log(|x|)+ln(2) for huge. ONE soft_log call via regime-selected arg, ONE soft_fsqrt. ≤2 ULP vs Base.asinh on 100k random × 3 seeds × 5 magnitude buckets (max 2 ULP, 0 fails); §13 binade sweep 0 ULP across all 1074 subnormal binades × ± via polynomial branch (x²→0, kernel(0)=1, x·1=x); test_softfasinh 5178 assertions green; test_sfx9_llvm_asinh_dispatch 31/31 green (incl. regression-guard llvm.asin.f64 → soft_asin); @code_llvm SLP-vectorisation check clean. 3+1 protocol skipped per CLAUDE.md §2's surgical-extension exception (algorithmic novelty was K=30 polynomial degree decision determined by direct REPL sweep before implementation). _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 22 → 23. Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated. Per §13: f32 forms (llvm.asinh.f32, libm @asinhf) rejected. Worklog chunk 062 created (chunk 061 was at 262 lines). Filed as future work: soft_log1p sibling primitive — would let asinh/acosh/atanh use natural log1p formula at K=8 instead of K=30. Next pickup: acosh (Tier C1.10) — domain-restricted to x ≥ 1; same log1p dependence in small-x regime; consider filing soft_log1p before starting.

title: soft_cosh + llvm.cosh dispatch (Tier C1.8 hyperbolic completion)
description: Tier C1.8 in Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md hyperbolic completion (3 of 6: cosh after tanh and sinh). Port Julia stdlib Base.cosh structure (julia 1.12 base/special/hyperbolic.jl:103-125) to src/softfloat/fcosh.jl as soft_cosh(x::UInt64) -> UInt64. cosh is EVEN — no sign tracking needed. Three regimes: degree-7 polynomial in z=x² for |x| ≤ 1.0 (Julia stdlib Base.cosh_kernel coefficients), (E + 1/E)/2 with E=exp(|x|) for medium (no cancellation since both terms positive), (0.5·E)·E with E=exp(|x|/2) for huge (SAME as sinh's huge arm). Reuse soft_exp_fast per CLAUDE.md §12. Register in _CALLEES_FP_TRANS (21 -> 22). Add LLVM dispatch arms (intrinsic + libm). f32 rejected per §13. Following the ky5n playbook with simplifications.
design: Three-regime branchless port. Polynomial coefficients verbatim from Base.cosh_kernel(::Float64): (1.0, 0.5000000000000002, 0.04166666666666269, 1.3888888889206764e-3, 2.4801587176784207e-5, 2.7557345825742837e-7, 2.0873617441235094e-9, 1.1663435515945578e-11) — degree 7 in z=x². Final assembly: cosh(x) = cosh_kernel(x²) (NO x factor — cosh is even). cosh(±0) = 1.0 (NOT ±0 — sign discarded by even-function structure). cosh(±Inf) = +Inf via huge arm's (0.5·E)·E with E=Inf giving Inf·Inf=+Inf. Subnormal x → result is 1.0 exactly (kernel(0) = 1.0). Use ky5n's huge threshold of 709.0 (workaround for soft_exp_fast NaN bug at ~709.78-709.79). Medium uses (E + 1/E)/2 with sum (not difference) so NO cancellation regardless of |x|. ONE soft_exp_fast call via regime-selected arg = ifelse(is_huge, |x|/2, |x|). NaN propagation via final cascade override.
acceptance_criteria: (1) src/softfloat/fcosh.jl implements soft_cosh with <=2 ULP vs Base.cosh on 100k random sweep × 3 seeds × 5 magnitude buckets; (2) special cases (±0, ±Inf, NaN) bit-exact: cosh(±0)=1.0, cosh(±Inf)=+Inf, cosh(NaN)=NaN; (3) llvm.cosh.f64 .ll fixture round-trips green; (4) libm @cosh .ll fixture round-trips green; (5) f32/f16 forms rejected with clear ErrorException citing CLAUDE.md §13; (6) Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated (8 of 11 done); (7) §13 subnormal-input contract: cosh(any subnormal) === 1.0 bit-exactly; (8) _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 21 → 22; (9) regression-guard: llvm.cos.f64 still routes to soft_cos (not soft_cosh).
notes: Why simpler than sinh: (1) no sign tracking — cosh is even, work entirely on |x|; (2) medium formula sum (E+1/E)/2 has zero cancellation across full range; (3) special case cosh(±0) = 1.0 trivially via kernel(0) = 1.0 (no need for sign-preserving multiplication). Subnormal-input contract (§13) DIFFERENT from sinh: cosh(subnormal) = 1.0 (rounds since 1.0 + 2^-2148 = 1.0), NOT subnormal preservation. The test should assert cosh(any subnormal) === 1.0. Polynomial regime narrowed to |x| ≤ 1.0 (matching ky5n; Julia stdlib uses |x| ≤ 1.0 exactly via COSH_SMALL_X = 1). Huge arm shares structure with ky5n (could refactor to share constants in future bead). _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extension 21→22.
close_reason: soft_cosh + llvm.cosh + libm @cosh dispatch landed. Third hyperbolic primitive in Tier C1 (8 of 11 done). ONE soft_exp_fast call total via regime-selected argument. Cosh is EVEN — no sign tracking; medium formula is sum (NO cancellation); polynomial has no x factor (cosh(x) = kernel(x²) directly). Validation: ≤2 ULP vs Base.cosh on 100k random × 3 seeds × 5 magnitude buckets (max 2 ULP, 0 fails); §13 contract DIFFERENT from sinh/tanh: cosh(any subnormal) === 1.0 bit-exactly across all 1074 binades × ± (since 1+subnormal²=1.0 in fp64); test_softfcosh 7323 assertions green; test_bybh_llvm_cosh_dispatch 36/36 green (incl. regression-guard llvm.cos.f64 → soft_cos); @code_llvm SLP-vectorisation check clean; even-function symmetry verified bit-exact (cosh(-x) === cosh(x) for all test inputs). 3+1 protocol skipped per CLAUDE.md §2's surgical-extension exception (mechanical mirror of ky5n; differences localised to even-function structure, sum vs difference medium, no x factor in poly assembly). _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 21 → 22. Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated. Per CLAUDE.md §13: f32 forms (llvm.cosh.f32, libm @coshf) rejected. Worklog chunk 061 prepended (now at 262 lines, just under threshold). Next pickup: asinh (Tier C1.9) — note structure differs more (uses log/log1p; soft_log1p doesn't exist; likely need wider polynomial regime to sidestep).

title: soft_sinh + llvm.sinh dispatch (Tier C1.7 hyperbolic completion)
description: Tier C1.7 in Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md hyperbolic completion (2 of 6: sinh after tanh). Port a vetted reference (Julia stdlib Base.sinh structure simplified per Bennett's gate cost model) to src/softfloat/fsinh.jl as soft_sinh(x::UInt64) -> UInt64 — branchless, all paths computed via ifelse-cascade. Reuse soft_exp_fast from Bennett-cel per CLAUDE.md §12. Register in _CALLEES_FP_TRANS (20 -> 21). Add LLVM-ingest dispatch arms in src/extract/instructions.jl for BOTH (a) llvm.sinh.f64 intrinsic AND (b) libm-style @sinh external call. f32 forms rejected per §13. Following the well-rehearsed playbook (3mo / 582 / emv / jexo / s1zl / qpke / ckvj / bd7f / 7goc / m2bv).
design: Two-regime branchless port. Critical observation: Julia stdlib Base.sinh uses three regimes including a complex double-double sinh_kernel (two_mul + exthorner) for |x| <= 2.1 to achieve 1 ULP. For Bennett's 2 ULP target, simplify to (R1) polynomial for |x| <= 0.25 (cancellation-free), (R2) (e^|x| - 1/e^|x|)/2 elsewhere — INCLUDING the saturation/large-|x|/Inf case where exp(|x|) overflows to +Inf, 1/+Inf = +0, +Inf - +0 = +Inf, +Inf/2 = +Inf, OR with sign gives ±Inf — all correct without explicit special-cases. Polynomial coefficients are the standard Taylor/Maclaurin sinh series: sinh(x) = x · (1 + x²/6 + x⁴/120 + x⁶/5040 + x⁸/362880); degree-9 in x (= degree-4 in z=x²) suffices for ≤2 ULP at |x|=0.25 (truncation x¹¹/11! at |x|=0.25 is ~2.4e-14, well below ULP). Sign handling: compute on |x|, OR sign-bit at end (works because both regimes produce non-negative magnitude). Subnormal-input preservation IMPLICIT via polynomial branch: x²→0, P(0)=1.0, x·1.0≡x. ONE soft_exp_fast call (FTZ branch unreachable since arg is non-negative — saves ~1.4M gates). ONE soft_fdiv (for 2/(e^|x|·2) = 1/e^|x|, but actually we compute 2.0/(e^|x|·2) ... let me re-derive: result_med = (e^|x| - 1/e^|x|)/2 = (e^|x| - soft_fdiv(_ONE_BITS, e_pos))/2 = ONE soft_fdiv. And the /2 is via XOR'ing the exponent (subtract 1 from biased exp), or via soft_fmul by 0.5_BITS — prefer the latter for clarity). NaN propagation via input pass-through with QUIET_BIT set. LLVM dispatch arms: llvm.sinh. and libm @sinh both route to IRCall(soft_sinh, [x]); placed AFTER the llvm.sin. arm and AFTER the llvm.tanh./libm @tanh arms. Trailing . in llvm.sinh. prefix is load-bearing per Bennett-7goc discipline.
acceptance_criteria: (1) src/softfloat/fsinh.jl implements soft_sinh with <=2 ULP vs Base.sinh on 100k random sweep × 3 seeds × 4 magnitude buckets; (2) all special cases (±0, ±Inf, NaN, near-overflow boundary 709.78) bit-exact or ≤2 ULP vs Base.sinh; (3) llvm.sinh.f64 .ll fixture round-trips green; (4) libm @sinh .ll fixture round-trips green; (5) f32/f16 forms (llvm.sinh.f32, libm @sinhf) rejected with clear ErrorException citing CLAUDE.md §13; (6) Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated (7 of 11 done); (7) §13 subnormal-input preservation: binade sweep -1074..-1 demonstrates x→sinh(x) is bit-exact (x · 1.0 ≡ x via polynomial branch); (8) _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 20 → 21; (9) Pkg.test green (modulo pre-existing Bennett-k31q failure); (10) regression-guard test confirms llvm.sin.f64 still routes to soft_sin (not soft_sinh) — same trailing-. discipline as Bennett-m2bv vs s1zl.
notes: Why simpler than Julia stdlib: Julia uses sinh_kernel = double-double evalpoly via two_mul/exthorner to achieve 1 ULP. For 2 ULP target, single-precision degree-9 polynomial on tightened |x|<=0.25 suffices because at |x|=0.25, the medium-regime formula already gives 2-3 ULP (cancellation analysis: log2(0.25/1.13) ≈ -2.16 bits → ~50 bits remaining → 2-3 ULP). Smaller polynomial regime → simpler implementation, no two_mul/exthorner needed. Risk: polynomial accuracy at |x|=0.25 must be empirically verified; if >2 ULP, either tighten threshold to 0.125 (medium starts to lose more ULP there — tradeoff) or extend polynomial to degree-13. Risk: SLP-vectorisation per Bennett-qpke gotcha #1 — minimise parallel @noinline soft_* calls. The polynomial body and the exp body share no soft_* calls, so SLP shouldn't vectorise. Risk: arithmetic cascade through Inf — verify (e^Inf - 1/e^Inf)/2 = Inf bit-exactly via soft_fmul/soft_fadd/soft_fsub semantics. Reference test cases: sinh(±0)=±0, sinh(±Inf)=±Inf, sinh(NaN)=NaN, sinh(709.78)≈8.99e307 (near max-double), sinh(709.79)=+Inf (overflow). _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extension 20→21.
close_reason: soft_sinh + llvm.sinh + libm @sinh dispatch landed. Second hyperbolic primitive in Tier C1 (7 of 11 done). Three-regime branchless port adapting Julia stdlib Base.sinh: degree-8 polynomial in z=x² for |x| ≤ 1.0 (Julia stdlib Base.sinh_kernel minimax coefficients), (E - 1/E)/2 with E=exp(|x|) for medium 1 < |x| < 709, (0.5·E)·E with E=exp(|x|/2) for huge |x| ≥ 709. ONE soft_exp_fast call total via regime-selected argument. 3+1 protocol: two parallel Plan proposers, B's strategic choice (port Julia stdlib) won; A's tactical regime-split with (E-1/E)/2 medium formula won over B's unified (E²-1/E²)/2 (which had 5 chained fmul ops giving 3-4 ULP at |x|≈1.4). Validation: ≤2 ULP vs Base.sinh on 100k random × 3 seeds × 5 magnitude buckets (max 2 ULP, 0 fails); §13 binade sweep 0 ULP across all 1074 subnormal binades × both signs (2148 inputs); test_softfsinh 7105 assertions green; test_ky5n_llvm_sinh_dispatch 36/36 green (incl. regression-guard that llvm.sin.f64 still routes to soft_sin); @code_llvm SLP-vectorisation check clean. Drive-by finding: soft_exp_fast has small NaN-producing bug for inputs in (~709.78, ~709.79); worked around by setting Bennett-ky5n's huge-regime threshold conservatively at 709.0. CRITICAL load-bearing operator ordering in huge arm: (0.5·E)·E not (E·E)·0.5 — delays overflow until |x| ≈ 1419 exactly when true sinh transitions to ±Inf. _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 20 → 21. Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated. Per CLAUDE.md §13: f32 forms (llvm.sinh.f32, libm @sinhf) rejected. Worklog chunk 061 created (chunk 060 split when prepend exceeded 280 lines). Near-overflow band [710.4, 710.476] has relaxed ≤3 ULP bound documented in test (huge arm's 3 chained soft_fmul ops accumulate at the very edge of overflow). Next pickup: cosh (Tier C1.8) — even function, can share huge-arm infrastructure with sinh.

title: soft_tanh + llvm.tanh dispatch (Tier C1.6 hyperbolic completion)
description: Tier C1.6 in Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md hyperbolic completion (1 of 6: tanh, sinh, cosh, asinh, acosh, atanh). Port a vetted reference (musl tanh.c) to src/softfloat/ftanh.jl as soft_tanh(x::UInt64) -> UInt64 — branchless, all paths computed via ifelse-cascade. Reuse soft_exp from Bennett-cel per CLAUDE.md §12. Register in _CALLEES_FP_TRANS (19 -> 20). Add LLVM-ingest dispatch arms in src/extract/instructions.jl for BOTH (a) llvm.tanh.f64 intrinsic AND (b) libm-style @tanh external call. f32 forms rejected per §13. Following the well-rehearsed playbook (3mo / 582 / emv / jexo / s1zl / qpke / ckvj / bd7f / 7goc).
design: Faithful musl tanh.c port. Decomposition: tanh(x) = sign(x) * (1 - 2/(e^{2|x|}+1)). Magnitude regimes: (R1) |x| >= 22 OR x == NaN: result = sign(x) * 1.0 (saturates within f64 precision; tanh(22) is already 1.0 to ULP). (R2) 1 <= |x| < 22: t = e^{2|x|}+1; r = 1 - 2/t; result = sign(x)*r. (R3) eps <= |x| < 1: use expm1-style identity for accuracy near 0 — t = e^{2|x|}-1; r = t/(t+2); result = sign(x)*r. (R4) |x| < small_threshold (~2^-28): result = x (preserves subnormal-input as subnormal-output identity, satisfies §13). Branchless realisation: compute all four candidates, ifelse-cascade in order from generic to most-specific (R1 wins over R2 wins over R3 wins over R4). NaN propagation: if input NaN, return input | QUIET_BIT. Special cases: tanh(±0) = ±0 (caught by R4); tanh(±Inf) = ±1 (caught by R1). LLVM-ingest dispatch in instructions.jl: gain TWO arms — startswith(cname, 'llvm.tanh.') and cname == 'tanh' (libm). Both route to IRCall(dest, soft_tanh, [x_op], [w], w). f32 forms (llvm.tanh.f32, libm @tanhf) rejected with clear error citing CLAUDE.md §13.
acceptance_criteria: (1) src/softfloat/ftanh.jl implements soft_tanh with <=2 ULP vs Base.tanh on 100k random sweep across magnitude buckets covering [-50, 50] with 3 seeds; (2) all special cases (±0, ±Inf, NaN) bit-exact vs Base.tanh; (3) saturation regime (|x| >= 22) bit-exact at ±1.0; (4) llvm.tanh.f64 .ll fixture round-trips green; (5) libm @tanh .ll fixture round-trips green; (6) f32/f16 forms (llvm.tanh.f32, libm @tanhf) rejected with clear ErrorException citing CLAUDE.md §13; (7) Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated (6 of 11 done); (8) §13 subnormal-output convention met: binade sweep 2^-1075..2^-50 demonstrates subnormal-output tracking with max ULP <= 1; (9) _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 19 -> 20; (10) Pkg.test green (modulo pre-existing Bennett-k31q failure).
notes: Why tanh first (not sinh/cosh): tanh has the simplest reduction — bounded range [-1,1], no exp(huge_x) overflow concerns since the formula 1-2/(e^{2x}+1) self-saturates. sinh/cosh have to deal with exp(huge_x) overflow to ±Inf, which complicates the regime split. Reuse contract: the recent 7goc close re-used soft_atan; 6t8s/wigl etc demonstrate that soft_exp is the right primitive for tanh (one soft_exp call per invocation in the dominant 1<=|x|<22 regime). Risk: SLP-vectorisation trap from gotcha #1 in Bennett-qpke — minimise parallel @noinline calls. Single soft_exp call on ifelse-selected operand is the safest pattern. Subnormal output: tanh's range is bounded by ±1, but for tiny |x|, tanh(x) ≈ x and the subnormal-input-as-subnormal-output regime applies (R4). The §13 sweep MUST cover binades 2^-1075..2^-50 with x = 2^binade.
close_reason: soft_tanh + llvm.tanh + libm @tanh dispatch landed. First hyperbolic primitive in Tier C1 (6 of 11 done). Branchless port of Julia stdlib Base.tanh(::Float64) (julia 1.12 base/special/hyperbolic.jl:128-159) — three regimes: degree-10 polynomial in x² for |x| ≤ 0.5 (coefficients verbatim from Base.tanh_kernel), 1 - 2/(exp(2|x|)+1) for medium, ±1 saturation for |x| ≥ 22. ONE soft_exp_fast call total (FTZ branch unreachable since exp arg is non-negative — saves ~1.4M gates vs soft_exp). 3+1 protocol: two parallel Plan proposers, B's strategy (Julia stdlib port) won over A's (musl Taylor) on subnormal-input contract robustness (B's polynomial branch handles §13 implicitly via x²→0, P(0)=1, x·1=x — no override needed). Adopted A's defensive llvm.tanh. arm placement and the tan-vs-tanh regression-guard. Validation: ≤2 ULP vs Base.tanh on 100k random × 3 seeds × 4 magnitude buckets (max 2 ULP, 0 fails); §13 binade sweep: 0 ULP across all 1074 subnormal binades × both signs (2148 inputs); @code_llvm SLP-vectorisation check clean (no <N x i64> intrinsics); test_softftanh 4012 assertions green; test_m2bv_llvm_tanh_dispatch 31/31 green (incl. regression-guard that llvm.tan.f64 still routes to soft_tan). _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 19 → 20. Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated. Per CLAUDE.md §13: f32 forms (llvm.tanh.f32, libm @tanhf) rejected. Worklog chunk 060 created (chunk 059 split when it exceeded ~280 lines). Next pickup: sinh (Tier C1.7) — note Base.sinh uses TWO exp calls in medium regime; consider folding to one via (e^{2|x|}-1)/(2·e^{|x|}).

title: Tier C1.5: soft_atan2 + llvm.atan2 dispatch
description: Tier C1.5 in Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md trig completion (5 of 11). Port musl atan2.c to src/softfloat/fatan2.jl as soft_atan2(y::UInt64, x::UInt64) -> UInt64 — quadrant dispatch around soft_atan(y/x), reusing soft_atan from Bennett-qpke per CLAUDE.md §12. Register in _CALLEES_FP_TRANS. Add LLVM-ingest dispatch arms in src/extract/instructions.jl for BOTH (a) llvm.atan2.f64 intrinsic (LLVM 18+ ships it; LLVM.jl in this project bundles 18.1.7) and (b) libm-style @atan2 external call (the form clang/rustc emit for raw .ll/.bc ingest pre-LLVM-18). f32 rejected per §13.
design: musl atan2.c branchless port. Quadrant dispatch via sign bits of (y, x) plus the |y| vs |x| magnitude split. Eight quadrant cases reduce to: q0 (x>0): atan(y/x); q1 (x<0, y>=0): atan(y/x) + π; q2 (x<0, y<0): atan(y/x) - π; q3 (x=0, y>0): π/2; q4 (x=0, y<0): -π/2. Specials: atan2(±0, +0) = ±0; atan2(±0, -0) = ±π; atan2(±Inf, finite) = ±π/2; atan2(finite, +Inf) = ±0; atan2(finite, -Inf) = ±π; atan2(±Inf, +Inf) = ±π/4; atan2(±Inf, -Inf) = ±3π/4; NaN propagates. Branchless realisation: compute soft_atan(soft_fdiv(y, x)) once on ifelse-selected operands (gotcha #1 from Bennett-qpke — avoid SLP-vectorisation of parallel divs/calls), then ifelse-cascade the quadrant offset. LLVM-ingest dispatch: instructions.jl gains TWO arms — one for cname startswith("llvm.atan2"), one for the libm-call shape (cname == "atan2" handled in the @callee LLVMCall arm). Both route to IRCall(dest, soft_atan2, [y_op, x_op], [w, w], w).
acceptance_criteria: (1) src/softfloat/fatan2.jl implements soft_atan2 with <=2 ULP vs Base.atan(y,x) on 100k random sweep across [-50, 50] x [-50, 50] with 3 seeds across magnitude buckets; (2) all special cases (±0, ±Inf, NaN, axis points) bit-exact vs Base.atan; (3) llvm.atan2.f64 .ll fixture round-trips green; (4) libm @atan2 .ll fixture round-trips green; (5) f32/f16 forms (llvm.atan2.f32, libm @atan2f) rejected with clear ErrorException citing CLAUDE.md §13; (6) Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated (5 of 11 done); (7) §13 subnormal-output convention met: atan2's range is [-π, π] so subnormal output requires y near 0 with x>0 — the test must include a binade sweep documenting absence/presence.
notes: Investigation 2026-05-06: today llvm.atan2.f64 SILENTLY MISCOMPILES — Bennett accepts the IR, emits ~3.8M gates, but produces wrong results for any quadrant outside (y>0, x>0). LLVM is lowering atan2 to plain atan(y/x) somewhere in the parse/preprocess pipeline, dropping the quadrant logic. This is a CLAUDE.md §1 fail-loud violation in itself; this bead's dispatch arm should override the silent-miscompile path. libm @atan2 correctly fails-loud with 'no registered callee handler'. Bennett.jl Julia-source path (reversible_compile(atan, Float64, Float64)) is separately broken — no Base.atan(::SoftFloat, ::SoftFloat) override exists in softfloat_dispatch.jl. That's a known gap for the whole Tier C1 family (sin/cos/tan/atan/asin/acos similarly lack Base.<f>(::SoftFloat) overrides) and should be filed separately if pursued — out of scope for atan2.
close_reason: soft_atan2 + dispatch arms for both llvm.atan2.f64 (LLVM 18+) and libm @atan2 external call landed. Faithful musl atan2.c port built on soft_atan (Bennett-qpke) per CLAUDE.md §12: ONE soft_fdiv(|y|, |x|) + ONE soft_atan(ratio) + ONE soft_fsub for quadrant offset; everything else is XOR / ifelse. ≤2 ULP vs Base.atan(y, x) on 100k random × 3 seeds × 4 magnitude buckets × 4 quadrants. All special cases bit-exact (axis points; ±0/±0; ±Inf finite; finite ±Inf; ±Inf/±Inf returns ±π/4 or ±3π/4; NaN propagation with first-NaN-wins semantics matching Base.atan and x86 SSE). Subnormal-output sweep per CLAUDE.md §13: input binades 2^-1075..2^-50 covering the q0/q1 subnormal-output regime (atan(|y/x|) ≈ |y/x|), max ULP ≤ 1. _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 18 → 19. f32 forms (llvm.atan2.f32 + libm @atan2f) rejected per §13. 138 primitive testset assertions + 32 dispatch assertions all green. **Drive-by fix to instructions.jl**: tightened all six trig prefixes (llvm.{sin,cos,tan,atan,asin,acos}.) with trailing '.' to fix the silent miscompile that pre-7goc had — startswith('llvm.atan') matched 'llvm.atan2.f64' and dispatched to soft_atan(y) dropping x; atan2(3,4) returned atan(3) ≈ 1.249 instead of 0.6435. Same class of bug pre-empted for sinh/cosh/tanh/asinh/acosh/atanh. **Investigation finding (not in scope, filed as followup)**: Julia Base.atan(y, x) does NOT lower to llvm.atan2.f64 NOR libm @atan2 — it inlines into Julia primitives + recursive 1-arg j_atan call. Same pattern across the entire Tier C1 family means the SoftFloat-typed Julia-source path (reversible_compile(<trig>, Float64[, Float64])) is broken because no Base.<f>(::SoftFloat) overrides exist for the trig functions. Out of scope for this bead. Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated (5 of 11 done). Pkg.test: 489508 / 489510 green; the 2 remaining failures are pre-existing — Bennett-k31q (cond_pair llvm.memset.p0.i64 allowlist gap, P3, OPEN, pre-dates 7goc) and one related broken-tag.

title: soft_acos + llvm.acos dispatch (Tier C1.4 trig completion)
description: Mirror the Bennett-ckvj (asin) playbook for acos. Port musl acos.c (FreeBSD/SunPro 1993, BSD-licensed) to src/softfloat/facos.jl as soft_acos — branchless, all paths computed via ifelse-cascade. Register in _CALLEES_FP_TRANS. Add llvm.acos.f64 dispatch arm in src/extract/instructions.jl. f32 forms rejected per CLAUDE.md §13. Tier C1.4 in the Enzyme parity north-star, follow-on to Bennett-ckvj (asin).
design: REUSE the rational R(z) and constants pS0..pS5/qS1..qS4/pio2_hi/pio2_lo defined module-private in fasin.jl (Bennett-ckvj). Both files live inside module SoftFloatLib so the helper and constants are visible by name without re-import. Per CLAUDE.md §12 no-duplicated-lowering. Four sign-and-magnitude regimes (musl acos.c branch shape): (a) |x| < 2^-57 → return pio2_hi (tiny override); (b) |x| < 0.5 → pio2_hi - (x - (pio2_lo - x·R(x²))); (c) x ≤ -0.5 (sign==1, |x|≥0.5) → 2·(pio2_hi - (s + w)) where z = (1+x)/2, s = sqrt(z), w = R(z)·s - pio2_lo; (d) x ≥ 0.5 (sign==0, |x|≥0.5) → 2·(df + w) where z = (1-x)/2, s = sqrt(z), df = high32(s), c = (z - df²)/(s+df), w = R(z)·s + c. Specials: x = 1 → 0; x = -1 → π; |x| > 1 or NaN → QNAN. _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 17 → 18.
notes: Acceptance criteria: (1) src/softfloat/facos.jl implements soft_acos with ≤2 ULP vs Base.acos on 100k random sweep across [-1,1] with 3 seeds; (2) §13 (Bennett-fnxg) subnormal-output convention — acos's range is [0, π] so it never produces subnormals; the test must include a documented binade sweep across the input range demonstrating no subnormal-output regime exists for acos. This satisfies the §13 contract by exhaustive coverage of the regime where subnormals could in principle occur (input near +1, output near 0); (3) llvm.acos.f64 dispatch end-to-end fixture green; (4) f32/f16 rejected; (5) Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated.
close_reason: soft_acos + llvm.acos.f64 dispatch landed. Faithful musl acos.c port (FreeBSD/Sun 1993, BSD); branchless 4-regime dispatch (tiny / small / pos-large / neg-large). REUSES the rational _asin_R(z) helper + 10 polynomial coefficients (pS0..pS5, qS1..qS4) + pio2_hi/pio2_lo defined module-private in fasin.jl per CLAUDE.md §12 — both files live inside module SoftFloatLib so the helper is visible by name without import/using. ≤2 ULP vs Base.acos on 100k random samples × 3 seeds; 1075-input subnormal-INPUT binade sweep + 52-input near-1 sweep both green; subnormal-OUTPUT proven absent across all input regimes (acos's range [0, π] never reaches subnormal). LLVM-ingest .ll roundtrip green for 14 typical + 5 special inputs. f32 rejected per §13. _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 17→18. test_kmuj counts updated 17→18 + total 78→79. Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated (4 of 11 done). Five gotchas captured incl. (a) z formula unifies across pos-large/neg-large via |x|, (b) acos(±0) = π/2 NOT ±0 unlike asin (different tiny-path semantic), (c) x=1 triggers 0/0 division in mid-path, masked by override ordering, (d) §13 contract met by demonstrated absence of subnormal-output regime, (e) Base.acos throws OOB like asin (random sweep needs split). Closes Tier C1.4.

title: soft_asin + llvm.asin dispatch (Tier C1.3 trig completion)
description: Mirror the Bennett-qpke (atan) playbook for asin. Port musl asin.c (FreeBSD/SunPro 1993, BSD-licensed; identical in glibc and Julia-via-openlibm) to src/softfloat/fasin.jl as soft_asin — branchless, all paths computed via ifelse-cascade like soft_atan. Register in _CALLEES_FP_TRANS. Add llvm.asin.f64 dispatch arm in src/extract/instructions.jl mirroring the qpke arm. f32 forms rejected per CLAUDE.md §13 (Bennett-3rph). Tier C1.3 in the Enzyme parity north-star, follow-on to Bennett-qpke (atan).
design: Reuse soft_fsqrt (Bennett-ux2 ✓). Define the rational R(z) helper (pS0..pS5, qS1..qS4) plus pio2_hi/pio2_lo as module-private constants in fasin.jl — these are SHARED with acos (Tier C1.4) per CLAUDE.md §12 no-duplicated-lowering. Three input regimes via |x|'s high word: (1) |x| < 2^-26 → return x bit-exact (tiny path matches musl); (2) |x| < 0.5 → x + x·R(x²); (3) 0.5 ≤ |x| < 1 → bifurcates again on |x| > 0.975 (uses pio2 form) vs |x| ≤ 0.975 (uses 0.5·pio2 + SET_LOW_WORD precision trick on f = high32(s)). Specials: |x| = 1 → ±π/2 bit-exact; |x| > 1 or NaN → QNAN. SLP-vectorisation trap (qpke gotcha #1): keep the structure to one R-call per regime — the polynomial body itself is fma-free fdiv at the end, no parallel-fdiv pattern. _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 16 → 17.
notes: Acceptance criteria: (1) src/softfloat/fasin.jl implements soft_asin with ≤2 ULP vs Base.asin on a 100k random sweep across [-1,1] with 3 seeds; (2) subnormal-output testset per CLAUDE.md §13 (Bennett-fnxg) — for asin, subnormal-output ⇔ subnormal-input since asin(x) ≈ x in the tiny regime; sweep binades 2^-1075 .. 2^-26 with max ULP ≤ 1; (3) llvm.asin.f64 dispatch lowers an end-to-end .ll fixture round-trip green; (4) f32/f16 rejected with precise error referencing this bead; (5) Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated to list this as closed.
close_reason: soft_asin + llvm.asin.f64 dispatch landed. Faithful musl e_asin.c port (FreeBSD/Sun 1993, BSD); branchless three-regime dispatch with single ifelse-selected R-call (avoids qpke SLP-vectorisation gotcha). The rational R(z) helper (_asin_R) and 10 polynomial coefficients + pio2_hi/pio2_lo are module-private and SHARED with soft_acos (Bennett-bd7f, Tier C1.4) per CLAUDE.md §12. ≤2 ULP vs Base.asin on 100k random samples × 3 seeds across all four input regimes (tiny/small-poly/mid/near-1); 1075-input subnormal binade sweep at 0 ULP; LLVM-ingest .ll roundtrip green for 14 inputs + 5 specials + 3000-sample OOB→NaN check. f32 rejected per §13. _CALLEES_FP_TRANS extended 16→17. test_kmuj counts updated 16→17 + total 77→78. Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md Tier C1 updated (3 of 11 done). Pkg.test post-fix: 489436 pass / 1 fail (pre-existing Bennett-k31q t0_preprocessing memset allowlist gap, unrelated).

title: Full-scale docs refresh post-Enzyme-parity pivot (drift sweep)
description:
## What

Comprehensive refresh of project-level documentation that has drifted since the 2026-05-01 → 2026-05-04 grind. Worklog chunks 047-057 landed major structural and feature changes; the public-facing docs (README.md, CLAUDE.md, BENCHMARKS.md, VISION-PRD) still describe the pre-pivot state.

## Drift inventory

**README.md:**
- Opcode coverage table (line ~67) says "38 core opcodes" — actual coverage now includes llvm.{sqrt,exp,exp2,fma,fmuladd,sin,cos,tan,log,log2,log10,pow,powi} via 1pb/h6f/3mo/s1zl/582/emv direct intrinsic dispatch + memset/memcpy fail-loud (lqif). Update count, table, and the "Missing opcodes" list (drop sqrt/sin/cos/log/exp/pow; keep frem, vector ops, pointer casts, atomics, EH, va_arg).
- "Project status" section (line ~272) frozen at "2026-04 mid" — needs the 2026-05 opcode-parity narrative + Enzyme parity north-star pointer.
- File-structure block in CLAUDE.md describes `src/lower.jl` and `src/ir_extract.jl` as monoliths; both were split (vdlg → `src/lowering/` 9-file, x3jc → `src/extract/` 9-file, zpj7 → `src/pebble/`). README architecture diagram still references the monolithic names.
- Test counts everywhere are stale (~67k assertions / 143 files quoted; actual ~487k+ pass post-2026-05-01).

**CLAUDE.md:**
- File-structure block (lines ~80-160) needs post-vdlg/x3jc/zpj7 update: `src/lowering/`, `src/extract/`, `src/pebble/` subdirs, BennettStrategy dispatch unification (i2ca), CompileOptions struct (u71l), softfloat/persistent submodules (iwv5).
- §6 was relaxed (baselines pin to explicit strategy, not :auto) per hjwp / U150 — verify the example baselines still match.
- §13 documents Float32 deviation per 3rph — verify cross-link to fpconv.jl docstring.
- Add cross-link to `Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md` in the PRD list (line 7 currently stops at v0.5).

**BENCHMARKS.md:**
- No transcendental head-to-head section yet (sqrt/exp/log/pow/sin/cos/tan all landed since the last refresh).
- `_CALLEES_FP_TRANS` count is 15 post-s1zl; pinned-count regression context not in BENCHMARKS.

**WORKLOG.md root index:**
- Index stops at chunk 046; chunks 047-057 not listed. Re-run `python3 scripts/shard_worklog.py` and verify byte-for-byte reverse-concat matches.

**Bennett-VISION-PRD.md:**
- Cross-link to `Bennett-Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.md` (filed 2026-05-03) so future agents find the active tier framework, not just the long-term v1.0 vision.

**docs/literature/SURVEY.md (if it exists):**
- Add musl tan.c / __tan.c / __sin.c / __cos.c / __rem_pio2.c / __rem_pio2_large.c references (3mo + s1zl ports). Add Cody-Waite & Payne-Hanek arg-reduction citations.

## Acceptance
