- README test count + project-status are current.
- CLAUDE.md file-structure block matches `src/lowering/`, `src/extract/`, `src/pebble/`, `src/softfloat/`, `src/persistent/` reality.
- BENCHMARKS.md has a transcendental section.

- VISION-PRD links to Enzyme-Parity-NorthStar.

## Out of scope

description: Port musl tan.c + __tan.c to src/softfloat/ftan.jl as soft_tan; register in _CALLEES_FP_TRANS; add llvm.tan dispatch arm in src/extract/instructions.jl. Mirrors the Bennett-3mo (sin/cos) playbook: musl-derived branchless port reusing __rem_pio2 / __rem_pio2_large infrastructure already in fsin.jl, with full Payne-Hanek argument reduction. f32 rejected per CLAUDE.md §13. title: Extract `[N x i8]` ArrayType allocas as IRAlloca(elem_w=8, n_elems=N)
description:
Sub-bead of Bennett-8bys (catch-all under Bennett-hao parent epic).

# Context
`src/extract/instructions.jl:1055-1068` rejects all non-IntegerType allocas with `elem_ty isa LLVM.IntegerType || return nothing`. Empirical scan of `build/t5_tr2_hashmap.ll` (the canonical Rust corpus): ALL 60 memcpy sites and ALL allocas in the file use `alloca [N x i8]` (ArrayType wrapping i8). Without extending the alloca extractor to accept `[N x i8]`, the post-Bennett-37mt / post-Bennett-9nwt landing doesn't move any t5 corpus needle — the allocas are silently dropped before memcpy/memset lowering can use them.

This is the SMALLEST scope and HIGHEST impact bead in the Bennett-8bys split.

# Scope
For `alloca [N x i8]` (LLVM ArrayType element type, i8 inner):
- Map to `IRAlloca(dest, elem_w=8, n_elems=iconst(N))` — same shape as `alloca i8, i32 N`.
- Other ArrayType element widths (e.g. `[N x i16]`, `[N x i64]`) stay rejected — defer to Bennett-8bys wider-element sub-bead.
- Other ArrayType inner types (struct, ptr) stay rejected.

# Design
Touch: `src/extract/instructions.jl:1055-1068` (alloca dispatch arm). The current code structure:
```julia
if opc == LLVM.API.LLVMAlloca
    elem_ty = LLVM.LLVMType(LLVM.API.LLVMGetAllocatedType(inst.ref))
    elem_ty isa LLVM.IntegerType || return nothing
    elem_w = LLVM.width(elem_ty)
    ...
end
```
Extend to accept ArrayType-of-i8: if `elem_ty isa LLVM.ArrayType` and `LLVM.eltype(elem_ty) isa LLVM.IntegerType` with width 8, set `elem_w = 8` and `n_elems = LLVM.length(elem_ty)`. Keep the existing IntegerType arm.

3+1 per CLAUDE.md §2 (instructions.jl is §2-core).

# Dependencies
None — independent of other Bennett-8bys sub-beads.

# Acceptance
- Synthetic fixture: `alloca [8 x i8]` + memcpy (or store/load) compiles end-to-end via existing Bennett-37mt path.
- t5_tr2_hashmap.ll lines 158/159 (24/32/48-byte memcpys between `[N x i8]` allocas) compile end-to-end.
- Existing test_lower_store_alloca.jl / test_store_alloca_extract.jl tests still pass (they use `alloca i8, i32 N` syntax which is unaffected).
- 3+1 protocol per §2.
close_reason: Sub-bead 1 of Bennett-8bys split shipped. Extended _alloca_elem_width_bits + alloca dispatch in src/extract/instructions.jl (~30 LOC) to recognise [K x i8] ArrayType allocas as IRAlloca(elem_w=8, n_elems=K). Wider inner widths and nested ArrayType still defer to Bennett-ixiz / future follow-ups. 61 testset asserts (test_munq_arr_i8_alloca.jl). Targeted regression sweep: 1186 asserts across 10 test files all green. 3+1 deviation explicitly documented in worklog/056 (mechanical extension; corpus survey catalogued the design space). Unblocks all 60 t5_tr2_hashmap.ll memcpy sites for the existing 37mt/9nwt paths (subject to other 8bys sub-beads landing for non-fresh dst, globals, etc.).

title: soft_pow: tighten to 100% bit-exact vs Base.^
description:
## Why this issue exists

The Bennett-emv close shipped soft_pow with 99.17% bit-exactness vs Julia's Base.^ across 10k random samples and 0% above 2 ULP across 13k extreme bivariate samples. The 0.83% one-ULP residual comes from algorithmic divergence between Bennett's Arm Optimized Routines / musl pow.c port and Julia's Base.^ implementation, which uses a different polynomial / precision tradeoff at the boundary.

This is the same pattern as Bennett-ys0d / U134 for exp: soft_exp is musl-bit-exact (~0.9% disagreement vs Base.exp), and a soft_exp_julia variant was added to match Base.exp bit-exactly. soft_log shipped 100% bit-exact in this same workstream, setting the bar for soft_pow.

## What needs to be done

Two acceptable paths:

### Path A: `soft_pow_julia` variant (matches Bennett-ys0d precedent)
- Add `soft_pow_julia` that calls Julia's `Base.^` algorithm bit-exactly. Likely involves porting Julia's `base/special/log.jl` + `base/special/exp.jl` chain rather than musl's pow.c.
- Wire `Base.:^(::SoftFloat, ::SoftFloat)` to soft_pow_julia (mirroring how Base.exp routes to soft_exp_julia per Bennett.jl line ~413).
- Keep existing soft_pow (musl-tracking) as the default for raw .ll/.bc ingest.
- Add `_CALLEES_FP_TRANS` entry; bump kmuj count.
- Test: 100% bit-exact vs Base.^ on 100k samples.

### Path B: correctly-rounded soft_pow (CORE-MATH style)
- Replace soft_pow body with a Ziv two-step strategy: fast path produces ~2^-72 relative error in double-double; slow path falls back to triple-double (~150 bits) for inputs that fall on a rounding boundary.
- Reference: Sibidanov-Zimmermann ARITH-2022, "Towards a correctly-rounded and fast power function in binary64".
- 0.5 ULP guaranteed for ALL inputs (correctly-rounded) — bit-exact vs both Julia AND musl/glibc since both are within 0.54 ULP and correct rounding is unique.
- Significantly more code than path A (CORE-MATH is ~1500 LOC for pow alone) and adds a dependency on triple-double primitives Bennett doesn't have yet.

## Acceptance criteria

- 100% bit-exact vs Base.^ across the 10k random sweep currently in `test/test_softfpow.jl` (`full-range random sweep — 10k samples` testset).
- 100% bit-exact vs Base.^ at the §13 bivariate subnormal-output boundary cases (the testsets currently use ccall as the reference because of the algorithmic divergence — if path A or B succeeds, those reference comparisons can switch back to Base.^).
- Existing 13k extreme bivariate sweep continues to pass.
- The `documented divergence: Julia's Base.^ ≠ musl pow at boundary` testset (which currently asserts 1 ≤ |Bennett vs Julia| ≤ 100) gets removed or its semantics flipped.

## Recommendation

Path A is the lower-risk implementation. Mirror what Bennett-ys0d did for exp: ship `soft_pow_julia` alongside `soft_pow`, default Base.:^ dispatch to the julia variant, keep musl-tracking variant for raw-LLVM ingest where the user already opted out of Julia frontend.

Path B is the more rigorous answer — correctly-rounded pow is a research-quality result and would be Bennett's first correctly-rounded transcendental — but it's a multi-week effort and depends on triple-double primitives that don't exist yet.

## Pointers

- src/softfloat/fpow.jl (current musl-tracking implementation)
- src/softfloat/fexp_julia.jl (precedent: Base.exp-bit-exact variant)
- worklog/054_2026-05-02_soft_log.md (Bennett-emv close session log; "Julia ≠ musl boundary" gotcha)
- test/test_softfpow.jl (existing accuracy contract tests)
- Bennett-ys0d / U134 (the matching exp/exp_julia split)
close_reason: Path A complete: soft_pow_julia bit-exact vs Base.:^ on 300k random samples; Base.:^(::SoftFloat,::SoftFloat) routes to it; soft_pow musl-tracking variant retained for llvm.pow.f64 raw .ll/.bc dispatch. See worklog/054 top entry.

title: bennett-irswap: IRSwap primitive (Hermes-style swap-mutate-swap)
description:
Architectural follow-on to Bennett-dnh. The Hermes/Janus reversible-programming-language tradition (Mogensen RC 2020 / SCP 2022, Yokoyama-Glück PEPM 2007) settled on swap-based update for indexed mutable memory:

  arr[i] := f(arr[i], v)   ==>   swap(arr, i, tmp); tmp := f(tmp, v); swap(arr, i, tmp)

Both swaps are self-inverse, the inner block sees a fresh wire, and bennett(lr) round-trips the whole composition with zero residue. This is the cleanest match for Bennett's discipline (CLAUDE.md §1).

Concrete plan:
  - src/ir_types.jl: struct IRSwap <: IRInst {dest, arr, idx, tmp, elem_width, n_elems}
  - src/lowering/memory.jl: lower_swap! emits index-decoder Toffoli ladder
  - src/bennett_transform.jl: recognise IRSwap as self-inverse for the _bennett_default short-circuit (mirrors Bennett-egu6 / U03)
  - Source-level expression: register Bennett.swap_at!(arr, i, tmp) as a callee (mirrors softfloat_dispatch.jl)
  - Tests: swap is self-inverse, two swaps = identity, swap-mutate-swap leaves arr modified and tmp at zero, bennett(lr) round-trips

Touches ir_types.jl + lower.jl + bennett_transform.jl => 3+1 protocol per CLAUDE.md §2 (two independent Plan proposers, implementer, reviewer).

Not on Bennett-dnh's critical path; Bennett-dnh closes after dnh-shapes + dnh-multi-origin. This bead is the substrate for bennett-qroam.
acceptance_criteria: IRSwap is its own inverse (verify_reversibility); two consecutive swaps return to identity; swap-mutate-swap pattern compiles end-to-end and bennett(lr) round-trips with zero ancilla residue.

title: cf @ max_n=64 compile-time regressed 33× (36s → 1213s) since 2026-04-20
description:
Found by Bennett-1xub during the persistent-DS sweep refresh.

The cf (Conchon-Filliâtre semi-persistent) demo at max_n=64 compiled in 36s on 2026-04-20 (commit 992b70a) and produced 17.4M gates. At HEAD (post-many cc0/U-series fixes, 2026-04-27), the same cell compiles in 1213.5s (33.7× slower) and produces 6.2M gates (2.8× fewer). Other cells got FASTER (ls 4: 28.8s → 3.1s; cf 4: 30.4s → 3.3s; cf 16: 31.1s → 5.1s) so the regression is specific to cf at large max_n.

Symptom shape:
  cf max_n=4:  30.4s  →  3.3s   (9.3× faster)
  cf max_n=16: 31.1s  →  5.1s   (6.1× faster)
  cf max_n=64: 36.0s  →1213.5s  (33.7× SLOWER)
  ls max_n=4..1000:    all faster

Hypothesis: a phi/select resolution path that was cheap pre-cc0 series is now O(max_n²) or worse on cf's diff-bookkeeping pattern. cf's structure has variable-depth Diff writes that branch on a per-call diff_depth — this is exactly the kind of dynamic-indexing pattern Bennett.jl was already known to handle quadratically (see benchmark/sweep_persistent_summary.md 'CF: O(max_n²) total'). What's new is that the COMPILE-time, not just gate count, has become super-linear.

Sites: src/lower.jl phi/select resolution, src/ir_extract.jl, src/persistent/cf_semi_persistent.jl.

Action: bisect 992b70a..HEAD on the cf max_n=64 cell, identify the compile-time inflection commit, then triage. Probably a phi-resolution or fold-constants regression; could correlate with one of the recent cc0 InsertElement / pointer-typed phi commits (cc0.7, tzb7) if those introduced O(N) work per phi.

Workload: julia --project=. benchmark/sweep_cell.jl cf 64 /tmp/scratch.jsonl (on a clean checkout per commit-under-test).

Filed by Bennett-1xub as out-of-scope for the BENCHMARKS.md refresh.
notes:
2026-04-27 bisect (Bennett-1xub follow-up, partial — interrupted by user request to switch focus):

| Commit | Position | Compile time @ cf max_n=64 |
|---|---|---|
| 0ce4000 (Bennett-6t8s)  | line 91 of 182  | 15.7 s  — FAST |
| 9539437 (worklog doh6)  | line 137 of 182 | 31.3 s  — FAST |
| e4bb6cd (Bennett-5qrn)  | line 138 of 182 | INTERRUPTED |
| db52e1c (Bennett-qcso)  | line 141 of 182 | UNTESTED (pure new src/compose.jl, not on hot path; unlikely cause) |
| e8b7127 (Bennett-zmw3)  | line 144 of 182 | >250 s — SLOW |
| f68b353 (Bennett-jepw)  | line 154 of 182 | >250 s — SLOW |
| fd4bd88 (Bennett-y986)  | line 168 of 182 | >250 s — SLOW |
| HEAD (aec5788)          | line 182 of 182 | 1213 s — SLOW (today's 1xub measurement) |

Regression appeared between 9539437 (FAST) and e8b7127 (SLOW). Two suspect commits in that window:

1. **e4bb6cd — Bennett-5qrn / U57: trivial-identity peephole.** Adds 121 LOC to src/lower.jl. Touches the lower_binop! hot path with a per-binop syntactic check (_try_identity_peephole!). Today's 1xub measurement showed the peephole adds <1s to small cells, but at scale (cf 64 has ~6M binops worth of SSA materialization) the cumulative per-binop cost could be super-linear. Test required: stub the peephole and rerun.

2. **e8b7127 — Bennett-zmw3 / U111: shift bounds + resolve! mask robustness.** Adds 47 LOC to src/lower.jl. Bead notes "non-algorithmic defensive change (assertions + mask cleanup), no pipeline BEHAVIOUR shift" — but compile-time wasn't measured. Touches `resolve!` which is called for every SSA materialization. STRONG CANDIDATE — defensive asserts in a hot loop are a classic O(N) → O(N²) failure mode at scale.

Methodology: `git checkout <commit> -- src benchmark`, then `timeout N julia --project=. benchmark/sweep_cell.jl cf 64 /tmp/bisect.jsonl`, watching for `[compile] done in <X> s`. Anything >100s is "regressed" for this purpose. The harness benchmark/sweep_cell.jl + benchmark/sweep_persistent_impls_gen.jl is unchanged across the bisect window — `git log 992b70a..HEAD -- benchmark/sweep_cell.jl benchmark/sweep_persistent_impls_gen.jl` returns nothing.

Next agent: complete the bisect by testing e4bb6cd alone. If FAST → regression is at e8b7127 (zmw3). If SLOW → regression is at e4bb6cd (5qrn). Then dig into the implicated commit's lower.jl additions to find the O(N²) hot-path scan. Don't bisect via timeout-kill alone — subprocess stdout flush survives kill, so the printed `[compile] done in X s` line does NOT print before kill, but elapsed wall time can be approximated by piping to ts(1) or measuring outer `time`.

DON'T run cf 64 in a tight loop; each slow run is 5-20 minutes. Use the 250s timeout heuristic to discriminate fast/slow.

Filed by Bennett-1xub. Bisect interrupted by user; regression window confirmed but final commit not yet identified.
close_reason: Root cause: sizehint!(gates, length(gates)+W) defeats push!'s amortized geometric growth (Julia 1.12 default shrink=true). 3 calls removed in src/lower.jl peephole helpers. cf max_n=64: 1213s → 42s (29× speedup). Gate counts bit-identical (6,206,464). All 84,620 tests pass.

title: test_karatsuba.jl Int8 exhaustive: 502 < 144 — Karatsuba slower than schoolbook on Int8
description: test/test_karatsuba.jl:30 asserts `gc_karat.Toffoli < gc_school.Toffoli` for Int8 multiplication; current measurement is 502 Toffoli (Karatsuba) vs 144 (schoolbook). Karatsuba is supposed to be CHEAPER for narrow widths because of the 3-vs-4 multiplications-of-half-width optimisation, but for Int8 the half-width muls each compile to themselves (recursion bottoms out at 4-bit?) plus per-recursion overhead, making it strictly worse. Pre-existing failure; uncovered 2026-04-25 evening when investigating Bennett-ca0i because the .git/hooks/pre-push wasn't installed (separate bead) so Pkg.test had been silently broken. Fix options: (a) raise the Karatsuba crossover threshold so it falls back to schoolbook below e.g. Int16; (b) tighten the Karatsuba recursion / cleanup; (c) downgrade the assertion from < to <= and document Int8 as the no-win width; (d) drop the assertion and rely on the Pareto-front benchmark file. Current owner: tobias.
close_reason: Shipped (81ed193): dropped the false 'Karatsuba<schoolbook' assertion in test_karatsuba.jl (442/442 green now); pinned the measured Toffoli table inline. Corrected the multiplier.jl docstring's false 'W=64 wins ~4×' / 'W ≥ 128 dominates' claims with the actual measurement (Karatsuba is 1.9-3.5× WORSE at every supported width, ratio decreasing with W consistent with O(W^log₂3) vs O(W²) but never crossing 1 below the unsupported W=128 ceiling). Deeper question (salvage or remove the implementation) filed as Bennett-tbm6 P3 follow-up.

title: U05-followup: lower_loop! body blocks need per-block path predicates for diamond-in-body
description:
Follow-up to Bennett-httg (U05, in progress). The MVP fix for lower_loop!
handles LINEAR multi-block bodies (one body block between header and
latch) via canonical _lower_inst! dispatch. Diamond-in-body patterns
— `a = (x > 0) ? a + x : a - x` inside the while loop — need per-block
path predicates computed in step (b) of the unroller so body-block phis
can resolve via lower_phi! + resolve_phi_predicated!.

The blocker: _compute_block_pred!(ctx, blabel, preds, branch_info, block_pred)
at src/lower.jl:1004 expects branch_info to be populated with the
predicate-carrying branch that targets blabel. For body blocks reached
from the header, that's the header's conditional branch (e.g. i<n), but
the header's branch_info entry isn't populated until step (c) of my
unroller (where we compute exit_cond_wire). Classic
chicken-and-egg.

Possible fixes (proposer-B-style):

  (a) Compute header-branch exit_cond at the START of each iteration
      (before body blocks), populate branch_info[hlabel], then proceed.
      Requires reorganizing the flow in lower_loop! and verifying the
      MUX-freeze still sees the right semantics.

  (b) For each body block, synthesize a fresh predicate wire equal to
      block_pred[hlabel] AND (appropriate branch cond), computed inline
      via _and_wire!. Avoids needing branch_info to be pre-populated.

  (c) Defer the problem and refuse diamond-in-body loops up front with
      a clear error, pending a deeper body-block-predicate design. Keeps
      the MVP clean.

Pre-fix: test_httg_loop_multiblock.jl:T3 (`_u05_branching_body`,
diamond inside while) is currently @test_broken. Unbreak when this
lands.

Ground truth:
  * src/lower.jl:825-920 (lower_loop! body-block loop)
  * src/lower.jl:1004 (_compute_block_pred!)
  * src/lower.jl:988-998 (lower_phi! / resolve_phi_predicated!)
  * test/test_httg_loop_multiblock.jl:T3

Catalogue: reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md U05 (partial closure).
Priority: P2 (MVP unblocks linear multi-block loops; diamonds deferred).
close_reason: Fixed via per-iteration LOCAL block_pred / branch_info / preds dicts inside lower_loop!. Approach (a) from the bead description, synthesised from the 3+1 proposers: compute the header's exit cond ONCE per iteration; seed iter_block_pred[hlabel] from the function-level dict; for each body block in topo order, compute iter_block_pred[blabel] via _compute_block_pred! against the iteration-local dicts; dispatch body-block instructions through a fresh iter_ctx so IRPhi resolves via _edge_predicate! against the local block_pred. Top-level pass skips loop_body_labels (option (ii)) — preempts the redundant top-level dispatch that would have crashed against iteration-local block_pred no longer in scope. Header-only loops (Collatz / soft_fdiv) gated on !isempty(body_block_order) → byte-identical pre-jepw path. add=:ripple preserved per Bennett-httg / U05 Cuccaro-trap mitigation. test/test_jepw_diamond_in_body.jl exercises three diamond shapes with exhaustive output-vs-Julia-oracle + verify_reversibility (168 assertions GREEN). test/test_httg_loop_multiblock.jl T3 unbroken. Full Pkg.test() 80986 pass / 2 broken (pre-existing) / 0 fail / 4m21s. Commit: f68b353.

title: U02-followup: value_eager_bennett leaks on SHA-256-style in-place arithmetic
description:
Follow-up to Bennett-rggq (U02, closed). The `_has_branching` fallback in
src/value_eager.jl catches the diamond-CFG case (≥2 `__pred_*` groups),
but the SHA-256 round function — straight-line bitwise arithmetic with
only one `__pred_top` group — ALSO fails `verify_reversibility` with
"input wire 98 changed from true to false".

That's Bennett's input-preservation invariant, raised by the
Bennett-asw2 (U01) fix. Phase-3 Kahn reverse-topo must be corrupting an
input wire somewhere; likely candidates:

  * Cuccaro adder's in-place `b += a` pattern: the GateGroup for `+`
    lists `result_wires` pointing back into an input's wire range, so
    the group's "forward" gates include mutations on a wire that Phase 3
    then tries to uncompute out of topo order.
  * SHA-256 round has ~20 GateGroups; maybe the topo linearisation of
    interleaved `soft_popcount`-style composite primitives drops a
    dep edge.

RED evidence (reproducible): test_value_eager.jl:158 SHA-256 round
currently @test_broken. Unbreak when fixed.

Ground truth to read before coding:
  * src/value_eager.jl:29-137 (Phase 3 Kahn)
  * src/lower.jl:379,389 (__pred_* producers — confirmed not the culprit)
  * src/adder.jl (Cuccaro in-place pattern)
  * test/test_value_eager.jl:126-164 (failing testset)

Fix options to evaluate:
  (a) Extend `_has_branching` to detect in-place groups (any group whose
      result_wires fall outside wire_start..wire_end).
  (b) Detect the specific SHA-256 failure mode and fall back on that too.
  (c) Fix the Kahn topo to register implicit wire-level deps on in-place
      targets.

Catalogue: reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md U02 (partial closure).
Unblocks: Phase 4 (persistent-DS EoL) keeps value_eager as a selectable
strategy.

Priority: P2 (not blocking the cascade; value_eager is opt-in and now
falls back safely on branching).
close_reason: ALREADY FIXED by U27 (Bennett-spa8, add=:auto→:ripple flip). test_value_eager.jl SHA-256 round testset passes verify_reversibility(c_eager) — 1558/1558 assertions in test_value_eager.jl green today. The test had already been upgraded from @test_broken to @test in U27's wake; this bead's reopening had stale evidence. The Cuccaro in-place adder pattern that caused the input-preservation invariant violation is no longer the default lowering path. Cleanup commit c7d1144 also removed dead Bennett._reset_names!() calls in 8 test files (U42 follow-through) so test_value_eager.jl actually runs cleanly now.

title: U59: No compose(c1, c2) / chain API
description:
Unified catalogue: U59 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #05 F3, #16 F13
Files: reviews/2026-04-21/05_integration_tests.md, 16_api_surface.md

Sites: absent

Claim: Severity HIGH (blocks Sturm `when(q) do f(x) end` integration; blocks controlled-of-composite use cases). No way to compose two compiled circuits into a third.

Fix: Design `compose(c1::ReversibleCircuit, c2::ReversibleCircuit)::ReversibleCircuit` with documented wire-aliasing semantics; export.
close_reason: Pipeline composition shipped in db52e1c. compose(c1, c2) returns a ReversibleCircuit with semantics simulate(compose(c1, c2), x) == simulate(c2, simulate(c1, x)). Algorithm: c1.gates ++ renumbered(c2.gates) ++ reverse(c1.gates) with positional aliasing (c2.input_wires[k] → c1.output_wires[k]) and wire-budget compaction (n_total = c1.n_wires + c2.n_wires - m). MVP rejects self-reversing inputs and explicit wire_map (filed as future extensions). 3+1 protocol followed: 2 proposers converged on the uncompute-c1 algorithm; synthesis took Proposer B's compaction math + Proposer A's edge-case enumeration. Pkg.test 80280/80283 (+2085 assertions). Unblocks Sturm when(qubit) do f(x) end integration per review F49/F50.

title: U57: Trivial-identity peepholes missing (x+0, x*1, x|0)
description:
Unified catalogue: U57 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #17 F3; related #04 F20
Files: reviews/2026-04-21/17_performance.md, 04_edge_cases.md

Sites: src/lower.jl `lower_add!`, `lower_mul!`, `_fold_constants` at :473-566

Claim: Severity HIGH (20-40% gate reduction on persistent-DS sweep). x+0 → 98 gates; x*1 → 692 gates (156 with fold_constants=true); fold pass misses x+0 entirely.

Fix: Peepholes on known-zero / known-one operands emit only CNOT copy-out; fix `_fold_constants` whole-operand-zero detection. Combine with DCE pre-pass for unused-dest instructions.
close_reason: Dispatcher-level peephole shipped in e4bb6cd. lower_binop! catches x+0, x*1, x|0, x&0, x&allones, x|allones, x⊕0, x⊕allones, x-0, x*0 (and commutative duals) before resolve! materialises the constant operand. Detection is syntactic on IROperand so the heavy adder/multiplier path is skipped entirely; the leaf-level lower_add!/lower_mul! are unaffected (they're called directly with wire vectors, not via lower_binop!). x*Int8(1) drops 692 → 26 gates (26.6× reduction); x+Int8(0) drops 86 → 26. Existing baselines preserved (x+1=58/114/226/450, x*3=220). 3+1 protocol followed (2 Plan-agent proposers, synthesis-driven impl). Pkg.test 78195/78198 (+5113 assertions in test_5qrn_identity_peepholes.jl). Side-effect: epwy test retargeted from x*1 to x*3 since the peephole eliminates the fold delta on x*1; x*3 still shows 3.24× fold ratio.

description:

Claim: Severity HIGH. `NOTGate`/`CNOTGate`/`ToffoliGate`/`ReversibleGate` documented public, not exported → `UndefVarError`. `ParsedIR`/`LoweringResult` return types unexported. `lower_add_cuccaro!`-class primitives in api.md unexported. Meanwhile 25+ persistent-DS research names (`AbstractPersistentMap`, `PersistentMapImpl`, 4 `*_IMPL` sentinels, 12 `*_pmap_*`, `OkasakiState`, `cf_reroot`, `pmap_demo_oracle`) and 21 soft-float primitives leak publicly with no stable ABI. `ControlledCircuit` exported with no docstring/`show`. `simulate`/`gate_count`/`ancilla_count`/`depth`/`print_circuit` missing docstrings despite being user-facing.

Fix: Export `NOTGate/CNOTGate/ToffoliGate/ReversibleGate/ParsedIR/LoweringResult`. Move persistent-DS → `Bennett.Persistent` submodule; soft-float → `Bennett.SoftFloat` submodule. Add docstrings on every exported fn. Remove primitives from api.md if keeping unexported.
close_reason:
2026-04-25 — sub-issues addressed:
1. EXPORTED 6 documented-public types: NOTGate, CNOTGate, ToffoliGate, ReversibleGate (gate primitives, docs/src/api.md:188/192/196), ParsedIR (return of extract_parsed_ir), LoweringResult (return of lower / input to bennett, api.md:139/211). Verified via names(Bennett).
2. ControlledCircuit docstring + missing simulate/gate_count/ancilla_count/depth/print_circuit docstrings — already shipped 2026-04-25 morning via Bennett-12bf / U157.
3. depth never tested — already shipped 2026-04-25 via Bennett-mlny / U63 (test_mlny_depth.jl).
4. Soft-float submodule wrap (Bennett.SoftFloat) — DEFERRED. Same rationale as Bennett-ph5m / U75: original concern was scale; current 21 soft_* exports are intentional public API; submodule wrap would force every consumer prefix-bump.
5. Persistent submodule wrap (Bennett.Persistent) — DEFERRED via Bennett-ph5m / U75 (cycle 6 audit-and-defer): persistent-related exports went 30 → 7 via U54, all 7 intentional public API.
6. 'Remove primitives from api.md if keeping unexported' — primitives ARE now exported (point 1), so api.md correctly reflects the public API.

title: U50: Project.toml version/compat drift
description:
Unified catalogue: U50 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #16 F3, #16 F4, #18 F6, #18 F7
Files: reviews/2026-04-21/16_api_surface.md, 18_julia_idioms.md

Sites: Project.toml (version 0.4.0, julia = "1.6", LLVM = "9.4.6" exact pin)

Claim: Severity HIGH. Version string stale; Julia 1.6 floor is ancient; LLVM pin is too tight; Manifest.toml committed for a library.

Fix: Bump to `0.5.0`/`0.5.0-dev`; `julia = "1.10"`; relax `LLVM = "9, 10"` and `PicoSAT = "0.4"`. Add `CHANGELOG.md`. Also: `git rm --cached Manifest.toml` (library, gitignored) — #18 F5.

Could combine with U64 (release-readiness).
notes:
2026-04-25 status: deferred from doc-work session — the four sub-scopes all require user judgment beyond doc work:

1. Version bump 0.4.0 → 0.5.0/0.5.0-dev: release decision, not mechanical.
2. julia = '1.6' → '1.10': breaking-compat change excluding 1.7-1.9 users. Per CLAUDE.md §11 (PRD-driven) needs explicit version-policy ruling.
3. LLVM = '9.4.6' exact pin → '9, 10': per CLAUDE.md §5 (LLVM IR not stable), relaxing without cross-version testing is risky. Needs a test matrix per version, not a one-line edit.
4. Add CHANGELOG.md: non-trivial content authoring (back-fill of release notes for v0.1-v0.4) — user authorship, not agent.
5. git rm --cached Manifest.toml: affects what fresh checkouts see. Library convention favours this but the user should sign off.

Bead notes 'Could combine with U64 (release-readiness)' which signals it belongs in a release sprint, not a doc-snack window. Recommend re-opening as part of a 0.5.0 release prep ticket.
close_reason: Shipped (30e94ff): Project.toml bumped 0.4.0→0.5.0; julia compat 1.6→1.10; LLVM compat 9.4.6 exact→'9, 10'; PicoSAT compat 0.4.1→'0.4'. CHANGELOG.md created with back-filled v0.1-v0.4 internal milestones + full v0.5.0 entry covering soft-float push, persistent-DS workstream, multi-language ingest, strategy variants, diagnostics, and the major fixes. Manifest.toml already untracked (item #5 was a no-op). Full Pkg.test() green under the new compat block on Julia 1.12.3 + LLVM 10. User explicitly authorised all five sub-decisions ('go ahead with all of this version changes. There are no users right now.').

title: U48: Callee IR re-extracted per call reference (no cache)
description:
Unified catalogue: U48 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #01 F10, #13 F1, #17 F15
Files: reviews/2026-04-21/01_structure_callgraph.md, 13_carmack.md, 17_performance.md

Sites: src/lower.jl:1864, 1935, 1928-1989

Claim: Severity HIGH. `lower_call!` re-runs `extract_parsed_ir` per callee reference at ~21 ms each; 10-fadd circuit pays ~200 ms redundant. `_callee_arg_types` also re-derives via `methods()`. Plus `:auto + tabulate` re-extracts IR under `reversible_compile`.

Fix: `Dict{Tuple{Function,Type{<:Tuple}},ParsedIR}` cache in `LoweringCtx` or module-scoped; pre-populate at load time via `register_callee!`.
close_reason: Module-scoped Dict{Tuple{Function,Type},ParsedIR} cache + ReentrantLock in src/ir_extract.jl. lower_call! routes through _extract_parsed_ir_cached so a circuit with N references to the same callee pays the ~21ms extract_parsed_ir cost once. Measured 1.20x speedup on a synthetic 5-fadd parent fn (cold=96ms→warm=80ms median); larger wins expected on SHA-256 / persistent-map circuits with many distinct callees. Test file test_ej4n_callee_ir_cache.jl pins 20 assertions (cache hit identity, distinct-key isolation, no-growth-on-recompile, multi-ref-collapse, simulation correctness, clear). Pkg.test green.

title: U47: Type instability in hot paths — boxed returns, abstract vectors
description:
Unified catalogue: U47 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #13 F6, #17 F7, #17 F9, #18 F11, #18 F13, #02 F10
Files: reviews/2026-04-21/13_carmack.md, 17_performance.md, 18_julia_idioms.md, 02_vision_scope.md

Sites: src/simulator.jl (simulate returns Union{Int8,...,Tuple}); _convert_instruction returns 17-arm Union + Vector; abstract Vector{ReversibleGate} boxes pointers (~56 MB overhead on 1.4M-gate circuits); src/tabulate.jl:158-168 uses Vector{Any}; _gate_controls allocates Vector{Int} per call

Claim: Severity HIGH. Multiple hot-path type-instability issues: boxed return unions, abstract-typed vectors, per-call allocations that should be tuples.

Fix: Typed `simulate(c, ::Type{T}, ...)::T` overload; split `_convert_instruction_single` vs `_convert_instruction_expand!`; `_gate_controls` return `Tuple` not `Vector{Int}`; tabulate rewrite as NTuple-parametric.

Multiple cuts, 3+1 for any storage layout change per CLAUDE.md §2.
close_reason: Investigated + partial fix shipped. Live @code_warntype on `simulate(c, x::Int8)` confirmed the bead's headline: return type is Union{Int8,Int16,Int32,Int64,UInt8,UInt16,UInt32,UInt64,Tuple} (9-arm). Shipped the typed `simulate(c, ::Type{T}, inputs)::T` overload (test/test_59jj_typed_simulate.jl, 44 assertions GREEN, @inferred-asserted concrete return type) — gives hot-loop callers a concrete return type via `raw % T` bit-pattern reinterpret. Stale subclaims documented: (1) `_gate_controls` already returns Tuple (src/dep_dag.jl:97-99); (2) tabulate.jl Vector{Any} already replaced by NTuple via Bennett-b2fs / U148. Remaining valid cuts split into follow-up beads with 3+1-protocol tags: Bennett-q04a (split _convert_instruction's 17-arm Union return into _single + _expand!) and Bennett-jc0y (ReversibleCircuit.gates abstract Vector{ReversibleGate} → tagged-union or struct-of-arrays storage). Full Pkg.test() 81198 pass / 2 broken (pre-existing) / 0 fail / 4m12s post-fix. Commit forthcoming.

title: U45: Error monoculture — 190+ error(...) all throw ErrorException
description:
Unified catalogue: U45 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #06 F12, #08 F5, #08 F7, #18 F3, #18 F4
Files: reviews/2026-04-21/06_antipatterns.md, 08_error_handling.md, 18_julia_idioms.md

Sites: whole src/; zero ArgumentError/DimensionMismatch/DomainError/AssertionError usage

Claim: Severity HIGH. 190+ error() sites all throw plain ErrorException; no discriminated error types; tests can't target specific failure categories.

Fix: Codemod: bad-arg→`ArgumentError`, wire-length→`DimensionMismatch`, invariants→`AssertionError`, IR coverage gaps→`ErrorException`. Tests tighten to `@test_throws ArgumentError`. Convert ~2 `@assert` + ~121 `error()` skew to proper split (#18 F4).

Chore-scope (like cc0.6).
notes: 2026-05-01 reconnaissance: 162 error() sites + 9 @assert across 27 files. Top: lowering/memory.jl=38, lowering/aggregate.jl=19, ir_types.jl=17, lowering/phi.jl=11, extract/helpers.jl=10. Per-site judgment required (not pure mechanical codemod) — wire/width mismatches → DimensionMismatch, unsupported features → ArgumentError, internal invariants → AssertionError, IR coverage gaps → ErrorException. Test side: 40 @test_throws ErrorException sites in test/ may need tightening; 0zsk's substring-form @test_throws are immune. Estimated ~2hrs focused work, deferred for dedicated session.

title: U43: LoweringCtx has 3 ::Any hot-path fields + 4 back-compat constructors
description:
Unified catalogue: U43 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #02 F9, #06 F7, #12 F4, #13 F3, #14 F15, #17 F5, #18 F2; also U44
Files: reviews/2026-04-21/02_vision_scope.md, 06_antipatterns.md, 12_torvalds.md, 13_carmack.md, 14_knuth.md, 17_performance.md, 18_julia_idioms.md

Sites: src/lower.jl:50-119; related ParsedIR::memssa::Any at src/ir_types.jl:180 (circular include)

Claim: Severity HIGH (type instability on every dispatch; reader-hostile). `preds::Any`, `branch_info::Any`, `block_order::Any` with "accept any dict shape" comment. 16 fields, 4 constructors (7/12/13/14-arg) accreting M2a→M3a milestones. Sentinel `entry_label = Symbol("")` guards semantic distinction in `_lower_store_via_shadow!`. `ParsedIR::memssa::Any` justified by circular dependency — fix by moving `MemSSAInfo` definition to `ir_types.jl`.

Fix: Concretise to `Dict{Symbol,Vector{Symbol}}` / `Dict{Symbol,Tuple{...}}` / `Vector{Symbol}` (they always are). Collapse to one kwarg constructor. Split `LoweringCtx` vs `MemCtx` if memory-less paths don't carry memory state. Expected 10-30% lowering speedup.

Requires 3+1 per CLAUDE.md §2.
close_reason: Three ::Any fields on LoweringCtx (preds, branch_info, block_order) tightened to documented concrete dict types: Dict{Symbol,Vector{Symbol}} / Dict{Symbol,Tuple{Vector{Int},Symbol,Symbol}} / Dict{Symbol,Int}. Pure type tightening, no new code, no caller updates needed. Other 'two halves' of the original ehoa description (back-compat constructors, ParsedIR::memssa) already closed by Bennett-tbm6 and Bennett-by8j respectively. All 419,228 tests pass. Per user 2026-05-01 directive: no 3+1 needed for type-only refactors with no new code.

title: U41: _convert_instruction is a 649-line opcode god-function
description:
Unified catalogue: U41 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md

Source reports: #01 F2, #06 F1, #12 F1; vector mirror #06 F9, #17 F9
Files: reviews/2026-04-21/01_structure_callgraph.md, 06_antipatterns.md, 12_torvalds.md, 17_performance.md

Sites: src/ir_extract.jl:1086-1734 (_convert_instruction); sibling _convert_vector_instruction at :2063-2299

Claim: Severity HIGH. 649-line opcode switch function with a sibling vector variant duplicating logic.

Fix: Dispatch table `_OPCODE_HANDLERS::Dict{Opcode,Function}` + `_INTRINSIC_HANDLERS::Dict{String,Function}`; each handler ~10-20 LOC; routing ~30 LOC. Share per-opcode helpers between scalar and vector dispatchers (or unify via lane-count trait).

Requires 3+1 per CLAUDE.md §2.
notes:
Stage 1 of 5 shipped 2026-04-27 evening (commit 904fe4d). _handle_intrinsic helper extracted (270-line LLVM-intrinsic prefix block) — first-cut of the 3+1-mandated refactor. Used the if/elseif over named helpers mechanism (Proposer B) over Dict{Opcode,Function} (Proposer A) — Dict adds hash + cmp + boxed Function values that risk regressing the 200 KiB allocation cap pinned by test_q04a_convert_instruction_contract.jl. All 84,352 assertions still pass byte-identically; 39 gate-count baselines unchanged.

Stages 2-5 deferred. The remaining arms (binop/icmp/select/phi/div_rem/cast/branch/extractvalue/insertvalue/unreachable/load/switch/freeze/fpto*/sitofp/fcmp/bitcast/fneg/store/alloca/gep) are each 6-30 LOC bodies — already AT or near the bead's '10-20 LOC handler' target. Wrapping them in named functions adds ~5 LOC signature/end overhead per arm with marginal clarity gain. Per CLAUDE.md §11, deferring until evidence accrues that the structural extraction would help (e.g. a future bug class concentrated in one arm).

Current state of _convert_instruction: 836 LOC → ~570 LOC (call arm now ~6 LOC instead of ~360, with the intrinsic block gone). Sibling _convert_vector_instruction (237 LOC) untouched in this stage; cross-dispatcher unification was rejected by both proposers as adding coupling, not reducing it.

title: T5-P6.3 ir_extract: handle thread_ptr GEP base (Julia TLS allocator)
description:
src/ir_extract.jl crashes with 'GEP base thread_ptr not found in variable wires' when parsing Julia code that uses Array{T}(undef, N) — Julia's heap allocator routes through the thread-local storage pointer (a call to julia.get_pgcstack or similar TLS intrinsic), which is then GEP'd, then alloca-d.

DISCOVERED FROM: Bennett-t61h (T5-P2a TJ4 RED test, Array{Int8}(undef, 256)).
BLOCKS: Bennett-z2dj (T5-P6 dispatcher), Bennett-w985 (T5-P2b C tests via clang — clang malloc has analogous semantics), Bennett-gl2m (T5-P2c Rust tests via rustc — Vec backing alloc same).

Reproduction:
  julia --project=. -e 'using Bennett; reversible_compile((x::Int8,i::Int8) -> let a=Array{Int8}(undef,256); a[mod(i,256)+1]=x; a[mod(i,256)+1] end, Int8, Int8)'

Fix: extend GEP-base-resolution in ir_extract.jl to recognize the TLS intrinsic call (likely julia.get_pgcstack or @jl_thread_ptr) as an opaque pointer source. Treat the resulting allocation as a fresh alloca for dispatch purposes. Acceptance: TJ4 from test_t5_corpus_julia.jl reaches _pick_alloca_strategy (where it can then dispatch to T4 shadow-checkpoint or T5 persistent — that's downstream).

Core change to ir_extract.jl per CLAUDE.md §2 — 3+1 protocol. Note: this is the most general fix — also unlocks clang malloc and rustc Vec backing.
notes:
--- 2026-04-20 sweep evidence ---

Re-triggered through a SECOND pathway during the persistent-DS scaling
sweep: @eval-defined functions (after cc0.3 fires first) emit LLVM IR
that hits the same TLS-allocator pattern downstream. So cc0.5 is broader
than originally scoped — it's not just Array{T}(undef, N), it's any
Julia-runtime-allocated function whose extraction reaches the GEP base
walker.

Forms a CHAIN with cc0.3: cc0.3 fires first on the @eval pathway; even
after cc0.3 is fixed, cc0.5 will still fire on the same code. Both must
be fixed in sequence to unblock metaprogrammed impl generation
(benchmark/codegen tooling, etc.).
close_reason: superseded by Bennett-gf3n; thread_ptr-GEP premise disproven by the 2026-05-21 heap de-risking spike — the actual wall is an inline-asm TLS read, not a GEP — see worklog/072

title: T5-P6.2 ir_extract: handle constant-pointer icmp eq
description:
src/ir_extract.jl crashes with 'Unknown operand ref for: i1 icmp eq (ptr @<name>, ptr @<name>)' when parsing Julia code with isnothing() on a Union{T,Nothing} field — LLVM emits a comparison between two constant pointer expressions (icmp eq with constant ptr operands).

DISCOVERED FROM: Bennett-t61h (T5-P2a TJ3 RED test, 3-node linked list with isnothing traversal).
BLOCKS: Bennett-z2dj (T5-P6 dispatcher) and Bennett-mcgk (T5-P3b Okasaki — RBT also uses Union-typed leaves).

Reproduction:
  mutable struct N; v::Int8; nxt::Union{N,Nothing}; end
  julia --project=. -e 'using Bennett; reversible_compile(x::Int8 -> let n=N(x,nothing); n.nxt===nothing end, Int8)'

Fix: extend the constant-operand handler in ir_extract.jl to recognize ConstantExpr with kind LLVMConstantPointerEq (or whatever LLVM.jl exposes — verify via lean_hover_info or LLVM.jl source). Two constant pointers are equal iff they refer to the same allocation site; under reversible compilation they reduce to a constant boolean. Acceptance: TJ3 from test_t5_corpus_julia.jl no longer errors at extraction.

Core change to ir_extract.jl per CLAUDE.md §2 — 3+1 protocol.
close_reason: Implemented in ir_extract.jl via _fold_constexpr_operand + _ptr_identity. TJ3 flipped RED→GREEN (180 gates, 44 Toffoli) at test/test_t5_corpus_julia.jl. New RED→GREEN gate test/test_cc04_repro.jl (256 exhaustive inputs + verify_reversibility). Full Pkg.test() GREEN, all WORKLOG baselines byte-identical (soft_fptrunc 36,474 / popcount32 2,782 / HAMT 96,788 / CF 11,078 / CF+Feistel 65,198). 3+1 protocol: docs/design/cc04_proposer_A.md + cc04_proposer_B.md + cc04_consensus.md. See WORKLOG.md 2026-04-21 session log.

title: T5-P6.1 ir_extract: handle LLVMGlobalAliasValueKind
description:
src/ir_extract.jl crashes with 'Unknown value kind LLVMGlobalAliasValueKind' when parsing Julia code that uses Vector{T}() or Dict{K,V}() — Julia represents these via LLVM global aliases.

DISCOVERED FROM: Bennett-t61h (T5-P2a TJ1/TJ2 RED tests).
BLOCKS: Bennett-z2dj (T5-P6 dispatcher).

Reproduction:
  julia --project=. -e 'using Bennett; reversible_compile(x::Int8 -> let v = Int8[]; push!(v,x); reduce(+,v) end, Int8)'

Fix: extend the LLVMValueKind dispatch in ir_extract.jl to handle GlobalAlias by following the alias to its target (LLVM.jl: aliasee()). Then dispatch on the target's kind. Acceptance: TJ1 + TJ2 from test_t5_corpus_julia.jl no longer error at extraction (they may still fail at lowering — that's downstream T5-P6 work, separate bead).

Core change to ir_extract.jl per CLAUDE.md §2 — 3+1 protocol.
notes:
--- 2026-04-20 sweep evidence ---

Re-triggered by a NEW pathway during the persistent-DS scaling sweep:
@eval-defined functions (dynamic function definition at runtime) emit
LLVM IR with global-alias references. So this bead now blocks not just
Vector/Dict patterns but ALSO any "generate impls programmatically"
tooling — relevant for benchmark/codegen/metaprogramming workflows.

The sweep had to fall back to PRE-WRITTEN concrete source files
(benchmark/codegen_sweep_impls.jl emits Julia source to disk, then
includes it) instead of generating impl variants at runtime. A clean
fix here would unblock that simpler pathway.

The sweep also showed cc0.3 forms a CHAIN with cc0.5: @eval-defined
functions hit cc0.3 first; if cc0.3 is fixed, cc0.5 fires next. Both
need to be fixed for metaprogrammed pathways to work.
close_reason: Closed

title: T5-P0g Acquire HAMT insert/delete reference (Clojure or Steindorfer-Vinju 2015)
description:
Acquire reference implementation(s) for HAMT insert and delete — neither algorithm appears in the Bagwell 2001 nor 2000 PDFs (only prose descriptions). Two preferred sources:

1. Clojure PersistentHashMap (free, no auth): https://github.com/clojure/clojure/blob/master/src/jvm/clojure/lang/PersistentHashMap.java — save the .java file to docs/literature/memory/Clojure_PersistentHashMap.java
2. Steindorfer & Vinju 2015 'Optimizing Hash-Array Mapped Tries for Fast and Lean Immutable JVM Collections' OOPSLA — Springer/ACM paywall, use TIB. Save to docs/literature/memory/SteindorferVinju2015_HAMT.pdf

Brief: docs/literature/memory/hamt_insert_delete_brief.md containing: (1) verbatim insert algorithm from chosen source, (2) verbatim delete algorithm, (3) collision-list handling at the leaf level, (4) reversibility prediction.

BLOCKS: Bennett-a7zy (T5-P3c HAMT impl) — that bead cannot complete insert/delete without this. Discovered during Phase 0 brief writing.

Subagent: sonnet. Clojure source is free; Steindorfer-Vinju needs Playwright if pursued. Either is acceptable; prefer Clojure for simplicity. Acceptance: PDF/source file + brief with verbatim insert+delete.
close_reason: Clojure source + brief landed; insert/delete verbatim from commit 56d37996b18df811c20f391c840e7fd26ed2f58d (2022-06-09). BitmapIndexedNode.assoc/without, HashCollisionNode.assoc/without, ArrayNode.pack, bitpos/mask/index helpers all extracted verbatim. Reversibility analysis flagged bitmap-shrink as key challenge; recommendation is ancilla-preservation (option a).

title: T5-P0f Acquire Kahrs 2001 RBT delete (JFP 11(4)) PDF + brief
description:
Acquire Kahrs 2001 'Red-black trees with types' JFP 11(4):425-432 — needed because the Okasaki 1999 paper does NOT contain the delete algorithm. Cambridge University Press / JFP Springer paywall, use TIB network via FQHE Playwright pattern (../FQHE/scripts/fetch_aps_papers.mjs and ../FQHE/node_modules/playwright with persistent profile). DOI 10.1017/S0956796801004026.

Save as: docs/literature/memory/Kahrs2001_rbt_delete.pdf
Brief: docs/literature/memory/kahrs_rbt_delete_brief.md (verbatim delete algorithm, balance-on-delete cases, reversibility prediction).

title: T5-P0c Acquire Conchon-Filliâtre 2007 PUF PDF + research brief

Save to docs/literature/memory/ConchonFilliatre2007_PUF.pdf. Write docs/literature/memory/cf_semipersistent_brief.md containing: (1) the version-tree construction, (2) the rerooting algorithm and why it gives semi-persistence (only ancestors of latest version remain accessible), (3) why this matches reversibility's linearity model, (4) gate-cost prediction: rebase cost is O(d) where d = distance to current root, amortizes to O(1) under sequential access. Acceptance: PDF + brief with verbatim rerooting pseudocode.

Subagent: sonnet. Free LRI download — straight curl.
close_reason: Brief written at docs/literature/memory/cf_semipersistent_brief.md; PDF at docs/literature/memory/ConchonFilliatre2007_PUF.pdf. Rerooting algorithm reproduced verbatim from p.4. Key insight documented: semi-persistent array IS Bennett's tape -- the Diff chain records history for uncompute, reroot IS the uncompute step.

title: T5-P3d Track C: Conchon-Filliâtre semi-persistent
description:
Implement Conchon-Filliâtre semi-persistent array at src/persistent/cf_semi_persistent.jl conforming to T5-P3a interface. Version-tree with rerooting on access. The semi-persistence (only ancestors of latest version addressable) is a NATURAL fit for reversibility: Bennett's tape walks the version chain in exactly one direction, never branches. Must support insert, lookup, delete. Measure gate count.

Subagent: sonnet drafts. Orchestrator reviews TIGHTLY — the rerooting algorithm rewrites pointers in place (semantically) and the reversibility argument depends on never having two live versions reachable simultaneously. Need to verify this invariant is preserved by Bennett's forward+copy+reverse. Reference: docs/literature/memory/cf_semipersistent_brief.md (from T5-P0c). Acceptance: file exists, rerooting invariant documented and tested, full verify_reversibility, gate-count table in WORKLOG.md.

title: T5-P4a Mogensen reversible hash-cons (naive table)
description:
Implement Mogensen 2018 reversible hash-cons table at src/persistent/hashcons_naive.jl. The hash TABLE itself must be reversible — this is the novel piece. Approach: open-addressing table with reversible insert. Hash function: Jenkins 96-bit reversible mix from Mogensen brief (T5-P0d). Insert(k,v) checks if (k,v) already present; if so returns existing slot id; if not allocates new slot. Reversibility: the slot-allocation order IS the version chain. Layered ON TOP OF each of the 3 DS implementations (P3b/c/d) → 3 combinations measured.

Implementer: tobias (orchestrator) — this is the novel part of T5, NOT delegated to subagent. Reference: docs/literature/memory/mogensen_hashcons_brief.md. MVP T4: add :shadow_checkpoint dispatcher arm for static-sized allocations where n*elem_w > 64 (currently rejected). Fans out N guarded shadow stores per dynamic-idx store, reusing emit_shadow_store_guarded! (M2c). Load mirrors M2b multi-origin pattern over slot axis. Acceptance: L10 GREEN (Array{Int8}(undef, 256) dynamic idx), plus new L7g RED-first (diamond CFG × T4 store) to pin false-path sensitization. Design: docs/design/m3a_consensus.md. Fix spans: (1) ir_extract.jl — accept pointer-typed phi/select operands; (2) lower.jl — multi-origin ptr_provenance; emit controlled stores/loads to each possible underlying alloca (disambiguated via MemSSA def-use or path predicate). CORE CHANGE per CLAUDE.md §2 → 3+1 agents. Test pins: test/test_memory_corpus.jl L7c, L7d. Parent: Bennett-cc0 (M2b of the memory epic).
close_reason: Closed

title: Bennett-cc0 M2d: path-predicate guarding for conditional MUX-stores
description: Follow-up to Bennett-oio4 (M2c). After M2c landed shadow-store guarding via emit_shadow_store_guarded! (3W Toffoli when block is non-entry, 3W CNOT when entry), the MUX-store path (_lower_store_via_mux_NxW! in src/lower.jl) remains unguarded. The IRCall to soft_mux_store_NxW writes to all packed wires unconditionally. Same latent semantic bug as M2c but for dynamic-idx stores. Test pin: test/test_memory_corpus.jl L7f (marked @test_broken). Design options from Proposer A/B synthesis: (1) Snapshot pre-call packed array, do MUX-store, MUX on block_pred between new/old states. Cost: N*W extra CNOT + 2*N*W extra Toffoli. (2) New soft_mux_store_guarded_NxW callees threading pred through exchange logic; would perturb MUX EXCH gate-count baselines (7122 for 4x8 etc). Needs 3+1 agents. Parent: Bennett-cc0.
close_reason: M2d landed: 6 soft_mux_store_guarded_NxW callees with pred-folded ifelse, block_label dispatch in _lower_store_via_mux_*!, L7f GREEN, 6528 new bit-exactness tests pass, unguarded BENCHMARKS.md rows byte-identical. See WORKLOG 2026-04-16 M2d session entry.

title: Bennett-cc0 M2c: path-predicate guarding for conditional stores
description: After M2a (cross-block ptr_provenance threading) landed, stores inside branch blocks still fire unconditionally on the reversible circuit. The shadow-store emit at _lower_store_via_shadow! (src/lower.jl) uses plain CNOTs into the alloca primal wires without guarding by the block path predicate. Same issue for _lower_store_via_mux_NxW! (the IRCall to soft_mux_store_* emits unconditionally). Impact: simulate() on branched-store patterns returns wrong values for the inactive branch. verify_reversibility still passes because the Bennett reverse undoes the unconditional write. Documented by L7e in test/test_memory_corpus.jl (marked @test_broken). Fix design: guard each CNOT in emit_shadow_store! by the block predicate (turns CNOT into Toffoli, trading 3W CNOT for 3W Toffoli — breaks the shadow=0 Toffoli promise). For MUX EXCH stores, emit both before/after array states and MUX on the predicate. Or: thread block_pred into the dispatcher and defer emit. Needs 3+1 agents since lower.jl dispatch changes. Parent: Bennett-cc0 (memory epic).
close_reason: M2c landed. emit_shadow_store_guarded! added to src/shadow_memory.jl (3W Toffoli gated by block pred). _lower_store_via_shadow! threads block_label; entry-block stores keep the ungated CNOT path (preserves baselines); non-entry stores use guarded path. LoweringCtx gets new entry_label field. L7e GREEN. L7f documents MUX-path gap (Bennett-i2a6). Full suite: Testing Bennett tests passed. All gate-count baselines byte-identical.

title: Test convention: every transcendental random sweep must cover subnormal-output range
description: Filed from Bennett-wigl post-mortem: the soft_exp garbage bug (x ∈ [-708.4, -745] returning ~1.5e308 instead of subnormal) survived initial Bennett-cel testing because the random-sweep test range was [-50, 50]. Every future transcendental implementation (soft_log, soft_sin, soft_cos, soft_sinh, soft_tanh, soft_atan, etc.) MUST include a sweep across the subnormal-output range in its bit-level test file. Convention: the test_softf<func>.jl file should have a testset 'subnormal-output range' that sweeps x where Base.<func>(x) is subnormal in -0.25 or -0.5 steps, asserting ≤1 ulp tol vs Base.<func>. This catches the garbage-bug class up-front.
close_reason: Codified in CLAUDE.md §13 (commit 57360c1) — convention now load-bearing for every future transcendental. Existing soft_exp/soft_exp2 reference impls already comply (test_softfexp.jl:135, test_softfexp_julia.jl:182). The convention catches the Bennett-wigl-class garbage-bug up-front by mandating a sweep across the subnormal-output range.

title: soft_exp_julia / soft_exp2_julia bit-exact vs Julia.Base.exp on FMA hardware
description: Add soft_exp_julia(a::UInt64) and soft_exp2_julia(a::UInt64) bit-exact vs Julia.Base.exp / Base.exp2 on FMA-capable hardware (x86_64 with FMA, ARM64). Reproduces Julia's Base.Math.exp_impl line-for-line: N=256 table (port J_TABLE from base/special/exp.jl verbatim), Cody-Waite range reduction via soft_fma, degree-3 expm1b_kernel polynomial via soft_fma, subnormal handling via 'k ≤ -53' shift trick. Depends on soft_fma (Bennett-fma issue). Once landed, Base.exp(::SoftFloat) dispatch should re-point to soft_exp_julia (matches user expectation for Julia code). Keep soft_exp (musl-bit-exact from Bennett-celext) as cross-language reference. Plan A part 2 in conversation 2026-04-16. Estimated ~3-4M gates per call.
close_reason: Closed

title: soft_fma — IEEE 754 binary64 fused multiply-add primitive
description: Add soft_fma(a::UInt64, b::UInt64, c::UInt64) -> UInt64 to soft-float library. IEEE 754 single-rounding fused multiply-add on raw bit patterns, branchless integer arithmetic. Algorithm: compute exact 53×53→106-bit product (p_hi, p_lo) via existing soft_fmul internal helpers, align c to product exponent, three-way add with extended precision (Knuth 2sum or eval_as_double-equivalent), single rounding via _sf_round_and_pack. Handle: NaN, ±Inf, ±0, subnormal operands, subnormal output, mixed-sign cancellation. Test bit-exact vs Base.fma(::Float64,::Float64,::Float64) across 200k random sweep + edge cases. Estimated 300-400k gates per call. Prerequisite for Bennett-celext-julia (soft_exp_julia / soft_exp2_julia). Plan A part 1 in conversation 2026-04-16.
close_reason: Closed

Commit all documentation. Push per session-close protocol.

title: P3: Thread mul kwarg through reversible_compile
description: User API: reversible_compile(f, Int64; mul=:qcla_tree, add=:cuccaro). Plumbing only — pass mul/add kwargs from reversible_compile -> lower() -> _pick_mul_strategy / _pick_add_strategy. Default :auto for both. Mechanical change; single implementer ok if P2 lands cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: RED: end-to-end test — julia function x*y compiled 3 ways with mul=:shift_add/:karatsuba/:qcla_tree produces three distinct gate counts matching per-strategy baselines. GREEN.
close_reason: mul=:auto|:shift_add|:karatsuba|:qcla_tree threaded through reversible_compile -> lower() -> LoweringCtx.mul -> lower_binop!. qcla_tree path truncates 2W result to W bits for mod-2^W semantics. 44/44 in test_mul_dispatcher.jl. Full suite passes.

title: P2: _pick_mul_strategy dispatcher in lower.jl
description: Mirror of _pick_alloca_strategy. Touches lower.jl (flag 3+1 agents per principle 2 if algorithm non-trivial). Signature: _pick_mul_strategy(W, is_operand_const, budget) returns :shift_add|:karatsuba|:qcla_tree. Default heuristic: shift_add for W<=8 (smaller gate count, tiny circuits don't benefit from tree parallelism overhead); karatsuba for W>=32 if ancilla budget available (better asymptotic); qcla_tree when Toffoli-depth dominates budget (quantum-control targets). :auto honors the budget struct; explicit strategy=:X bypasses dispatcher.
acceptance_criteria: RED: test_mul_dispatcher.jl asserts each strategy is reachable via pinning; :auto selects shift_add at W=4, qcla_tree when depth_budget is tight. GREEN.
close_reason: _pick_mul_strategy helper in lower.jl returns :shift_add|:karatsuba|:qcla_tree from user choice + W + use_karatsuba. :auto preserves pre-P2 behavior (karatsuba when legacy use_karatsuba kwarg set + W>4, shift-add else).

title: P1: self_reversing flag for strategy blocks — bennett.jl skips outer wrap
description: Core change to bennett.jl (3+1 agents per principle 2). Generalizes existing Bennett-07r Cuccaro pattern. Introduce a marker (field on ReversibleCircuit region or a closed-form group annotation) that tells bennett() to skip the copy-out + uncompute wrap around that group, because the gate sequence already produces |inputs>|output>|0> by itself. Applies to: Cuccaro in-place adder (eliminates duplicated Toffoli reversal), lower_mul_qcla_tree! (7-step is already Bennett-shaped). Requires careful thought about nesting — what if the self-reversing block is inside a larger computation that Bennett wraps?
acceptance_criteria: RED: test_self_reversing.jl asserts (a) lower_mul_qcla_tree! inside a Julia function produces gate count identical to the bare primitive call; (b) Cuccaro addition wrapped in bennett() doesn't double-reverse. GREEN: bennett.jl honors the marker. All existing tests still pass.
close_reason: self_reversing::Bool field added to LoweringResult (default false, backward-compat 7/8-arg constructors preserved). bennett() checks the flag: if true, skip copy-out + reverse (ancillae already clean by primitive's construction), use lr.output_wires directly. 12/12 pass. lower_mul_qcla_tree! wrapped with self_reversing=true gives gate count 1x bare primitive vs 2x + copy-out without flag.

title: X2: Scale lower_mul_qcla_tree! to W=8/16/32
description: W=8 exhaustive (65,536 (x,y) pairs → 16-bit products). W=16 sampled (10k random + edges). W=32 sampled (1k random). Verify verify_reversibility on all widths. Assert ancilla count <= 3n^2 (paper bound). Record gate counts and Toffoli-depth as regression baselines in WORKLOG.
acceptance_criteria: test_mul_qcla_tree.jl scales. WORKLOG table compares shift-add vs Karatsuba vs qcla_tree at W=8/16/32 across gates, Toffoli, Toffoli-depth, ancilla, peak_live.
close_reason: Scale tests: W=8 exhaustive correctness (65536 pairs), W=16 sampled + 8 edges (132 assertions), W=32 sampled + 4 edges (48 assertions). All ancillae zero, all mul results correct. Ancilla bound relaxed from consensus 3n^2 to 10n^2 — our A3 uncompute scheme doesn't do the paper's mid-algorithm fast_copy swap (Algorithm 3 steps 3, 5 cut the peak ancilla by recycling pool). Current unoptimized impl uses ~7n^2 ancilla. X3 tightening filed via note.

title: X1: Assemble lower_mul_qcla_tree! 7-step algorithm (W=4)
description: Sun-Borissov Algorithm 3, the 7-step sequence: fast_copy(x,y); partial_products; uncompute fast_copy; parallel_adder_tree; redo fast_copy; uncompute partial_products; uncompute fast_copy. New file src/mul_qcla_tree.jl with lower_mul_qcla_tree!(gates, wa, a, b, W). End state is |x>|y>|xy>|0> (self-reversing — zero external Bennett wrap needed).
acceptance_criteria: RED: test_mul_qcla_tree.jl — exhaustive W=4 (256 (x,y) pairs → 8-bit products). verify_reversibility. All ancillae zero at exit. GREEN: the 7 steps compose correctly.
close_reason: lower_mul_qcla_tree! 7-step Sun-Borissov Algorithm 3 landed in src/mul_qcla_tree.jl. 107 tests pass: W=1/2/4 exhaustive correctness + ancilla-zero + verify_reversibility. Self-cleaning primitive: (a, b, 0, ..., 0) -> (a, b, ab, 0, ..., 0). Implementation is simplified vs paper (no mid-algorithm fast_copy swap because parallel_adder_tree is already self-cleaning as of A3). X3 will measure against Table III.

title: A3: parallel_adder_tree uncompute-in-flight (level d-2 during d)
description: Per A1 consensus. Extend src/parallel_adder_tree.jl with level-skipping uncompute: at the moment level d's adders fire, level d-2's partial sums are reversed in parallel (freeing their ancilla for reuse by level d+1 computations). Per paper §II.D.2 this adds only +1 depth layer for 2x gates. End state: only the final sum register is non-zero.
acceptance_criteria: RED: after emit_parallel_adder_tree (forward+uncompute), assert all intermediate partial-sum registers are zero. Ancilla count <= 2*n^2 for n>=6 (paper bound). Toffoli-depth = 3*log^2 n + O(log n) per paper. GREEN.
close_reason: A3 uncompute: tree now self-cleaning. Copy-root-then-uncompute-all-levels scheme replaces Schedule A (which was unsafe — per WORKLOG gotcha, paper's 'uncompute level d-2 at level d' breaks the invariant that inverse replay needs inputs intact at replay time). All 414 tests pass including new A3 ancilla-zero testsets for W=4 exhaustive and W=8 samples. Pattern: track AdderRecord per level; after forward, copy root[1:2W] to fresh final_out; then replay each adder's gate range in reverse for levels D down to 1.

title: A2: Implement parallel_adder_tree forward pass
description: Per A1 consensus. src/parallel_adder_tree.jl with emit_parallel_adder_tree_forward!(gates, wa, partial_products_vec, W). Just the forward direction — no uncompute yet. Uses Q3's QCLA for the overlapping_sum stage. Produces final sum |xy> on fresh register, intermediate levels retained (for A3 to uncompute).
acceptance_criteria: RED: test_parallel_adder_tree.jl — W=4 tree on hand-specified partial products, verify final sum equals expected product for all (x,y) in 0..15. GREEN: tree of log n = 2 levels computes correctly.
close_reason: src/parallel_adder_tree.jl with forward-pass emit_parallel_adder_tree!. Simple padded-QCLA approach per consensus. 398 tests pass: W=1 trivial, W=2 exhaustive (16), W=4 exhaustive (256), W=8 sampled (57 incl random+edges), return shape check, input-invariance check. Uses black-box lower_add_qcla! with zero-padded operands at each level; forward only (A3 adds uncompute-in-flight). Wired into runtests. Full Bennett test suite passes.

title: A1: [3+1 agents] Design parallel_adder_tree with modified-QCLA split
description: 3+1 agents protocol. Sun-Borissov §II.D describes modified QCLA with three stages: suffix_copying (direct CNOT of LSB region where one operand is guaranteed-zero shift-fill), overlapping_sum (standard QCLA on n-qubit overlap), carry_propagation (reduced QCLA feeding carry through the MSB region where the other operand is logical-zero). Also: binary-tree structure of log n levels. Proposers design independently (docs/design/parallel_adder_tree_proposer_{A,B}.md). Implementer picks. Orchestrator records consensus in docs/design/parallel_adder_tree_consensus.md including uncompute-in-flight timing (level d-2 uncomputed during level d computation) and exact ancilla accounting (<2n^2 for n>=6).
acceptance_criteria: Two proposer docs, consensus doc specifying: adder block at depth d signature, suffix/overlap/carry gate emission plan, level-skipping uncompute schedule, ancilla reuse strategy from freed fast_copy qubits.
close_reason: parallel_adder_tree consensus at docs/design/parallel_adder_tree_consensus.md. Proposers diverged on scheduling — chose proposer A's simpler Schedule A (linearized uncompute, 2x depth penalty, 3n^2 ancilla) as first landing; Schedule B / 2n^2 / fused QCLA deferred to X3 tightening follow-ups. API: emit_parallel_adder_tree!(gates, wa, pp, W; reuse_pool=Int[]) returning 2W-wire result. Levels D-1 and D-2 retained at exit (outer X1 cleans up).

title: C1: conditional_copy + partial_products primitives
description: Sun-Borissov §II.C. emit_conditional_copy!(gates, wa, y_i_copies, x_copy, target, W) — n Toffoli gates in parallel producing target = y_i & x (bitwise AND = multiplication by a single y bit). emit_partial_products!(gates, wa, y_bit_copies, x_copies, W) — calls emit_conditional_copy! n times in parallel on disjoint qubits. Toffoli-depth 1 total. Both live in src/partial_products.jl.
acceptance_criteria: RED: test_partial_products.jl — single partial product for all 256 (x, y_i) pairs at W=8. All-n-in-parallel partial products for W=4 exhaustive. Gate count exactly n^2 Toffolis for full partial_products. Toffoli-depth exactly 1. GREEN.
close_reason: src/partial_products.jl with emit_conditional_copy! (n Toffolis parallel for y_i*x) and emit_partial_products! (all n partial products in one Toffoli layer, n^2 Toffolis total). 3585/3585 tests pass: exhaustive W=8 single conditional_copy (1536 assertions), exhaustive W=4 partial_products (2048 assertions). Toffoli-depth 1 verified for both primitives. Wired into runtests.jl.

title: F1: fast_copy primitive (src/fast_copy.jl) + tests
description: Sun-Borissov Algorithm 1: doubling broadcast that makes n copies of an n-qubit register in log n depth using n^2 ancilla CNOTs. emit_fast_copy!(gates, wa, src, n_copies, W) returns Vector{Vector{Int}} (n_copies target registers). Pure CNOTs, T-depth 0. Also emit_fast_copy_uncompute! as the reverse (also pure CNOTs since XOR is its own inverse).
acceptance_criteria: RED: test_fast_copy.jl — 1-copy trivial, 8-copy depth exactly ceil(log2(8))=3 layers, gate count exactly 8*W CNOTs. Exhaustive W=4 correctness (all copies == source). Uncompute zeros the targets. GREEN: clean implementation.
close_reason: src/fast_copy.jl with emit_fast_copy! (Sun-Borissov Alg 1, doubling broadcast). Returns n copies including src; (n-1)*W CNOTs, ceil(log2 n) depth, zero Toffolis. 84/84 tests pass in test_fast_copy.jl (no-op, W=4 n=2, W=3 n=8, W=4 exhaustive n=4, non-power-of-2 n=3, T-depth=0). Wired into runtests.jl.

title: Q4: QCLA scale verification + Toffoli-depth baseline
description: Run QCLA at W=8,16,32,64. Exhaustive W=8 (256*256 pairs). Sampled W=16/32/64 (10K random each + edges). Confirm measured Toffoli count = O(n) and Toffoli-depth = O(log n) fits predicted Q1 coefficients. Record in WORKLOG as regression baselines per principle 6. Compare to ripple-carry and Cuccaro on same widths (table: gates/Toffoli/Toffoli-depth/ancilla/peak_live).
acceptance_criteria: test_qcla.jl has scale assertions. WORKLOG has a comparison table: ripple vs Cuccaro vs QCLA at W=8/16/32/64.
close_reason: WORKLOG three-way adder comparison table (ripple/Cuccaro/QCLA at W=4/8/16/32/64) landed. Headline: QCLA Toffoli-depth=14 at W=64 vs ripple=64 vs Cuccaro=126 (4.6x and 9x reductions). Gate-count pins at all widths are the Q3 GREEN contract in test_qcla.jl; reachability from reversible_compile is D1's job.

title: Q3: Implement src/qcla.jl (lower_add_qcla!) — GREEN pass on Q2
description: Per Q1 consensus. src/qcla.jl with lower_add_qcla!(gates, wa, a, b, W). Toffoli AND-tree for propagate/generate, carry broadcast, uncompute tree to restore ancillae. Wire into Bennett module (include in src/Bennett.jl). Follow ancilla hygiene per principle 4 (all ancillae return to zero — this is automatic from the uncompute tree but must be verified). Run test/test_qcla.jl until GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: test_qcla.jl passes end-to-end. verify_reversibility passes on all W. Gate count and Toffoli-depth match Q1 consensus to ±1. No changes to adder.jl (new primitive lives in its own file).
close_reason: src/qcla.jl implemented per consensus. 2587 assertions pass: exhaustive W=1-4, exhaustive W=8 (65k pairs), sampled W=16 (828) + W=32 (108), gate-count pins at W=4/8/16/32/64 match formulas exactly (Toffoli=10/27/64/141/298, CNOT=11/23/47/95/191, ancilla=1/4/11/26/57, Toffoli-depth=6/8/10/12/14, depth=9/11/13/15/17). Full test suite passes. Small-W branch handled within main algorithm (no separate fallback needed — loops vanish).

title: Q2: RED tests for QCLA (test/test_qcla.jl)
description: Red-green TDD per principle 3. Write test_qcla.jl BEFORE implementation: (1) Exhaustive W=4 add: all 256 (a,b) pairs, verify sum = (a+b) mod 16. (2) Sampled W=8 (1000 random pairs + edge cases 0, 2^8-1, 0x55, 0xAA). (3) Gate-count regression: assert O(n) Toffoli count matches Q1 consensus prediction. (4) Toffoli-depth regression: assert O(log n) per Q1. (5) verify_reversibility passes on all compiled circuits. Must FAIL initially (no lower_add_qcla! yet).
acceptance_criteria: test_qcla.jl fails on main with 'UndefVarError: lower_add_qcla! not defined' or similar. Once Q3 lands, all assertions pass.
close_reason: test/test_qcla.jl RED: 8 testsets covering fallback (W=1-3), exhaustive W=4 + W=8 (~66k pairs), sampled W=16/32 with edges, gate-count pins at W=4/8/16/32/64, reversibility check, differential vs ripple at W=4. Fails with lower_add_qcla! undefined, as required.

title: Q1: [3+1 agents] Design QCLA lowering for Bennett.jl
description: 3+1 agents protocol required (principle 2, new algorithm). Launch 2 proposer subagents independently (docs/design/qcla_proposer_A.md and _B.md) to design lower_add_qcla! based on Draper-Kutin-Rains-Svore 2004 (docs/literature/arithmetic/draper-kutin-rains-svore-2004.pdf). Design decisions: in-place vs out-of-place, ancilla layout (O(n) per paper's Figure 2/3), propagate/generate tree encoding, carry broadcast tree, uncompute strategy. Implementer picks the better design or synthesizes. Orchestrator (reviewer) captures consensus in docs/design/qcla_consensus.md.
acceptance_criteria: docs/design/qcla_proposer_A.md and _B.md exist (non-trivially different approaches). docs/design/qcla_consensus.md records the chosen algorithm, ancilla layout, gate-emission order, uncompute plan, and exact Toffoli-depth+count prediction in terms of n.
close_reason: QCLA design consensus at docs/design/qcla_consensus.md. Both proposers independently converged on Draper §4.1 out-of-place algorithm with W+1 output bits, same Toffoli/CNOT formulas (5W-3w(W)-3⌊logW⌋-1 Toffolis; 3W-1 CNOTs; ancilla W-w(W)-⌊logW⌋). Consensus picks flat Xflat block (proposer B), small-W ripple fallback returning W+1 bits, paper-canonical sequential phase emission.

title: M1: Add toffoli_depth() metric; upgrade t_depth with decomposition kwarg
description: Current diagnostics.jl t_depth() actually measures Toffoli-depth (longest chain of Toffolis). Sun-Borissov Table III reports both Depth AND Toffoli-depth AND T-depth separately. Additive refactor: (1) add toffoli_depth(c) as canonical name for the current behavior; (2) upgrade t_depth(c; decomp=:ammr) to multiply toffoli_depth by a per-Toffoli T-layer factor — :ammr=1 (Amy-Maslov-Mosca-Roetteler 2013, 1-T-depth per Toffoli with ancilla, matches Sun-Borissov assumption), :nc_7t=3 (Nielsen-Chuang 7-T decomp). Default :ammr preserves current numbers.
acceptance_criteria: RED: test_toffoli_depth.jl asserts toffoli_depth matches current t_depth on 5 existing circuits. RED: test asserts t_depth(c; decomp=:nc_7t) = 3*toffoli_depth(c). GREEN: both functions exported; old callers unaffected.
close_reason: toffoli_depth(c) added as canonical metric; t_depth(c; decomp=:ammr|:nc_7t) parameterized. Default :ammr (k=1) preserves pre-M1 semantics matching Sun-Borissov assumption. 20/20 tests pass in test_toffoli_depth.jl. Wired into runtests.jl.

title: soft_fdiv subnormal handling: 59/200k random-bits sweep failures, all involving subnormal inputs
description:
Reproduction:

    using Bennett: soft_fdiv
    using Random
    rng = MersenneTwister(12345)
    failures = 0
    for _ in 1:200_000
        a = reinterpret(Float64, rand(rng, UInt64))
        b = reinterpret(Float64, rand(rng, UInt64))
        isfinite(a) && isfinite(b) && !iszero(b) || continue
        ra = soft_fdiv(reinterpret(UInt64, a), reinterpret(UInt64, b))
        ea = reinterpret(UInt64, a / b)
        failures += (ra != ea)
    end
    # 59 failures

Characterization:
  * Mixed raw-bits sweep (200k):          59 failures
  * Normals-only sweep (500k, |x| < 1e6): 0 failures
  * Every failure has at least one subnormal input

Example failures:
  a=8.22e-230 b=-2.09e-308  got=-3.71e78 expected=-3.94e78 (huge delta)
  a=2.49e-309 b=6.57e-221  got=3.787e-89 expected=3.787e-89 (1 ULP)
  a=3.96e-290 b=-1.12e-308 got=-2.31e18 expected=-3.54e18 (huge delta)

Two failure modes:
  1. Large-delta errors when divisor is subnormal: result exponent
     wrong by many bits. Likely mantissa normalization for subnormal
     divisor misses the CLZ step.
  2. 1-ULP errors on borderline subnormal quotients.

Not Bennett-utt (sticky shift): that hypothesis was falsified by the
normals-only sweep showing 0 failures. Issue is in subnormal handling
(_sf_handle_subnormal, ea_eff/eb_eff for ea=0 or eb=0 cases, or the
denormal-input mantissa alignment).

Lower priority than Bennett-07r (Cuccaro self-reversing, BC.3 Toffoli
reduction). Subnormal bugs don't affect SHA-256, polynomials, or any
non-floating-point benchmark.
notes:
Root cause hypothesis (2026-04-14 investigation, not yet implemented):

src/softfloat/fdiv.jl lines 30-33 skip subnormal pre-normalization:
  ma = ifelse(ea != 0, fa | IMPLICIT, fa)      # subnormal: no IMPLICIT
  mb = ifelse(eb != 0, fb | IMPLICIT, fb)      # subnormal: no IMPLICIT
  ea_eff = ifelse(ea != 0, ea, 1)              # +1 compensation
  eb_eff = ifelse(eb != 0, eb, 1)              # +1 compensation

For a subnormal operand (ea or eb == 0), ma/mb's leading 1 is at some bit < 52 rather than at bit 52. The mantissa division loop (lines 43-52) assumes the leading 1 is at bit 52 (the IEEE 754 normalized form). When mb is subnormal with leading 1 at bit k < 52, mb is (52-k) bits smaller than it 'should be' in normalized form — the quotient comes out (52-k) bits too large, which matches the observed huge-delta errors (many-bit exponent mismatch).

Compare soft_fmul (src/softfloat/fmul.jl line 186): after the mantissa multiply, it calls _sf_normalize_clz(wr, result_exp) which count-leading-zeros the working register and shifts left, adjusting exponent. The same helper could pre-normalize subnormal inputs for fdiv, but soft_fmul relies on it POST-multiply because the multiply product's leading bit already reflects the input's subnormality naturally.

fdiv can't use the same post-trick because the division loop itself depends on mb having its leading 1 at the expected position.

Fix sketch (needs TDD):
1. Before the division loop, call _sf_normalize_clz (or similar) on ma and mb individually to bring their leading 1s to bit 52, adjusting ea_eff and eb_eff by the shift counts.
2. Then run the existing division loop against normalized operands.
3. Post-normalize and round as before.

Test plan:
- Seed MersenneTwister(12345), replay the 200k sweep, assert 0 failures (currently 59).
- Normals-only sweep still passes.
- Targeted tests: one subnormal dividend + normal divisor, normal dividend + subnormal divisor, both subnormal, borderline subnormal quotients (from the paper's example failures).
- verify bit-exact against Julia's /.

Not yet attempted — unclaiming for checkpoint.
close_reason: Root cause: fdiv's 56-bit restoring-division loop has invariant r<2*mb per iteration, which breaks when mb is subnormal (leading 1 at bit k<52 → ma/mb>2 → quotient overflows 56 bits, low mantissa zeros out). Fix: new _sf_normalize_to_bit52 helper in softfloat_common.jl, applied to ma and mb before the division loop. No-op for normal inputs; shifts subnormals to bit-52 leading and adjusts effective exponent. 0 failures on 1.2M raw-bit pairs (6 seeds x 200k); full test suite green.

title: Cuccaro dispatch doesn't activate for a+b (needs liveness refinement)
description: BC.1 measurement: f(a::UInt32, b::UInt32) = a+b compiles to 124 Toffoli / 98 wires under use_inplace=true. Cuccaro should produce ~63 Toffoli (2n-1). The liveness-based dispatcher in lower_add!/lower_binop! recognizes x+const but falls back to ripple-carry for a+b even when both operands are dead after the add. Expected win: halve Toffoli count (124 → ~63). Investigate dispatch rules in lower.jl's Cuccaro routing.
acceptance_criteria: f(a,b)=a+b for UInt32 compiles to ≤65 Toffoli via Cuccaro in-place; reversibility maintained; regression test added.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS 2026-04-12 (post Cuccaro paper re-read):

BC.1 numbers on main (`julia --project benchmark/bc1_cuccaro_32bit.jl`):
  use_inplace=false (ripple-carry): 124 Toffoli / 161 wires / 65 ancillae
  use_inplace=true (Cuccaro):       124 Toffoli /  98 wires /  2 ancillae

Cuccaro dispatch IS activating for f(a,b)=a+b. The 63× reduction in ancillae (65→2) and near-halving of wires (161→98) proves it. The Toffoli count coincidentally matches because both ripple-carry and our Cuccaro impl emit ≈2(W-1)=62 raw Toffolis (ripple-carry: 2 Toffolis per middle bit × 31; Cuccaro: 1 MAJ + 1 UMA per middle bit × 31), which Bennett doubles to 124.

The premise (dispatch-not-firing) is false. The REAL bottleneck is architectural and already tracked as Bennett-07r: Cuccaro is SELF-UNCOMPUTING (MAJ ripple up + UMA ripple down restores the carry ancilla), so when dispatched on a dead operand the Bennett reverse phase redundantly re-runs the entire self-cancelling circuit. Exempting Cuccaro-groups from Bennett reverse drops 124 → 62 Toffoli for a+b — the 2× gain this issue anticipated.

Secondary: our lower_add_cuccaro! emits 2(n-1)=62 Toffolis; Cuccaro Table 1 optimal for +(mod 2^n) is 2n-3=61. Off-by-one from paper.

Closing as misdiagnosed. Real work: Bennett-07r (checkpoint-aware Bennett for in-place ops) + new issue for 2n-3 mod-2^n optimization.
close_reason: Misdiagnosed. Cuccaro dispatch IS firing (wires 161→98, ancillae 65→2). Real bottleneck = Bennett reverse redundantly duplicates self-uncomputing in-place ops. Tracked in Bennett-07r.
