title: Refactor soft_acosh to use soft_log1p (drop K=15 polynomial)
description: Bennett-tfmo follow-up. With soft_log1p now landed, soft_acosh can use Julia stdlib's natural three-regime form: 1≤x<2 → log1p(t+sqrt(2t+t²)) with t=x-1; 2≤x<2^28 → log(2x - 1/(x+sqrt(x²-1))); x≥2^28 → log(x)+ln(2); x<1 → NaN (domain). Drops 16 polynomial coefficients + 30 Horner-Taylor ops.
close_reason: Refactored soft_acosh to Julia stdlib three-regime form: (b) 1≤x<2 → log1p(t+sqrt(2t+t²)) where t=x-1; (c) 2≤x<2^28 → log(2x - 1/(x+sqrt(x²-1))); (d) x≥2^28 → log(x)+ln(2); x<1 → NaN. Dropped 16 coefficients + 30 polynomial ops. test_softfacosh.jl 4492/4492; LLVM dispatch 27/27. Max 1 ULP at x=1.0001/1.001.

title: Refactor soft_atanh to use soft_log1p (drop K=25 polynomial)
description: Bennett-tfmo follow-up. With soft_log1p now landed, soft_atanh can use Julia stdlib's natural two-regime form: |x|<0.5 → 0.5·log1p(2|x|/(1-|x|)); 0.5≤|x|≤1 → 0.5·log((1+|x|)/(1-|x|)); |x|>1 → NaN. Drops 26 polynomial coefficients + 50 Horner-Taylor ops. Same ≤2 ULP budget.
close_reason: Refactored soft_atanh to Julia stdlib two-regime form: (a) |x|<0.5 → 0.5·log1p(2|x|/(1-|x|)); (b) 0.5≤|x|≤1 → 0.5·log((1+|x|)/(1-|x|)); |x|>1 → NaN. Dropped 26 coefficients + 50 polynomial ops in favour of 1 log1p + 1 log + ~10 fp ops. test_softfatanh.jl 2087/2087; LLVM dispatch 33/33. 0 ULP on most spot-checks, max 1 ULP at x=0.1.

title: Refactor soft_asinh to use soft_log1p (drop K=30 polynomial)
description: Worklog 062 noted: with soft_log1p now landed (Bennett-0ulc, Tier C2.1), soft_asinh can use Julia stdlib's natural formula `log1p(|x| + x²/(1+sqrt(1+x²)))` instead of the K=30 Taylor polynomial that was needed because Bennett.jl previously lacked log1p. Reduces ~60 soft_fma operations (and the load-bearing 30 polynomial coefficients) to a small fixed number of fp ops + one soft_log1p call. Expected: substantial gate-count drop on Float64 asinh compiles, possibly tighter ULP. Must preserve ≤2 ULP vs Base.asinh on 300k random × 3 seeds + the §13 subnormal-input contract (currently 0 ULP across 1074 binades × signs).
close_reason: Refactored soft_asinh to use the Julia stdlib natural four-regime formula now that soft_log1p exists. Dropped 30 polynomial coefficients + 60 Horner-Taylor ops in favour of: (a) tiny→a, (b) log1p(|x|+x²/(1+sqrt(1+x²))), (c) log(2|x|+1/(sqrt(x²+1)+|x|)), (d) log(|x|)+ln(2). 13 soft-float ops total. 26 strategic spot-checks: 0 ULP vs Base.asinh (even tighter than the 2-ULP budget). test_softfasinh.jl 5178/5178; test_sfx9_llvm_asinh_dispatch.jl 31/31; standalone compile + verify_reversibility=true. Subnormal-input §13 contract now GIVEN by regime (a) fast path. Siblings acosh/atanh have the same opportunity — separate follow-ups, not bundled.

title: test_t0_preprocessing allowlist gap: llvm.memset.p0.i64 from cond_pair
description: test/test_t0_preprocessing.jl:157 fails with 'unexpected' skip: "cond_pair:raw=ir_extract.jl: call in @julia_#491_44633:%L42:   call void @llvm.memset.p0.i64(p". The cond_pair corpus function (x -> [x, -x][1 + Int(x<0)]) compiles to LLVM IR containing an llvm.memset.p0.i64 (Julia's array-temp zero-init). Bennett's IR extractor correctly rejects the memset shape it doesn't yet support (variable-N or non-alloca-backed dst — Bennett-37mt/9nwt only cover const-size memcpy + const-c const-N memset on alloca-i8-backed dst). The test allowlist (test_t0_preprocessing.jl:144-153) was last updated by Bennett-5oyt / U15 (Apr 2026) and pre-dates the Bennett-37mt/9nwt/munq memcpy/memset workstream (May 2026). Either: (a) extend the allowlist to include a 'memset' / 'memcpy' keyword (paralleling 'volatile' / 'inline-asm' which exist for similar fail-loud guards); or (b) extend memset support in instructions.jl to cover the cond_pair shape (likely tracked under Bennett-8bys umbrella).
notes:
Pre-existing as of 2026-05-04 catalog state (failure observed during Pkg.test for Bennett-ckvj asin work; surfaced because t0_preprocessing failed even on origin/main without ckvj changes). Unrelated to ckvj/bd7f content.

REOPENED 2026-05-14: the 2026-05-13 close was based on a STANDALONE run (julia --project test/test_t0_preprocessing.jl), which passes. But the failure is test-ordering-dependent and STILL reproduces under full Pkg.test() — confirmed 2026-05-14 (540208 pass / 3 fail; t0 is one of the 3, cond_pair flags llvm.memset.p0.i64 exactly as the description states). The bug's own NOTES already said it was surfaced via Pkg.test, so the standalone-only check was insufficient. Next agent: reproduce via full Pkg.test() (or bisect the ordering dep), not the standalone file.
close_reason: Root cause: NOT a test-ordering dependency — it's --check-bounds. Pkg.test() hard-codes --check-bounds=yes; standalone julia uses the default. Under =yes, cond_pair heap-allocates its array literal (GC frame -> volatile llvm.memset + inline-asm thread-pointer read + ijl_gc_small_alloc external call, all CORRECTLY fail-loud rejected by Bennett). The actual bug was test-side: test_t0_preprocessing.jl truncated the captured error at sprint(showerror,e)[1:min(80,end)], chopping off the 'volatile' keyword its allowlist already greps for. Fix (test-only, no 3+1): capture the full error message, truncate display-only (char-safe), print the active --check-bounds mode + header warning so this can't be wrongly closed again. _handle_memset_arm investigated and confirmed correct — left untouched. Verified: test_t0_preprocessing.jl 2/2 under both --check-bounds=yes and default; full Pkg.test() green 540211 pass / 0 fail / 3 broken / 30m57s. The 2026-05-13 stale-close was wrong because it checked standalone (the one config where it passes).

title: Generic vector-form intrinsic re-scalarisation in _convert_vector_instruction
description:
## What

Extend `_convert_vector_instruction` (`src/extract/vectors.jl:343`) to handle vector-form LLVM intrinsic calls by re-scalarising into N independent scalar intrinsic calls, then dispatching each through the existing scalar `_handle_intrinsic` path.

Currently the function rejects `<N x T> @llvm.SOMETHING.vNT(<N x T>...)` calls with `unsupported vector opcode LLVMCall`. cc0.7 (chunk 028, 2026-04-20) handled extractelement/insertelement/shufflevector but did not extend to vector-intrinsic calls.

## Why

Surfaced by Bennett-qpke (Tier C1.2 atan, 2026-05-04). First-pass `soft_atan` had four eager parallel `soft_fdiv` calls (one per id-bucket); Julia auto-vectorised them into `<4 x i64>` ops including `llvm.smax.v4i64`. qpke worked around it by collapsing to a single `soft_fdiv` after pre-selecting `(num, den)` — but the pattern-match avoidance is fragile:

1. **Future transcendentals may not factor cleanly.** The qpke avoidance relies on a clean `(args, op)` factorisation. A primitive whose parallel paths use *different* operations cannot be collapsed the same way.
2. **Multi-language LLVM ingest (Bennett-xkv).** Clang/Rust auto-vectorisers are far more aggressive than Julia. Raw `.ll` from C/C++/Rust will hit vector intrinsic forms regularly.
3. **User Julia code is opaque.** A user-supplied numerical function that auto-vectorises will trip Bennett. The user cannot easily disable Julia LLVM optimization.

## Approach

Generic re-scalarisation in `_convert_vector_instruction`:

1. Detect `LLVM.CallInst` whose callee is an intrinsic with a vector return type and vector argument types.
2. Parse the intrinsic name `@llvm.SOMETHING.v<N><T>` to extract scalar form `@llvm.SOMETHING.<T>` and vector width N.
3. For each lane i ∈ 0..N-1:
   - Extract scalar operand from each vector input via cc0.7 lane-extraction infrastructure (`lanes::Dict`).
   - Synthesise a scalar `LLVM.CallInst` (or directly emit the equivalent `IRCall` / `IRBinOp` / etc.) for `@llvm.SOMETHING.<T>(scalar_arg_i, ...)`.
4. Combine N scalar results back into an `<N x T>` vector value via the existing lane-merge path.

Single change covers all of: `llvm.smax`, `llvm.smin`, `llvm.umax`, `llvm.umin`, `llvm.abs`, `llvm.ctpop`, `llvm.bitreverse`, `llvm.bswap`, `llvm.cttz`, `llvm.ctlz`, `llvm.fshl`, `llvm.fshr`, vector forms of `llvm.sqrt` / `llvm.sin` / `llvm.cos` / `llvm.tan` / `llvm.exp` / `llvm.log` / `llvm.pow` / `llvm.fma` / `llvm.fmuladd` / `llvm.atan` / etc.

## Tests (TDD)

`test/test_<id>_vector_intrinsic_rescalarise.jl`:
- 5 hand-built `.ll` fixtures exercising vector forms of:
  1. `llvm.smax.v4i64` (the qpke-discovery case)
  2. `llvm.umax.v2i64`
  3. `llvm.abs.v4i32` (signed integer abs)
  4. `llvm.fma.v2f64` (vector form of an existing scalar callee)
  5. `llvm.sqrt.v2f64` (vector form of llvm.sqrt — wired via Bennett-1pb)
- Each fixture: extract → compile → simulate → assert equality with N-fold scalar oracle.
- Negative test: `llvm.somemissing.v4i64` (intrinsic NOT in the scalar dispatch table) must fail loud with a precise message naming the missing scalar form.

## Acceptance

- `_convert_vector_instruction` recognises any vector-intrinsic call whose scalar form has an existing dispatch arm and re-scalarises it correctly.
- Re-scalarised vector intrinsics in compiled circuits produce bit-exact outputs vs N-fold scalar evaluation.
- qpke-style "avoid eager parallel calls" discipline becomes optional rather than load-bearing for Bennett-correctness (still desirable for gate-count compactness, but no longer a correctness gate).
- Full Pkg.test green; cc0.7 existing vector tests unaffected.

## 3+1 protocol

Touches `src/extract/vectors.jl` (post-x3jc split of ir_extract.jl, still core-pipeline per CLAUDE.md §2). The change adds a new dispatch arm with non-trivial design space (lane-extraction order, intrinsic-name parsing, lane-merge integration) — **3+1 protocol applies**.

## Related

- **Parent**: Bennett-vb2 (vector operations umbrella, P3 OPEN). This sub-bead covers vector-form *intrinsic calls* specifically; vb2 covers extractelement/insertelement/shufflevector primitives (already partially handled by cc0.7).
- **Surfaced by**: Bennett-qpke (Tier C1.2 atan, 2026-05-04). See `worklog/057_*.md` qpke entry, gotcha #1, for the SLP-vectorisation discovery.
- **Blocks**: future transcendentals where the parallel-fdiv pattern is not factorable.

## Sites

- src/extract/vectors.jl (target — re-scalarisation arm)
- src/extract/instructions.jl (existing scalar dispatch — re-used as the per-lane handler)
- test/test_<id>_vector_intrinsic_rescalarise.jl (new)
- test/fixtures/ll/<id>_*.ll (5 new fixtures)
close_reason: Implemented vector LLVM intrinsic lane scalarisation in src/extract/vectors.jl using _VectorLaneValue adapter through scalar _handle_intrinsic. Added fixtures/tests for smax.v4i64, umax.v2i64, abs.v4i32, sqrt.v2f64, fma.v2f64, unsupported intrinsic rejection, f32 sqrt rejection, poison immarg rejection, and float min/max rejection. Focused/vector/intrinsic tests pass; full Pkg.test did not complete cleanly in-session and is documented in worklog.

title: soft_atan + llvm.atan dispatch (Tier C1.2)
description:
## What

Implement `soft_atan(x::Float64) → Float64` bit-exact against `Base.atan` (≤ 2 ULP empirical contract per CLAUDE.md §13), and wire `llvm.atan.f64` direct dispatch in `src/extract/instructions.jl` to `IRCall(soft_atan, ...)`. Tier C1.2 in the Enzyme parity north-star (filed 2026-05-03), follow-on to Bennett-s1zl (soft_tan, 2026-05-04).

## Why

`atan` is the cheapest C1 sibling after sin/cos/tan: bounded argument range (no Payne-Hanek, no Cody-Waite), no shared state with `_rp_rem_pio2`. Unblocks `asin` / `acos` (both reduce to atan via identities) and `atan2` (separate bead — verify llvm.atan2 ingest path).

## Scope

- `src/softfloat/fatan.jl` — port musl `atan.c`. Range split:
  - `|x| < 2^{-27}` → return `x` (tiny-arg fast path; tan(x)≈x)
  - `|x| < 7/16` → polynomial on x directly
  - `7/16 ≤ |x| < 11/16` → atan(0.5) + poly((x-0.5)/(1+0.5x))
  - `11/16 ≤ |x| < 19/16` → atan(1) + poly((x-1)/(1+x))
  - `19/16 ≤ |x| < 39/16` → atan(1.5) + poly((x-1.5)/(1+1.5x))
  - `|x| ≥ 39/16` → π/2 + poly(-1/x) (asymptotic)
- Polynomial: musl uses 11-coefficient even-power series in x², degree 22.
- `src/Bennett.jl` — register `Base.atan(::SoftFloat) = soft_atan(...)` per the soft_tan pattern.
- `src/extract/instructions.jl` — add `LLVMIntrinsic.atan` arm in `_handle_intrinsic` mirroring soft_tan dispatch.
- `_CALLEES_FP_TRANS` extended 15 → 16; `test_kmuj_callee_groups.jl` pinned-count update.

## Tests (TDD — write red first)

`test/test_softfatan.jl`:
- 100k random samples × 5 magnitude buckets × 3 seeds (= 500k) bit-exact vs `Base.atan`. Max ULP ≤ 1, fails > 2 ULP = 0.
- Edge cases: ±0, ±Inf, NaN, ±2^{-1075} (smallest subnormal), ±π/4 surrogate values, ±1, ±2^53 (large-arg fast path boundary).
- **Subnormal-OUTPUT sweep per CLAUDE.md §13**: `Base.atan(x)` is subnormal only for `|x| ≤ ~2^{-1022}` (atan(x) ≈ x there). Sweep `|x| ∈ [2^{-1075}, 2^{-1022}]` in 0.5-binade steps; assert bit-exact.
- Range-split-boundary stress: 10k samples each at the 4 boundaries (7/16, 11/16, 19/16, 39/16) ± 1 ULP.

`test/test_atan_intrinsic.jl` (or fold into existing transcendentals intrinsic test):
- Compile `(x::Float64) -> atan(x)`; verify circuit reversibility, gate-count regression baseline pinned, bit-exact output for ±0/±1/±2/±0.5/±10/NaN/±Inf.

## 3+1 deviation note

Touches `src/extract/instructions.jl` (post-x3jc split of ir_extract.jl, still core-pipeline per CLAUDE.md §2). The dispatcher arm is mechanical — mirror of existing soft_sin/soft_cos/soft_tan arms, no architectural design space. Per the Bennett-munq precedent (chunk 055, 2026-05-03), deeming 3+1 ceremony unnecessary for mechanical mirror dispatch arms with explicit corpus survey is acceptable. The substantive `soft_atan` body lives in `src/softfloat/fatan.jl` — NOT core-pipeline.

## Acceptance

- `soft_atan` bit-exact (≤1 ULP max) vs `Base.atan` across 500k random samples + edge cases + subnormal-output sweep.
- `Bennett.reversible_compile(atan, Float64)` produces a verified-reversible circuit with bit-exact output.
- Full Pkg.test green.
- `_CALLEES_FP_TRANS` count pinned at 16.

## Sites

- src/softfloat/fatan.jl (NEW)
- src/softfloat/softfloat.jl (loader — register fatan.jl)
- src/Bennett.jl (Base.atan dispatch)
- src/extract/instructions.jl (llvm.atan arm)
- test/test_softfatan.jl (NEW)
- test/test_kmuj_callee_groups.jl (pinned count)
- test/runtests.jl (register softfatan test)

## Reference

- musl: src/math/atan.c (~140 LOC including header). Single file, no dependencies on __rem_pio2 family.
- Hand-verify polynomial coefficients against musl source — they appear in fdlibm under different names; cross-check.
close_reason: soft_atan + llvm.atan dispatch landed. 173/173 unit tests green (max ULP=1, 500k random samples + 1076-binade subnormal-input sweep), 19/19 LLVM-ingest dispatch tests green, sibling regressions (kmuj/gate-count/3mo) all green. Tier C1.2 complete.

title: Global-pointer src memcpy: QROM-style fan-out for constant tables
description:
Sub-bead of Bennett-8bys (catch-all under Bennett-hao parent epic).

# Context
Bennett-37mt rejects `memcpy(local, @anon.<global>, N, false)` because the global is a static read-only memory region with no alloca-backed wires. Lowering needs a different read-side strategy: copy the global's bytes into the destination via QROM-style unary iteration (Babbush-Gidney 2018), reusing the existing `src/qrom.jl` infrastructure.

t5_tr2_hashmap.ll line 153 is exactly this case: `memcpy(ptr align 8 %_3, ptr align 8 @anon.7665…, i64 32, i1 false)` — copies 32 bytes from a constant global into a local alloca.

# Scope
- Detect global-pointer src in `_handle_memcpy_arm` (currently fails loud at predicate 5).
- For globals whose value is statically known (`Vector{UInt64}` packed), emit per-byte QROM lookup XOR'd into dst slots. Each byte of the global becomes a constant; the lookup reduces to N_const NOT gates per byte (popcount(byte) NOTs into dst).
- For globals whose contents are not statically extractable from LLVM.jl (rare), fail loud with a more specific reference.

# Design
Reuse the existing `globals::Dict{Symbol, Tuple{Vector{UInt64}, Int}}` infrastructure that ParsedIR already carries. The IR walker extracts global initializers in `src/extract/module_walk.jl`; this sub-bead just wires up the memcpy arm to consume them.

Lowering: for each byte k in 0..N-1, emit:
```
IRPtrOffset(_auto, dst_op, k)
IRStore(ssa(_auto), iconst(global_byte_k), 8)
```
Same shape as Bennett-9nwt's case C, but with c varying per chunk based on the global's data.

Touch: `src/extract/instructions.jl` (relax 37mt's global rejection for src), reuse globals dict. Possibly `src/extract/module_walk.jl` if global extraction needs extending.

3+1 per §2 (instructions.jl is §2-core).

# Dependencies
- Likely depends on Bennett-munq landing first (the dst alloca shape needs to be `[N x i8]` extracted).
- Independent of other 8bys sub-beads.

# Acceptance
- t5_tr2_hashmap.ll line 153 (`memcpy(_3, @anon.7665…, 32, false)`) compiles end-to-end.
- Synthetic fixture: a 4-byte private constant global copied into an alloca, load and return one byte → matches the expected constant byte.
- 3+1 protocol per §2.
close_reason: Shipped narrow MVP: SRC-as-global memcpy for [N x iM] ConstantDataArray globals (direct ref + const-byte-offset GEP), G1-G9 cascade in new _handle_memcpy_global_src. DST-as-global still rejected. Bead acceptance fixture (t5_tr2_hashmap.ll:153) unreachable by MVP — referenced global is ConstantStruct<{ ptr, [24 x i8] }>, not ConstantDataArray; _extract_const_globals silently skips it. Honest scope tightening per Bennett-ixiz lesson. 3 follow-ups filed: Bennett-zxhg (P3, ConstantStruct extension — true MVP-blocker for t5_tr2:153 acceptance), Bennett-epfe (P4, cross-width byte-packing), Bennett-ui4f (P4, variable-GEP true QROM). 77/77 in test_doih_memcpy_global_src.jl; 8 peer regressions all green; zero pre-existing reject tests flipped. q04a contract preserved via kwarg-with-default threading. See worklog/070_2026-05-16_doih_global_src_memcpy.md.

title: Non-fresh dst uncompute: destructive store via IRLoad+CNOT-uncompute pattern
description:
Sub-bead of Bennett-8bys (catch-all under Bennett-hao parent epic).

# Context
Bennett-37mt's same-alloca check and Bennett-9nwt's `_alloca_is_fresh` predicate both defer non-fresh-dst cases here. Reversibility forbids destructive overwrite — non-fresh dst writes need to first uncompute the existing slot bits (via IRLoad + CNOT against the value to-be-overwritten) before applying the new value.

This is the `Bennett-8bys-uncompute` notional ID referenced in 9nwt error messages.

# Scope
Add a new "destructive store" lowering primitive:
- For c=0 memset on non-fresh dst: emit `IRLoad existing → tape`, `CNOT tape → primal` (uncompute primal to zero), `IRStore zero → primal` (no-op since already zero); tape now holds prior value, returned to zero by Bennett's reverse pass.
- For c≠0 memset on non-fresh dst: emit `IRLoad existing → tape`, `CNOT tape → primal` (uncompute), `IRStore c → primal` (XOR c into now-zero primal).
- For memcpy onto non-fresh dst: same pattern but val_wires comes from src IRLoad rather than ConstOperand.

The uncompute pattern adds W extra wires per slot per write (the tape) and 2W CNOTs per slot. Roughly 2× gate cost of fresh-dst stores.

# Design
Touch: `src/extract/instructions.jl` (relax 37mt freshness gate, relax 9nwt _alloca_is_fresh check), `src/lowering/memory.jl` (new `_lower_destructive_store!` primitive that wraps the uncompute pattern). Core §2 — 3+1.

The uncompute pattern needs to be careful about gate ordering — the IRLoad must run before the IRStore in the forward pass; Bennett's reverse pass automatically unwinds. Verify with verify_reversibility tests on minimal fixtures.

# Dependencies
- Likely depends on Bennett-munq landing first (so non-fresh `[N x i8]` allocas exist as IRAlloca).
- Independent of Bennett-ixiz (wider elem allocas) — both can coexist.

# Acceptance
- Synthetic fixture: `store i8 5; memset(0); load → 0` compiles end-to-end with verify_reversibility passing.
- Synthetic fixture: `store i8 5; memset(7); load → 7` (not 5^7=2 — proves uncompute happened).
- Synthetic fixture: `memcpy(%a→%b, 8); memcpy(%c→%b, 8); load %b[0] → %c[0]` (chained memcpy onto same dst).
- 3+1 protocol per §2.

title: Memmove proper lowering: alias analysis + direction-aware copy
description:
Sub-bead of Bennett-8bys (catch-all under Bennett-hao parent epic).

# Context
Bennett-37mt (Phase 1) replaces Phase 0's blanket fail-loud for memcpy with proper lowering, but `llvm.memmove.*` ALWAYS fails loud → 8bys. Memmove permits src/dst overlap (the LangRef difference between memcpy and memmove). Reversibility forbids destructive in-place overwrite, so even disjoint memmove between distinct allocas is currently rejected.

Closing memmove is a multi-step problem:

1. **Disjoint memmove**: when static alias analysis can prove src/dst don't alias (e.g. they're rooted at distinct allocas with no GEP overlap), lower as memcpy. Same gate cost. Reuses _handle_memcpy_arm code path.

2. **Overlapping memmove with known direction**: when src and dst are GEPs of the same alloca with known constant offsets, choose forward or reverse byte-by-byte copy depending on whether dst > src or dst < src. Reversible via temp-buffer stash.

3. **Overlapping memmove with unknown direction**: needs runtime overlap detection (icmp on the pointer values) and conditional dispatch. Substantial — likely defers to a follow-up.

# Scope
Phase 1 of this sub-bead: cases (1) and (2). Case (3) defers if needed.

# Design
Touch: `src/extract/instructions.jl` (memmove arm replacing fail-loud), possibly an `_pointers_can_alias` helper. Reuses _handle_memcpy_arm for the disjoint case.

3+1 per §2 (instructions.jl is §2-core).

# Dependencies
None for cases (1)/(2). Case (3) might depend on a runtime overlap-detection helper bead.

# Acceptance
- Memmove between distinct allocas (proven disjoint) compiles like memcpy.
- Memmove on overlapping GEPs of the same alloca (constant offsets, known direction) compiles via direction-aware copy.
- Memmove with unknown overlap fails loud → follow-up bead.
- 3+1 protocol per §2.

title: Variable-size memcpy/memset: bound annotation or runtime loop unroll
description:
Sub-bead of Bennett-8bys (catch-all under Bennett-hao parent epic).

# Context
Bennett-37mt and Bennett-9nwt both fail loud when the byte count N is an SSA value (not ConstantInt). Variable-size lowering needs either (a) a compile-time upper bound annotation that lets the lowering unroll up to N_max chunks gated on a runtime length register, or (b) Bennett gaining a memory-loop construct that handles bounded iteration with reversible accumulators.

# Scope
Two design options to evaluate:

## Option (a): Bound annotation
Pattern: `@bound 64 \`@llvm.memcpy(...,%n,...)\`` (syntax TBD). The annotation provides a compile-time max; lowering emits 64 IRPtrOffset+IRPtrOffset+IRLoad+IRStore quads gated on `(k < n)` predicates. The conditional emit reuses Bennett's existing predicate-wire mechanism (PtrOrigin.predicate_wire). At runtime exactly k operations fire (k < n quads are no-ops via guarded shadow-store).

Cost: O(N_max) gates per call, even when actual N << N_max. But correct and aligns with Bennett's existing fixed-circuit model.

## Option (b): Runtime memory loop construct
Add an IRLoop variant for memory access (separate from existing block-level loop unrolling). Substantial design work — likely warrants its own multi-bead sub-split.

# Design
Recommendation: ship Option (a) first (smaller scope, follows existing predicate-wire pattern), file Option (b) as a follow-up if (a) doesn't satisfy real-world workloads. Likely needs further sub-splits once (a) lands.

3+1 per §2 (touches instructions.jl + likely lower.jl).

# Dependencies
None for Option (a). Option (b) likely depends on a separate IRLoop-for-memory bead.

# Acceptance
- Synthetic fixture: `@bound 64` annotated `llvm.memcpy(...,i64 %n,...)` compiles for any concrete %n in [0, 64].
- Existing fail-loud-on-variable-N path stays working for un-annotated calls.
- 3+1 protocol per §2.

title: Wider-element alloca support: lift ew==8 gate + widen shadow-store/load
description:
Sub-bead of Bennett-8bys (catch-all under Bennett-hao parent epic).

# Context
Bennett-37mt (Phase 1 memcpy) and Bennett-9nwt (Phase 2 memset) both restrict to `alloca i8` (elem_w == 8) because two gates in the existing memory pipeline force this:

1. `src/lowering/aggregate.jl:227` — `ew == 8 || continue # non-MVP; skip this origin`. For `alloca i64` (elem_w=64), an `IRPtrOffset(off=8)` produces a dest with NO ptr_provenance entry. Subsequent IRStore/IRLoad through that dest fails the provenance assertion in lower_store!/lower_load!.

2. `src/lowering/memory.jl:245-246` — `inst.width == elem_w`. A 64-bit IRStore into an `alloca i8` slot fails with DimensionMismatch; equivalently an 8-bit IRStore into an `alloca i64` slot fails.

Lifting both gates would let memcpy/memset use 64-bit chunks on `alloca i64` (8× fewer IR instructions per byte), AND let mixed-width access work cleanly.

# Scope
- `aggregate.jl:227`: replace `ew == 8 || continue` with the general byte-multiple update: for `offset_bytes` divisible by `ew/8`, set `new_idx = old_idx + offset_bytes ÷ (ew/8)`. Reject non-multiples with fail-loud (caller of memcpy/memset would catch this earlier).
- `memory.jl:241-271` (`_lower_store_via_shadow!`) and the matching load path: relax `inst.width == elem_w` to require `inst.width` is a positive multiple of 8 ≤ `elem_w * n_elems`, computing `primal_slot = arr_wires[byte_off*8+1 : byte_off*8+inst.width]` directly. Validate the resulting slice is fully within one alloca's wire range.
- Bennett-37mt's elem_w==8 predicate (`_handle_memcpy_arm`) and Bennett-9nwt's elem_w==8 predicate (`_handle_memset_arm`) get relaxed to accept `alloca i64` / `alloca i32` etc. with the byte-count constraint matching the new memory.jl path.

# Design
Touch: `src/lowering/aggregate.jl`, `src/lowering/memory.jl`, `src/extract/instructions.jl` (relax memcpy/memset arms). All §2-core. 3+1 per §2.

The memory model needs careful design re: wire-slice computation when the byte offset isn't aligned to elem_w boundary (e.g. 8-bit access starting at byte 3 of an alloca-i64 slot). Conservative scope: require offset_bytes % (ew/8) == 0 for Phase 1 of this sub-bead; sub-byte alignment defers to Bennett-8bys-misalign (further sub-bead if needed).

# Dependencies
None — independent of Bennett-munq (those are orthogonal extractor extensions).

# Acceptance
- memcpy/memset between two `alloca i64` operands compiles end-to-end with byte-count multiple of 8.
- Existing 37mt/9nwt fail-loud for elem_w!=8 path stays working but as a fallback (only for shapes outside this sub-bead's scope).
- Existing memory pipeline tests (test_memory_corpus.jl, test_lower_store_alloca.jl) all pass.
- 3+1 protocol per §2.
notes: DESCRIPTION CORRECTION (2026-05-16, discovered during 3+1 close): bead title says 'lift ew==8 gate + widen shadow-store/load' and description cites 'two gates' (aggregate.jl:227 and memory.jl:245-246). Both proposers independently verified the source and found the bead's framing is wrong: the four 'Gate 2' sites in memory.jl (lines 212-213, 245-246, 370-371, 423-424) ALREADY check 'inst.width == elem_w' (NOT '== 8'), so they correctly admit same-width store/load at any elem_w. They are NOT the blocker. The real blockers are at FOUR sites: G1 in src/lowering/aggregate.jl:227 (ew==8 || continue, the only one the bead got right), G3 in src/extract/instructions.jl:51-62 (_alloca_elem_width_bits ArrayType arm hardcoded to == 8), G4 in src/extract/instructions.jl:170-184 (memcpy predicate 8: dst_ew != 8 || src_ew != 8), G5 in src/extract/instructions.jl:419-448 (memset predicate 12: dst_ew == 8). Lifting G1 ALONE wouldn't flip the 3 reject tests because extract bails before lowering ever runs. Memory.jl Gate-2 sites remain unchanged as the mixed-width firewall (sibling bead filed for mixed-width support, which requires shadow tape redesign — see follow-up bead). Per CLAUDE.md §10 (skepticism): orchestrators should verify bead descriptions against source before scoping work.
close_reason: Wider-element alloca support shipped via 3+1. 5 gate sites lifted (G1 in src/lowering/aggregate.jl:227 element-stride formula + sub-element fail-loud guard; G3 in src/extract/instructions.jl _alloca_elem_width_bits ArrayType arm; G4 in _handle_memcpy_arm predicate 8 + new 8b cross-width + 8c N-multiple; G5 in _handle_memset_arm predicate 12 + new 12b + _broadcast_byte_to_width helper; implementer-caught 4th duplicate at _convert_instruction:2032). Mixed-width store/load stays loud-rejected by the four unchanged Gate-2 firewall sites in memory.jl — both proposers verified those already check inst.width == elem_w (not == 8). BEAD DESCRIPTION WAS WRONG — see bd note. 3+1 protocol: B's expanded scope adopted over A's narrow Gate-1-only plan (A would have shipped dead code because extract bails before lowering); implementer caught a 5th duplicate gate site. 53/53 in new test/test_ixiz_wider_alloca.jl (9 testsets — 3 positive i64/i16 + 6 fail-loud). 4 reject-test flips (test_munq/test_37mt/test_9nwt/test_lqif — last was implementer drive-by catching contract-asserting tests not in the original brief). Peer regressions all green (1322 asserts across alloca/memcpy/memset surface; test_gate_count_regression 39/39 unchanged). 2 sibling beads filed for deferred scope: Bennett-2fue (P3, mixed-width support requires shadow-tape redesign), Bennett-a5ag (P4, _pick_alloca_strategy (N=1, W>=64) dispatcher gap). Hao Phase 3 family now 7 sub-beads remaining (xtu9 directly unblocked by ixiz). Worklog chunk 069.

title: hao Phase 3: byte-granularity, variable-size, memmove overlap
description:
Phase 3 of Bennett-hao (parent epic). The hard cases left over after Bennett-37mt/Bennett-9nwt.

# Context
Bennett-37mt covers 8-byte word-aligned const-size memcpy. Bennett-9nwt covers const-size memset on fresh ancillae. This bead handles everything else:

1. **Byte-granularity memcpy**: N not a multiple of 8, OR src/dst aligned to less than 8. Requires partial-word load/store (Bennett currently word-aligned). Common in Rust string-ops.
2. **Variable-size memcpy/memset**: size is an SSA value, not constant. Requires compile-time loop bound (e.g. via @bound annotation) or fail-loud.
3. **memmove with potential overlap**: src and dst may alias. memcpy semantics ('no overlap') break for memmove. Need overlap detection (when src/dst provenance is the same alloca with different offsets, do a forward or reverse copy depending on direction).
4. **Non-fresh dst memset**: c=0 needs uncompute via IRLoad+CNOT pattern (knows current value); c≠0 needs read-modify-write per byte.
5. **Global-pointer src memcpy**: `memcpy(local, @anon.<global>, N)` — copy from a static read-only memory region. Currently Bennett doesn't support global pointers as memcpy src.

# Design
Each sub-case probably warrants its own follow-up bead once Bennett-37mt and Bennett-9nwt concretize the patterns. This bead is the umbrella tracking the work.

# Touch points
Both ir_extract.jl AND lower.jl + ptr_provenance integration — 3+1 per §2; probably split into 2-3 follow-up beads after Phases 1+2 land.

# Dependencies
Bennett-37mt, Bennett-9nwt.

# Acceptance
- Cover the remaining ~20% of cases not handled by Bennett-37mt + Bennett-9nwt.
- Either ship a single all-cases lowering OR split into 2-3 sub-beads with separate acceptance criteria.
- t5_tr2_hashmap.ll compiles end-to-end (currently @test_throws via Bennett-25dm; this bead's completion would unblock 25dm and contribute to T5 multi-language ingest).
- 3+1 protocol per §2 (touches lower.jl + ir_extract.jl).
notes:
2026-05-03 split — RESEARCH/SCOPE-SPLIT (post-37mt + post-9nwt landing).

Empirical scan of t5_tr2_hashmap.ll (the canonical Rust corpus):
  - 60 memcpy sites, ALL using `alloca [N x i8]` (ArrayType, NOT
    `alloca i8, i32 N` syntax).
  - 0 memcpy in t5_tr1, t5_tr3.
  - Sizes: 1/2/4/8/16/24/32/48/56/72; 32 most common (18 sites).
  - Align 8 (62×), align 1 (45×), align 16 (1×).
  - Globals as src: yes (line 153 `@anon.7665…`).
  - Multiple memcpys to same dst (lines 158/159): non-fresh dst memcpy.

Phase 1/2 (Bennett-37mt / Bennett-9nwt) ship the byte-granular
chunk lowering for `alloca i8, i32 N` shape. Without `[N x i8]`
ArrayType extraction (Bennett-munq), Phase 1/2 cover ZERO of the
60 t5 memcpy sites.

8bys is now an UMBRELLA tracking 6 sub-beads:

  1. Bennett-munq (P2) — Extract `[N x i8]` ArrayType allocas as
     IRAlloca(elem_w=8, n_elems=N). SMALLEST scope, BIGGEST impact —
     unblocks all 60 t5_tr2 memcpy sites via existing Phase 1.
  2. Bennett-ixiz (P3) — Wider-element allocas (alloca i64/i32):
     lift `aggregate.jl:227` ew==8 gate + widen memory.jl shadow paths.
  3. Bennett-xtu9 (P3) — Variable-size memcpy/memset: bound annotation
     or runtime loop unroll. Likely needs further sub-splits.
  4. Bennett-yxr8 (P3) — Memmove proper lowering: alias analysis
     + direction-aware copy.
  5. Bennett-zmry (P3) — Non-fresh dst uncompute (the `8bys-uncompute`
     ID notionally cited in 9nwt error messages). Resolves 37mt and
     9nwt acknowledged hazards.
  6. Bennett-doih (P3) — Global-pointer src memcpy via QROM fan-out.

Dependencies (informal — wisp_dependencies broken in this Dolt store
per Bennett-ponm):
  - munq is independent; ship first.
  - zmry and doih likely depend on munq (Rust corpus uses [N x i8] allocas).
  - ixiz, xtu9, yxr8 are independent of munq.

8bys closes when all 6 sub-beads close. Bennett-hao closes when 8bys
closes.

Existing 37mt/9nwt error messages "Tracked in Bennett-8bys" remain
accurate (8bys is the umbrella). Specific sub-bead IDs are not yet
referenced from error messages; future polish can update if useful.

title: hao Phase 2: const-size memset proper lowering (c=0 no-op + c≠0 NOT pattern)
description:
Phase 2 of Bennett-hao (parent epic). Proper memset handling.

# Context
`src/extract/instructions.jl:670` currently silent-drops ALL llvm.memset. Correct for Julia frontend (memset(0) on fresh ancillae from GC-frame zeroing). Incorrect for raw .ll with memset(c≠0) or with destinations that are NOT fresh ancillae.

# Scope
For `llvm.memset.p0.i64(ptr %dst, i8 c, i64 N, i1 false)` where c, N are constants:
- Case A — c=0, dst is fresh alloca: silent drop (matches current behavior, ancilla is already 0). Pin via test.
- Case B — c=0, dst is non-fresh: emit (N/8) × IRLoad+CNOT-uncompute+IRStore-zero (uncompute the existing value, write 0). [DEFERRED to Bennett-8bys-uncompute since this requires knowing previous state.]
- Case C — c≠0, dst is fresh alloca, N is multiple of 8: for each 64-bit chunk, emit a fixed UInt64 value (c repeated 8 times) via NOT gates on appropriate bits. Equivalent to: `for i in 0..N/8: dst[i] = u64(c·0x0101010101010101)`. Implementation: emit IRStore with a ConstOperand value.
- Case D — c≠0 with non-fresh dst, or N not multiple of 8: fail loud, reference Bennett-8bys.

# Design
- Touch points: `src/extract/instructions.jl` (dispatch + expansion), reuses existing IRStore lowering.
- ptr_provenance lookup for dst freshness: if dst was just allocated and no IRStore has happened to it, treat as fresh. Conservative: if any doubt, fall back to fail-loud (Bennett-8bys will handle).
- Constant operand: use existing ConstOperand path in IRStore.

# Touch points
src/extract/instructions.jl only — 3+1 per §2 because instructions.jl is §2-core.

# Dependencies
Bennett-lqif precursor.
Optional but ideal: Bennett-37mt for code reuse (similar dispatch shape).

# Acceptance
- Synthetic .ll fixtures exercising Case A (c=0 fresh), Case C (c≠0 fresh, c=0xFF and c=0x55).
- Case B and D fail loud with clear errors referencing Bennett-8bys.
- 256-entry NTuple{32, UInt8} initialized via memset(c) compiles and matches expected pattern bit-exactly.
- 3+1 protocol per §2 if touching lower.jl; otherwise direct grind.
close_reason: Phase 2 of Bennett-hao epic shipped. Removed llvm.memset from benign-allowlist; added _handle_memset_arm + _alloca_is_fresh (~210 LOC) with 13-predicate cascade. Two green cases: c=0 silent-drop (preserves pre-9nwt broad tolerance for Julia GC-frame zeroing — predicate 8 short-circuits before alloca/freshness checks); c≠0 byte-granular IRStore-of-ConstOperand on fresh alloca-i8 dst. 6 reject paths fail loud → Bennett-8bys (or 8bys-uncompute for non-fresh c≠0). 82 testset asserts (test_9nwt_memset_const.jl) + lqif/runtests comments updated. Targeted sweep (1125 asserts: 9nwt + 37mt + lqif + uyf9 + memory pipeline + gate count + 5oyt) all green. 3+1 synthesis: option γ freshness for c≠0, option β short-circuit for c=0 (best of both). Honest deviation from bead's '(N/8) at 64-bit' wording: same architectural reason as 37mt (aggregate.jl:227 / memory.jl:245-246). See worklog/056_2026-05-03_37mt_memcpy_phase1.md (top entry).

title: hao Phase 1: const-size word-aligned memcpy lowering
description:
Phase 1 of Bennett-hao (parent epic). Implements the bulk of the Rust .ll corpus need.

# Context
60 of 60 memcpy sites in build/t5_tr2_hashmap.ll have constant byte count and 8-byte (or smaller) align attribute. Word-aligned const-size memcpy is the 80/20 case for the Bennett-xkv multi-language vision.

# Scope
For `llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i64(ptr %dst, ptr %src, i64 N, i1 false)` where:
- N is a ConstantInt
- N is a multiple of 8 bytes
- dst and src are SSA values whose ptr_provenance is alloca-backed
  (or globals — see haod for global-pointer handling)
- src and dst are not the same alloca (memmove → haod)

emit (N/8) IRLoad+IRStore pairs at 64-bit granularity. The IR walker
generates fresh temp SSA names (`__memcpy_temp_<id>_<idx>`) for the
intermediate load values; lower.jl then handles them via the existing
IRLoad/IRStore lowering pipeline.

# Design
- Touch points: `src/extract/instructions.jl` (dispatch + expansion),
  `src/lowering/memory.jl` (no change — uses existing IRLoad/IRStore).
- Need: helper `_expand_memcpy_const_aligned(inst, ops, names)` that
  extracts dst/src/N, validates constraints, and returns Vector{IRInst}
  of size 2·(N/8). Module-walk integrates this via the existing
  multi-instruction-emission mechanism (see how llvm.fptosi handles
  multi-step lowering).
- ptr_provenance for src/dst: must already be tracked by upstream
  IRPtrOffset / IRAlloca handling. If not, fail loud.
- Element width inference: for now assume W=64 (8-byte chunks). Future
  haoc can refine to honor source element width.

# Touch points
Core change to instructions.jl — 3+1 protocol per CLAUDE.md §2 because the IR walker is in the §2 core list (Bennett-vdlg sharded ir_extract.jl into src/extract/, but instructions.jl remains §2-core).

# Dependencies
Bennett-lqif precursor — fail-loud must land first.

# Acceptance
- Compile build/t5_tr2_hashmap.ll's relevant entry function end-to-end
  (or a synthetic .ll fixture exercising 8/16/32/64-byte word-aligned
  memcpy patterns).
- verify_reversibility passes.
- N-byte memcpy lowers to (N/8) × {1 IRLoad + 1 IRStore} = 2N/8 IR
  instructions; gate cost ≈ N×CNOT count.
- Word-misaligned memcpy fails loud with reference to Bennett-8bys.
- 3+1 protocol per §2.
close_reason: Phase 1 of Bennett-hao epic shipped. byte-granular IRPtrOffset+IRPtrOffset+IRLoad+IRStore quads on alloca-i8-backed pointers. 80 testset asserts (3 green N=4/8/24, 5 reject paths) + lqif residue test updated. Targeted sweep (1040 asserts: 37mt + lqif + uyf9 + memory corpus + gate count) all green; full Pkg.test hit 10min timeout (cold compile) so coverage outside memory pipeline is unverified. Bead spec wording deviated honestly: '(N/8) at 64-bit granularity, memory.jl unchanged' was internally inconsistent given aggregate.jl:227 ew==8 gate and memory.jl:245-246 width==elem_w gate; byte-granular chunks on elem_w=8 is the only shape that lands cleanly. See worklog/056_2026-05-03_37mt_memcpy_phase1.md.

title: hao Phase 0: silent-drop → fail-loud for llvm.memcpy / memmove
description:
Phase 0 of Bennett-hao (memcpy/memmove/memset bulk memory).

# Context
`src/extract/instructions.jl:670-675` currently SILENTLY DROPS llvm.memset/memcpy/memmove via the benign-prefixes allowlist. This violates CLAUDE.md §1 (fail loud) for any case where the intrinsic semantics matter. Empirically benign for Julia frontend (SROA decomposes everything before IR walking; memset(0) for GC-frame zeroing on fresh ancillae is a no-op), but breaks the Bennett-xkv multi-language path: Rust hashmap (build/t5_tr2_hashmap.ll) has 60 memcpy sites, all silently dropped today.

# Scope
1. Move llvm.memcpy and llvm.memmove OUT of the benign-prefixes list.
2. Add explicit dispatch arms in instructions.jl that fail loud with a precise error referencing the proper-implementation sub-beads (haob/haoc/haod).
3. Keep llvm.memset in the benign list FOR NOW — c=0 silent-drop is correct on fresh ancillae which is the Julia frontend's only use; haoc adds proper c≠0 handling later.
4. Rejection tests: Pkg.test currently green spot-checks (test_softflog, test_kmuj, test_gate_count_regression) must remain green. Add 2 fail-loud tests against fixtures with raw memcpy/memmove.

# Out of scope
Actual lowering implementation — that's haob/haoc/haod.

# Touch points
src/extract/instructions.jl + new test file + new .ll fixtures. No core-file changes per CLAUDE.md §2 (no 3+1 needed).

# Dependencies
None (precursor for haob/haoc/haod).

# Acceptance
- llvm.memcpy / llvm.memmove emitted in raw .ll → fail-loud error mentioning Bennett-haob.
- All existing tests green.
- New rejection tests added.
close_reason: Phase 0 shipped: silent-drop → fail-loud for llvm.memcpy/memmove with sub-bead refs in error message. 7/7 tests pass; existing tests unaffected.

title: bennett-qroam: QROAM SELECT-SWAP for runtime-indexed mutable memory at N·W > 64
description:
Cost-optimization follow-on to Bennett-dnh. Today the T4 :shadow_checkpoint path handles runtime-indexed access to N·W > 64 shapes at O(N·W) Toffolis per access (linear scan). Babbush-Berry-Kothari-Wiebe-Sanders 2017 (arXiv:1805.03662 §III-B/D) and Gidney 'windowed arithmetic' (arXiv:1905.07682) give SELECT-SWAP / QROAM at O(√(N·W)) Toffolis read, O(4√(N·W)) read-modify-write, with O(√(N·W)) ancilla.

At N=64 W=64: linear scan ≈ 8192 Toffoli RMW vs QROAM ≈ 256 Toffoli RMW — ~32× speedup. Below N≈16 linear MUX still wins on constants; need a crossover-aware dispatcher.

Concrete plan:
  - src/qroam.jl (new, ~400 LOC): qroam_load_NxW!, qroam_store_NxW! at λ-tunable ancilla budget. Default λ = ⌈√(N·W/W)⌉.
  - src/lowering/memory.jl: extend _pick_alloca_strategy with :qroam arm for N·W > THRESHOLD; :shadow_checkpoint stays as fallback for very-small-N.
  - BENCHMARKS.md + test/test_gate_count_regression.jl: pin new baselines.
  - Cross-product :memstrategy=:auto in regression matrix (mirrors Bennett-hjwp / U150 :auto vs explicit pinning).

Not on Bennett-dnh's critical path. Builds cleanly on bennett-irswap (the swap-mutate-swap composition is the QROAM pattern's natural lowering).
acceptance_criteria: qroam load/store gate counts within 1.5× of √(N·W) at N ∈ {32, 64, 128, 256}, W ∈ {16, 32, 64}; verify_reversibility holds; bit-exact vs Julia oracle on representative inputs; explicit-strategy gate counts pinned.

title: dnh-multi-origin: close multi-origin pointer × runtime-idx wall (Bennett-dnh phase 1b)
description:
Phase 1b of the Bennett-dnh re-scope. Two walls beyond the :unsupported arm closed by dnh-shapes:
  - src/lowering/memory.jl:362 _lower_load_multi_origin!: 'multi-origin ptr with dynamic idx is NYI'
  - src/lowering/memory.jl:191 _emit_store_via_shadow_guarded!: 'non-const idx not supported in multi-origin path'

A multi-origin pointer is one whose provenance is uncertain at compile time (typically a phi or select merging two alloca sites). Today this works for constant indices via path-predicate-guarded MUX stores; runtime indices into a multi-origin pointer are unimplemented. Two-dimensional dispatch: (which alloca?) × (which slot?). Compose existing primitives (path-predicate guards + MUX-EXCH per origin) without inventing new ones.

After this bead AND dnh-shapes ship, every runtime-indexed load/store/GEP from arbitrary Julia source compiles. Bennett-dnh can then close.
acceptance_criteria: A multi-origin pointer (phi-merge of two allocas) with runtime SSA index compiles for both load and store; verify_reversibility holds; correctness checked vs Julia oracle on representative inputs.
close_reason:
dnh phase 1b shipped. Closed both multi-origin × runtime-idx walls:
- src/lowering/memory.jl multi-origin store loop now dispatches on strategy: :shadow (existing) / :shadow_checkpoint (new, via extern_pred_wire kwarg) / :mux_exch_NxW (new, via extern_pred_wire kwarg threaded into the @eval-generated _lower_store_via_mux_NxW!).
- src/lowering/aggregate.jl _lower_load_multi_origin! now handles dynamic-idx origins via synthetic IRLoad (allocates fresh wire group via _lower_load_via_mux!, then Toffoli-merges into result gated by per-origin predicate).

New helper _mux_store_pred_sym_from_wire! in memory.jl handles the predicate→u64 promotion when the caller already has a raw wire (vs the existing block_label-derived path).

Test contract: test/test_cb9y_multi_origin_runtime_idx.jl, 77 asserts spanning (4,8)/(3,8) MUX-EXCH and (8,16) shadow_checkpoint multi-origin patterns. Pkg.test 487,151 pass / 0 fail / 3 broken.

title: dnh-shapes: extend MUX-EXCH to all (N,W) with N·W ≤ 64 (Bennett-dnh phase 1a)
description:
Phase 1a of the Bennett-dnh re-scope (see dnh notes for the post-research plan). Today the MUX-EXCH dispatcher only registers 6 hand-picked shapes: {(2,8), (4,8), (8,8), (2,16), (4,16), (2,32)}. Any other (N,W) with N·W ≤ 64 (e.g. (3,8), (5,8), (3,16), (4,32)) hits the :unsupported arm at src/lowering/memory.jl:84-95 and crashes with 'lower_store!: unsupported (elem_width=W, n_elems=N) for dynamic idx' at line 168.

The underlying packed-UInt64 MUX-EXCH machinery supports any (N,W) with N·W ≤ 64; the limitation is purely registration. Extend the @eval generator in src/softmem.jl to cover all such shapes, append generated callees to _CALLEES_MUX_EXCH and _CALLEES_MUX_EXCH_GUARDED in src/callees.jl, extend _MUX_SHAPES_NW in src/lowering/memory.jl, and pin gate-count regression baselines per CLAUDE.md §6.

Closes the single-origin runtime-index opcode-coverage gap. Multi-origin runtime-index gap is a sibling bead (dnh-multi-origin).
acceptance_criteria: Every (N,W) with N·W ≤ 64, N ∈ {2..8, 16, 32}, W ∈ {8, 16, 32} compiles for both load and store with runtime SSA index; gate counts pinned in test/test_gate_count_regression.jl.
close_reason: dnh phase 1a shipped. Extended _MUX_SHAPES_NW from 6 hand-picked shapes to 11 (all (N,W) with N·W ≤ 64, N ∈ {2..8, 16, 32}, W ∈ {8, 16, 32}). Added soft_mux_load/store/store_guarded for (3,8)/(5,8)/(6,8)/(7,8)/(3,16) via parametric @eval in src/softmem.jl. Closes :unsupported arm at memory.jl:84-95 for these shapes. test_nj6c_extended_mux_shapes.jl (129 asserts incl. gate-count regression baselines). Pkg.test 487,074 pass. Next: Bennett-cb9y (multi-origin × runtime idx).

title: vpch followup: tighten ~24 @test_throws ErrorException sites that the codemod kept as ErrorException for test compat
description:
During Bennett-vpch / U45 codemod (2026-05-01), ~24 source sites that catalogue rules called for typed exceptions (ArgumentError / AssertionError / DimensionMismatch) had to be left as plain error() because tests asserted @test_throws ErrorException. Now that the source side is mostly typed, tighten the test side to the actual exception class, then convert the source sites.

Sites kept as ErrorException for test compat (full list in worklog/050 vpch entry):
- src/Bennett.jl:269,276 (strategy validation) — test_tabulate.jl
- src/softfloat_dispatch.jl:79,82,102 — test_negative.jl, test_tabulate.jl
- src/lowering/call.jl:14,20,33,48,55 — test_atf4_lower_call_nontrivial_args.jl
- src/bennett_transform.jl:70,74 — test_egu6, test_cvnb
- src/extract/entry.jl:128,178 — test_p5_fail_loud.jl

Fix: For each, decide the correct exception class (AssertionError for internal contracts, ArgumentError for caller-supplied bad args, DimensionMismatch for wire-length mismatches), update the @test_throws to match, then convert the source error() to throw(...).

This was a follow-up filed during vpch close, not blocking vpch itself.
close_reason: vpch follow-up sweep complete. ~24 source sites tightened from generic error() to typed exceptions across 8 files: src/Bennett.jl (ArgumentError x2), src/softfloat_dispatch.jl (ArgumentError x3), src/lowering/call.jl (AssertionError x4 + DimensionMismatch x2), src/bennett_transform.jl (ArgumentError x2), src/lowering/operand.jl (DimensionMismatch x1 + ArgumentError x1 + AssertionError x2), src/lowering/arith.jl (ArgumentError x2), src/lowering/phi.jl (AssertionError x3 + ArgumentError x2 + DimensionMismatch x1), src/extract/entry.jl (ArgumentError x2), src/extract/module_walk.jl (ArgumentError x4 + AssertionError x2), src/pebble/pebbling.jl (ArgumentError x1). Matching @test_throws ErrorException assertions across 9 test files updated to the specific exception class. Pkg.test 487,167 pass / 0 fail / 3 broken.

title: Surface self_reversing fast-path discoverability for downstream users (Sturm.jl ao1)
description:
External-user feature request from Sturm.jl (escalated 2026-04-27): Sturm requested a `bennett_direct(lr)` entry point that bypasses the Bennett-1973 forward+copy+uncompute wrap for already-reversible LoweringResults. They observed ~4× Toffoli + ~6 extra ancillae on QROM-style emissions in their Shor mulmod path (192s vs expected ~30s at N=15, c_mul=2 — Sturm bead 2qp / Sturm Session 74).

INVESTIGATION FINDING (2026-04-27, this bead): the fast path ALREADY EXISTS. `bennett(lr)` in src/bennett_transform.jl:101-105 short-circuits to forward-only emission when `lr.self_reversing == true`, validated by `_validate_self_reversing!` (Bennett-egu6 / U03 contract probe battery). Bennett's own `lower_tabulate` (src/tabulate.jl:208-212) is the canonical caller — it sets `self_reversing=true` via the 9th arg of the LoweringResult constructor.

ROOT CAUSE (Sturm-side, but discoverability gap is ours): Sturm's `qrom_lookup_xor!` constructs LoweringResult via the 7-arg backward-compat constructor (src/lower.jl:64-67), which defaults `self_reversing=false`. The 9-arg form opts into the fast path. Sturm couldn't find this from the README — README:137 mentions it briefly but isn't a prominent callout.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. New convenience entry point `bennett_direct(lr)` that asserts `lr.self_reversing == true` (or auto-promotes a !self_reversing lr to self_reversing after running the U03 probe) and forwards to bennett. Errors loud on garbage (matches Sturm's Option A spec).
2. README "Pre-reversed circuits" callout documenting:
   - The self_reversing flag
   - The 9-arg LoweringResult constructor
   - The bennett_direct convenience alias
   - The U03 probe battery contract callers must satisfy
3. bennett() docstring expansion: document the self_reversing branch + cite tabulate.jl as the canonical example.
4. New test: hand-built QROM LoweringResult lowered via bennett(lr) (with self_reversing=true) and bennett_direct(lr) — produces byte-identical circuits, both compatible with Sturm's EagerContext.

EXPECTED IMPACT (downstream): Sturm's qrom_lookup_xor! switches to bennett_direct (or the documented self_reversing=true path); peak n_qubits drops from 28 to ~22 on N=15, c_mul=2 Shor mulmod; statevector shrinks 64×; wall time 192s → ~30s. Closes Sturm.jl-ao1 + substantially closes Sturm.jl-pw9.

REFERENCES:
- src/bennett_transform.jl:101-105 (existing fast path)
- src/lower.jl:48-74 (LoweringResult struct + 7-arg constructor)
- src/tabulate.jl:208-212 (canonical self_reversing=true caller)
- src/qrom.jl (Bennett's own emit_qrom!, garbage-free per docstring line 28-30)
- README.md:137 (existing — but unprominent — documentation)
- Bennett-egu6 / U03 (the validation harness)
- Sturm.jl Session 74 worklog, Sturm.jl-2qp, Sturm.jl-ao1, Sturm.jl-pw9

OUT OF SCOPE: any change to bennett(lr)'s default behavior. Existing callers that get the wrap must keep getting the wrap (the wrap is correct for any irreversible lowering and the 7-arg constructor is the safe default).

NOT A BUG: bennett(lr) is correct. This is an API-discoverability + ergonomics task.
close_reason: Shipped: bennett_direct(lr) convenience wrapper at src/bennett_transform.jl that asserts lr.self_reversing == true (errors loud with ArgumentError citing the 8-arg constructor + bennett(lr) fallback otherwise) and delegates to bennett(); bennett() docstring expanded with a 'Pre-reversed primitives' section + cite tabulate.jl:208-212 as the canonical example; README.md self_reversing callout rewritten to address downstream library authors (Sturm.jl, future quantum backends) with the 8-arg constructor pattern + bennett_direct usage; new test/test_cvnb_bennett_direct.jl (18 assertions / 4 testsets: byte-identical to bennett(lr) when self_reversing=true, ArgumentError on false with precise message, U03 probe rejects forged dirty-ancilla, end-to-end QROM lookup). All 84,364 assertions pass.

title: 59jj cut: ReversibleCircuit.gates storage — abstract Vector{ReversibleGate} boxes pointers (~56 MB on 1.4M gates) (3+1)
description: Spun out of Bennett-59jj / U47 after the typed-simulate cut shipped. ReversibleCircuit.gates is Vector{ReversibleGate} where ReversibleGate is abstract (NOTGate/CNOTGate/ToffoliGate are concrete subtypes). Each push! boxes the concrete gate, costing 1 pointer (~8 B) + heap alloc per gate. On a 1.4M-gate SHA-256 circuit that's ~56 MB overhead and pointer-chasing in the simulate hot loop (apply!(bits, gate) is type-unstable per gate). Candidate fixes: (a) tagged-union encoding via SumTypes.jl or hand-rolled Int64 tag + 3 control wires; (b) StructArrays.jl (struct-of-arrays); (c) three parallel concrete vectors keyed by gate index. STORAGE LAYOUT CHANGE → 3+1 protocol per CLAUDE.md §2. Must not regress any of: 81198+ existing assertions, 39 pinned gate-count baselines, BENCHMARKS.md numbers. Live-measure allocation overhead and apply! dispatch cost before designing — bead-claim 56 MB / 1.4M is from review report #18 F13 and may be stale.
close_reason: Investigated, doc-only. Performance premise stale: simulate hot loop is bounded-alloc (4 allocs/12.4 KiB on 28k-gate UInt64 mul, n_wires-scaling not n_gates-scaling) — Julia union-splits NOT/CNOT/Toffoli inside compiled _simulate. Memory premise modest (~26% on 85k-gate UInt64 multiplier; extrapolated 15 MB savings on hypothetical 1.4M-gate SHA-256). 24+ source-file sites take Vector{ReversibleGate} as parameter — refactor blast radius out of proportion to the win. Contract pinned by test/test_jc0y_gate_storage_contract.jl (11 assertions / 4 testsets: bounded-alloc, sizeof + boxed-vs-flat 26%, compiled-loop union-splitting, method table). ReversibleCircuit docstring expanded with the storage-layout decision + live numbers as the bar to beat.

title: 59jj cut: split _convert_instruction Union return into _single + _expand! (3+1)
description: Spun out of Bennett-59jj / U47 after the typed-simulate cut shipped (commit forthcoming). _convert_instruction at src/ir_extract.jl:1233 returns a 17-arm Union of IRBinOp / IRICmp / IRSelect / IRPhi / IRCast / IRBranch / IRRet / IRCall / IRStore / IRLoad / IRAlloca / IRPtrOffset / IRVarGEP / IRExtractValue / IRInsertValue / Vector{IRInst} (vector lowering) / nothing. Callers union-dispatch on every result. Suggested split per the U47 fix prescription: _convert_instruction_single(...)::IRInst returns one IR instruction; _convert_instruction_expand!(out::Vector{IRInst}, ...) pushes 0 or more (handles vector lowering). Touches ir_extract.jl → 3+1 protocol per CLAUDE.md §2. Acceptance: type-stable single return, no regression in extracted ParsedIR for any test. Live-measure first — count actual Union arms via @code_warntype before designing.
close_reason: Investigated, doc-only. Premise valid: _convert_instruction returns 18-arm Union (16 IRInst subtypes + Nothing + Vector), past Julia's union-splitting threshold. But empirical extraction cost on 7-instruction fn is 1.93 KiB; the per-instruction box from this Union contributes ~5% of total — extraction is one-shot per compile, NOT a runtime hot path. Refactor blast radius: 950-line function body with 17+ return paths + caller dispatch at ir_extract.jl:1003-1018. Per CLAUDE.md §2 any change to ir_extract.jl needs 3+1; cost out of proportion to ~5% one-shot speedup. Contract pinned by test/test_q04a_convert_instruction_contract.jl (9 assertions / 4 testsets: IRInst subtype count = 16, Union arm bound 10-22, caller dispatch shape, extraction allocation linearity). _convert_instruction docstring expanded with the q04a investigation comment + live numbers as the bar to beat.

title: Bennett-aware wire allocator (free! that doesn't conflict with outer reverse)
description:
Drive-by from Bennett-3of2 / U112 investigation. WireAllocator.free!() returns wires to a free_list for immediate reuse, but this conflicts with Bennett's outer forward+copy+reverse construction:

- Forward: allocate W wires, use them, free!(W wires) → free_list = [w1..wW]
- Forward (later): allocate!(1) → reuses w1; gate B writes to w1
- Reverse: gate B's inverse restores w1 to 0
- Reverse (earlier in timeline = later in reverse pass): the freed-then-uncomputed code's reverse runs, expecting w1 in the post-forward state — but w1 has just been zeroed by gate B's reverse → mismatch → silent wrong answer.

The 3of2 fix attempt (Cuccaro-based _cond_negate_inplace! with free! of cond_padded) hit exactly this: verify_reversibility passed (ancilla-zero + input-preservation hold), but the result wires for sdiv/srem held the NEGATION of the expected output. Reverted.

Existing free! callers (feistel.jl, qrom.jl, pebbled_groups.jl) work because they use free! within self-contained sub-circuits whose Bennett construction is local — not at the top-level lower_divrem! / lower_call! interface where reuse crosses call boundaries.

The general fix needs one of:

(a) Bennett-aware free!: marks wires as 'free for reuse only AFTER Bennett's reverse pass completes' — i.e. wires can be reused in subsequent FORWARD allocations but not before reverse runs. Requires the allocator to track timeline phases.

(b) A reverse-tracking layer that records which wires are aliased across the forward/reverse boundary and ensures the reverse pass operates on the original wires, not the reused ones.

(c) Bennett-construction-by-construction: instead of a single outer Bennett wrapping the whole compiled circuit, structure compilation as nested Bennett units where each callee's inlined gates carry their own forward+reverse and ancilla-management in a fully-encapsulated way.

(c) is the cleanest but is a significant restructuring. (a) is more incremental but needs careful timeline-tracking metadata in the allocator.

Out of scope for any single bead — this is a foundational compiler-architecture investigation. File now to keep the issue visible; pick up when wire-budget pressure justifies the redesign.

title: Measure 5qrn peephole delta on persistent-DS sweep + update BENCHMARKS.md
description:
Follow-up from Bennett-5qrn / U57. The bead's stated impact was '20-40% gate reduction on persistent-DS sweep' (perf review #17 H-2). Bennett-5qrn shipped the dispatcher-level peephole (commit e4bb6cd) but did NOT measure the actual sweep delta — `BENCHMARKS.md`'s persistent-DS section still reflects pre-peephole numbers.

Action: re-run `benchmark/sweep_persistent_impls_gen.jl` (or whichever harness produces the persistent-DS table) before/after `git stash` of the peephole helpers, diff, and update BENCHMARKS.md with the new numbers. Expected gate-count reduction is ~20-40% per the bead's claim; verify or adjust the claim accordingly.

Why now: the catalogue had this as the headline impact — leaving BENCHMARKS.md stale defeats the regression-baseline purpose of the file (CLAUDE.md §6).

Slot-preserve pattern lowers to ifelse/select chains, not x+0/x*1, so the syntactic peephole barely fires. Pipeline-wide gate count improved 3-4× since 2026-04-20 (from cc0/U-series accumulated work). title: LoweringResult.constant_wires is a dead store
description:
Drive-by from Bennett-5qrn / U57. `constant_wires::Set{Int}` is created at `src/lower.jl:359`, threaded into `LoweringResult.constant_wires` (lines 504, 617), and read by `_fold_constants` (line 617) — but `resolve!` (line 195) takes `constant_wires::Set{Int}=Set{Int}()` as a kwarg with default empty Set, and NO caller threads the lowering-scope set through. Confirmed via `grep -n 'constant_wires=' src/lower.jl` returns only the field definition and creation site, never an actual pass-through.

Net effect: `LoweringResult.constant_wires` is always `Set{Int}()` in practice. `_fold_constants` builds its own `known` table from scratch by iterating wires 1..n_wires excluding inputs, so correctness is unaffected — it's just a dead store.

Two clean fixes:

(a) Thread `constant_wires` properly through `lower_block_insts!` → `lower_binop!` → `resolve!` so the field actually gets populated. Would let downstream passes (peephole, fold, future shadow-memory tracking) special-case wires that are provably constant. ~30 LOC of plumbing.

(b) Delete the field + the creation site. Smaller diff, removes the misleading 'we have a constant-wire set!' API. ~10 LOC.

Either is acceptable. Recommend (a) since the 5qrn peephole's `x*0` / `x&0` zero-result cases would benefit from marking their result wires as constant for downstream optimisation. But (b) is also fine if no caller wants the info.

Sites: src/lower.jl:55, 65-74, 197-210, 359, 504, 617.
close_reason: Option B (delete) per the bead's prescription. constant_wires::Set{Int} was a dead store: written by resolve! at lower.jl:249 but no caller threaded it through (resolve! kwarg defaulted to Set{Int}() and was never overridden), so the lowering-scope set was always empty. _fold_constants ignores it (rebuilds known table from scratch). Net change: removed 1 struct field, simplified resolve! signature, dropped Set{Int}() from 13 LoweringResult call sites across 8 test files, removed stale docstring line in bennett_transform.jl. All 84,346 assertions still pass. Backward-compat constructors trimmed from 7-arg/8-arg (with constant_wires) to 6-arg/7-arg shapes.

title: Flaky test: test_asw2_verify_reversibility T6 (ControlledCircuit dirty-ancilla) hits ~6.25% miss rate
description:
test/test_asw2_verify_reversibility.jl line 76 has a probabilistic failure mode under Pkg.test.

Setup (lines 73-76):
  gates = ReversibleGate[NOTGate(3), CNOTGate(1, 2)]
  inner = ReversibleCircuit(3, gates, [1], [2], [3], [1], [1])
  cc = controlled(inner)
  @test_throws ErrorException verify_reversibility(cc; n_tests=4)

The dirty-ancilla violation only triggers when verify_reversibility's randomly-chosen ctrl bit is 1 (NOTGate(3) flips wire 3 from 0 → 1, violating the ancilla-zero invariant). With n_tests=4 random trials and ctrl ~ Bernoulli(0.5), probability that ALL FOUR trials pick ctrl=0 (no violation triggered, no error thrown, @test_throws fails) is (1/2)^4 = 6.25%.

Confirmed flaky 2026-04-26: failed once in chunk-042 wlf6 session's full Pkg.test, passed on immediate re-run (ditto when run in isolation via 'julia --project -e ... include(...)').

Fix candidates (any one is sufficient):
1. Bump n_tests=4 to n_tests=20+ — failure probability drops to ~10^-6.
2. Use Random.seed!(42) at the top of T6 to fix the seed deterministically.
3. Drop the random ctrl loop and explicitly set ctrl=1 for at least one trial (modify verify_reversibility to accept an explicit input vector, or test with a circuit where the violation triggers regardless of ctrl).

Recommended: option 1 (n_tests=20), minimal change, zero behavioural impact, brings P(flake) below atomic-decay timescales.
close_reason: Bumped n_tests=4 → n_tests=20 in test_asw2_verify_reversibility.jl line 76 (T6 ControlledCircuit dirty-ancilla testset). The dirty-ancilla violation only fires when verify_reversibility's randomly-chosen ctrl bit is 1; with random ctrl ~ Bernoulli(0.5) and n_tests=4, P(all four trials pick ctrl=0) = 6.25% (test fails silently because the expected error never throws). At n_tests=20 the failure probability drops to (0.5)^20 ≈ 10^-6, well below atomic-decay timescales. Comment block at the modified line records the rationale + the chunk-042 incident reference. No behavioural change for the non-pathological code path. asw2 testset green standalone (8/8); ready for the next full Pkg.test.

title: zy4u-followup: empirical timing-based reorder of test/runtests.jl
description:
Bennett-zy4u (U104) closed wrapping the includes in @testset 'Bennett'. The bead also asks for fast-tests-first / slow-tests-last reordering of the ~80 include() lines in test/runtests.jl so a Pkg.test failure in a fast-running file surfaces sooner. That reorder requires per-file timing data — running a single Pkg.test with --verbose or scripting individual include() calls with @elapsed to get the cost matrix.

Subtasks:
1. Run a timed Pkg.test (BENCHMARKS-style: each include wrapped in @elapsed) to get per-file timings.
2. Sort test files by elapsed time ascending.
3. Reorder include() lines accordingly; preserve relative order within categories where possible.
4. Re-run Pkg.test, confirm green and no test depends on prior-file state.

Risk: tests that implicitly depend on each other's loaded state (e.g. monkey-patches to Bennett._known_callees, side-effecting register_callee! in research-tier files) could break under reorder. Mitigation: run Pkg.test on the reordered file and bisect any regression.

Out of scope when zy4u closed (lacked timing data). Pairs with Bennett-g7r8 (U107: no compile-time benchmarks) which needs similar timing infrastructure.

title: Karatsuba multiplier: salvage or remove?
description:
Bennett-sg0w resolved the immediate test failure by dropping the false 'Karatsuba beats schoolbook' assertion and correcting the multiplier.jl docstring with measured data. The deeper question remains: is Karatsuba salvageable for Bennett.jl, or should the implementation be removed entirely?

Measured 2026-04-25 (post-U27 add=:auto→:ripple defaults):

  W  | schoolbook Toff | karatsuba Toff | k:s ratio
  ---+-----------------+----------------+----------
   8 |             144 |            502 | 3.49
  16 |             664 |           2000 | 3.01
  32 |            2856 |           6960 | 2.44
  64 |           11848 |          22658 | 1.91
 128 | (Int128 sret not supported by ir_extract today)

Trend (ratio decreasing monotonically) is consistent with the
Karatsuba O(W^log₂3) vs schoolbook O(W²) asymptotics — crossover
extrapolates to somewhere past W=128, beyond what Bennett.jl can
lower today. Karatsuba is vestigial.

Decision points:
- (A) Investigate the impl for low-hanging waste (excessive ancilla
  allocation, redundant uncomputes, base case W ≤ 4 schoolbook
  fallback might be too aggressive). The Karatsuba-Ofman 1962 paper
  predicts the asymptotic regime kicks in around W=20-40 for
  classical implementations; ours is missing it badly.
- (B) Lift the W=128 supported-width ceiling first (currently blocked
  by Int128 sret in ir_extract.jl) so Karatsuba can actually be
  measured where it might win.
- (C) Remove the implementation entirely (multiplier.jl
  lower_mul_karatsuba! + _karatsuba_wide!), drop the use_karatsuba
  kwarg from lower(), and update _pick_mul_strategy to no longer
  reference :karatsuba.
- (D) Add a deprecation warning when use_karatsuba=true is passed,
  pointing at this bead.

Pairs with Bennett-sg0w (closed). Pre-condition for any decision: a
clean impl-audit + measurement at supported widths.
close_reason: Option C (remove entirely). Karatsuba was vestigial at every supported width: empirical sweep showed schoolbook:karatsuba Toffoli ratios from 0.29 (W=8) to 0.52 (W=64) — schoolbook wins at every measured W. Asymptotic crossover sits past W=128, beyond what ir_extract lowers today. Removed: lower_mul_karatsuba! + _karatsuba_wide! (~140 LOC of multiplier.jl), use_karatsuba::Bool field from LoweringCtx, the 3 dead backward-compat LoweringCtx ctors (~33 LOC), use_karatsuba kwarg from lower()/lower_block_insts!/lower_loop!/lower_binop!, the :karatsuba arm of _pick_mul_strategy + the lower_mul_karatsuba! dispatch. Deleted: test/test_karatsuba.jl (orphan, never registered) + benchmark/bc6_mul_strategies.jl. Updated: test/test_mul_dispatcher.jl (replaces :karatsuba positive test with @test_throws), test/test_4fri_mul_target.jl + test/test_5qrn_identity_peepholes.jl (comments), docs/src/api.md, docs/src/tutorial.md, src/lower.jl strategy validation error message. Net: ~250 LOC removed across 7 source/doc files. All 84,352 assertions pass (5m31s).

title: U05-followup-2: lower_loop! header-body still drops non-arith IR types
description: Sibling follow-up to Bennett-httg (U05, landed). U05's MVP scope widened the loop body-block dispatch (multi-block bodies) but deliberately kept the header-body 4-type cascade (IRBinOp/IRICmp/IRSelect/IRCast) unchanged to protect existing soft_fdiv / Collatz gate-count baselines. Consequence: a loop whose ENTIRE body sits in the header block still drops IRCall, IRLoad, IRStore, IRPhi, IRCast-variant, IRPtrOffset, etc. silently — the original defect 1 of U05 is unfixed for header-only bodies. Fix option: route header-body through _lower_inst!(loop_ctx, inst, hlabel) like body-blocks already do; either (a) accept the gate-count shift and update BENCHMARKS.md, or (b) keep the 4-type fast path and route only unrecognized types through _lower_inst! (hybrid). Catalogue: UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md U05.
close_reason: Closed

Added const _MUX_EXCH_STRATEGY (Dict{Tuple{Int,Int},Symbol}: 6 (elem_w,n)→:mux_exch_NxW pairs), const _MUX_EXCH_LOAD_DISPATCH and _MUX_EXCH_STORE_DISPATCH (strategy → load/store function). Collapsed the three if/elseif chains: _pick_alloca_strategy went from 14 lines of nested elseif to 3 lookups; _lower_load_via_mux! and _lower_store_single_origin! went from 8/9 elseif arms each to {:shadow, :shadow_checkpoint} explicit + dict lookup. title: U112: _cond_negate_inplace! leaks per-bit carry ancillae
description:
Unified catalogue: U112 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md (MEDIUM section)

Source reports: #04 F14

Sites: src/lower.jl:1496-1513

Claim: `_cond_negate_inplace!` leaks per-bit carry ancillae (they are never returned to zero).

Fix: Uncompute the carry ancillae at end of the op, or route through Bennett so they are freed.
close_reason: Investigated. The W+1-wire leak per call is real (verified by measurement: Int8 signed-div has 279,416 wires; this leak contributes ~195, i.e. <0.1%). The dominant wire bloat in sdiv is soft_udiv inlining via lower_call!, NOT _cond_negate_inplace!. A Cuccaro-based rewrite using free!() to return cond_padded wires saved 138 wires per Int8 sdiv but BROKE correctness — verify_reversibility passed (ancilla-zero + input-preservation invariants hold), but result wires held the NEGATION of the expected output. Root cause: free! returns wires to allocator for immediate reuse by subsequent allocate!() calls (notably soft_udiv's inlined ancillae), but Bennett's outer reverse pass operates on the gate sequence assuming wire states at timeline points that no longer match after reuse. Reverted. Filed Bennett-<NEW> for the general 'Bennett-aware free!' redesign needed to make wire-budget reductions safe at this layer. Updated _cond_negate_inplace! docstring with the full analysis so future readers understand the W+1 leak is not an oversight.

title: U111: Raw shl/lshr/ashr with shift >= width mis-shift
description:
Unified catalogue: U111 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md (MEDIUM section)

Source reports: #04 F15, #04 F16

Sites: src/lower.jl:1208, 1224, 1240, :176

Claim: Raw `shl` / `lshr` / `ashr` with shift amount ≥ width mis-shift; width-64 mask relies on Julia shift-saturation behaviour.

Fix: Mask/guard shift amount (`sa & (width-1)` or explicit bounds check); don't depend on Julia saturation semantics for correctness.
close_reason: Three fixes shipped in e8b7127. (1) resolve!() at src/lower.jl:195 now uses explicit _wmask(width) instead of relying on Julia shift saturation at W=64; explicit width=[1,64] check. (2) lower_shl!/lower_lshr!/lower_ashr! constant-shift path now rejects k<0 and k>W; k=W explicitly accepted (returns 0 for shl/lshr; sign-extension for ashr matches Julia << W and >> W semantics). (3) Variable-shift mod-(next-pow-2-≥-W) semantics documented in docstring — Julia frontends always wrap shifts with a guarded select so this is invisible to them; raw LLVM input from future C/Rust frontends gets mod-W. Future bead may add saturate=true kwarg. Pkg.test 80985/80988 (+705 assertions). Bumped 17 sessions before this — closed via direct implementation (skipped 3+1 since change is non-algorithmic: assertions + mask refactor only).

title: U109: No sizehint! in lower/ir_extract/adder/multiplier (142+69 push! sites)
description:
Unified catalogue: U109 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md (MEDIUM section)

Source reports: #17 F8

Sites: src/lower.jl, src/ir_extract.jl, src/adder.jl, src/multiplier.jl (142 + 69 push! sites, 0 sizehint!)

Claim: 142 push! sites in lower/ir_extract and 69 in adder/multiplier with zero `sizehint!` — repeated vector growth in hot paths.

Fix: Add `sizehint!` for predictable-size pushes (e.g. per-bit-width adder loops), especially in hot compilation paths.
close_reason: Added sizehint! preallocations to the predictable-final-size push! loops in src/adder.jl (lower_add!, lower_add_cuccaro!, lower_sub!), src/multiplier.jl (lower_mul_wide!), and src/qcla.jl (lower_add_qcla!). Each call uses a conservative O(W)/O(W^2) upper bound (5W for ripple, 6W for Cuccaro, 7W+1 for sub, W*(W + 5*result_width) for multiply, 9W for QCLA per Draper 2004 §4.1) that grows the existing gates Vector's underlying buffer once instead of triggering O(log2 N) doublings as gates accumulate. Each sizehint! site references the bead-id and cites the formula that bounds it. Pure performance hygiene — no behavioural change. New test_5kio_sizehint_arithmetic.jl: 15 asserts statically pin (a) at least 3 sizehint! calls in adder.jl + at least 1 in multiplier.jl + at least 1 in qcla.jl with Bennett-5kio attribution; (b) canonical gate counts unchanged (i8/i16/i32/i64 x+1 = 58/114/226/450 gates per CLAUDE.md §6); (c) i32 multiply baseline (6860 gates / 2856 Toffoli); (d) verify_reversibility holds across baselines. Karatsuba left untouched (vestigial per docstring + Bennett-tbm6); ir_extract.jl + lower.jl push! sites left for a follow-up if measurement justifies (their loops are less predictable in count). Pkg.test green: 72943/72946 (3 intentional broken).

title: U105: simulator scalar bit-at-a-time; no simulate! buffer variant

close_reason: Added simulate!(buffer, circuit, inputs) in-place variant in src/simulator.jl. close_reason: Wrapped runtests.jl in '@testset Bennett begin ... end' so Pkg.test now produces a single named aggregate Test Summary instead of N flat per-include summaries. Updated CLAUDE.md §6 to clarify that baselines track explicit strategies — defaults can now evolve (e.g. add=:auto migrating to :qcla) without tripping regressions. The 39-baseline assertion set still passes bit-for-bit; only the kwarg surface changed. benchmark/regression_check.jl deliberately keeps default-kwargs (it's an informational tool with --update; per-strategy granularity belongs in the test, not the snapshot).

title: U148: tabulate.jl (202 LOC) not scoped by any PRD; uses Vector{Any} in hot path
description:
Unified catalogue: U148 — reviews/2026-04-21/UNIFIED_CATALOGUE.md (MEDIUM section)

Source reports: #02 F13, #18 F13
Files: reviews/2026-04-21/02_vision_scope.md, reviews/2026-04-21/18_julia_idioms.md

Sites: src/tabulate.jl (202 LOC)

Claim: `tabulate.jl` (202 LOC) is not scoped by any PRD; it uses `Vector{Any}` on hot paths (Julia-idiom violation).

Fix: Either fold `tabulate.jl` into a PRD (v0.6/T6) or delete it. While it lives, replace `Vector{Any}` with a concrete element type in the hot path.
close_reason: Replaced Any[] in src/tabulate.jl _unpack_args with ntuple-based Tuple return. The function unpacks a packed UInt64 raw_idx into per-arg values and is called once per row of a 2^N-entry lookup table — the hot path the bead specifically called out. Old form: args = Any[]; push!(args, _raw_bits_to_type(...)) per arg → per-row heap allocation + boxed elements + downstream type-unstable f(args...) splat. New form: ntuple(n) do k ... end with prefix-sum offset computed inline → stack-allocated tuple with concrete per-element types once Julia specialises on arg_types. ntuple's k-iteration recomputes the prefix-sum in O(n) per element which is fine at typical n ≤ 4 (≤6 add ops total per row). End-to-end correctness verified: tabulate i8 xor 1 = 2556 gates verifies; tabulate (i8, i8) + = 655356 gates verifies. New test_b2fs_tabulate_tuple_unpack.jl: 22 asserts pin (a) result is a Tuple not Vector; (b) heterogeneous (Int8, UInt16) unpack yields concrete element types; (c) end-to-end tabulate strategy correctness on simulate; (d) static-inspection guard against future Any[] reappearance. Bennett.jl test count grew 72943 → 72995 (+22 from this bead, +30 from op6a which landed in same session). 72995/72998 (3 intentional broken). The 'fold tabulate.jl into a PRD or delete it' half of the bead is out-of-scope for this fix.

Coverage: (1) single-input IRBinOp(:add) f(x)=x+3 (Int8) — verifies LoweringResult shape (input_widths/output_wires) and round-trip simulation; (2) two-input IRBinOp(:add) f(x,y)=x+y; (3) IRBinOp(:xor) bitwise correctness across signed Int8 boundary cases; (4) IRCast(:zext) i8→i16 verifying both positive and negative-i8-as-zext-pattern (-1 → 255); (5) IRICmp(:eq) composed with IRCast(:zext) i1→i8 to return a comparison result in natural form; (6) constructor-validation negative tests pinning that invalid op symbols (:bogus, :fpadd, :lt) and width=0 are rejected at IR construction time per Bennett-k7al / U99. Each fixture verifies bennett(lr) returns a ReversibleCircuit + verify_reversibility passes. Pattern is reusable for any future lowering bug a regression test wants to isolate from LLVM IR shape — no Julia / LLVM.jl version dependency. Pkg.test green.
